examples.en: Similarly, the reduction in corporate taxes has fostered significant growth within the manufacturing sector.

examples.es: Del mismo modo, el aumento en la inversión en energías renovables ha impulsado una transición más rápida hacia la sostenibilidad ambiental.
examples.en: Similarly, the increase in investment in renewable energy has driven a faster transition toward environmental sustainability.

cards.example_sentence: Similarly, the researchers observed that the use of active learning strategies fosters greater student participation in the classroom.
cards.example_es: Del mismo modo, los investigadores observaron que el uso de estrategias de aprendizaje activo fomenta una mayor participación de los estudiantes en el aula.
cards.example_en: Similarly, the researchers observed that the use of active learning strategies fosters greater student participation in the classroom.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine building a SAME LEGO set in the SAME way, just like del mismo modo

cards.definition_en: It means 'in spite of that' or 'nevertheless'.

examples.es: No obstante, decidió continuar con el proyecto.
examples.en: Nonetheless, he decided to continue with the project.

examples.es: Los resultados del experimento fueron inconsistentes; no obstante, el equipo decidió publicar sus hallazgos.
examples.en: The experimental results were inconsistent; nonetheless, the team decided to publish their findings.

examples.es: El presupuesto inicial era limitado; no obstante, logramos implementar todas las mejoras necesarias.
examples.en: The initial budget was limited; nonetheless, we managed to implement all the necessary improvements.

cards.example_sentence: Nonetheless, he decided to continue with the project.
cards.example_es: No obstante, decidió continuar con el proyecto.
cards.example_en: Nonetheless, he decided to continue with the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a stubborn no obstinate man shouting Nonetheless to push forward

cards.definition_en: As a result; for that reason.

examples.es: El presupuesto del proyecto fue recortado; por lo tanto, tuvimos que cancelar la investigación.
examples.en: The project budget was cut; therefore, we had to cancel the research.

examples.es: Los datos muestran una tendencia al alza; por lo tanto, se espera un aumento en las ventas este trimestre.
examples.en: The data show an upward trend; therefore, an increase in sales is expected this quarter.

examples.es: La evidencia científica es concluyente; por lo tanto, debemos reconsiderar nuestra estrategia actual.
examples.en: The scientific evidence is conclusive; therefore, we must reconsider our current strategy.

cards.example_sentence: The project budget was cut; therefore, we had to cancel the research.
cards.example_es: El presupuesto del proyecto fue recortado; por lo tanto, tuvimos que cancelar la investigación.
cards.example_en: The project budget was cut; therefore, we had to cancel the research.
cards.mnemonic_en: I pour low tango sauce, therefore the dance stops.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a statement that differs from or opposes what has just been mentioned.

examples.es: El primer grupo completó la tarea rápidamente; en cambio, el segundo grupo necesitó más tiempo para terminar.
examples.en: The first group completed the task quickly; in contrast, the second group needed more time to finish.

examples.es: Las zonas rurales han experimentado un descenso poblacional; en cambio, las grandes ciudades siguen creciendo de forma acelerada.
examples.en: Rural areas have experienced a population decline; in contrast, large cities continue to grow rapidly.

examples.es: El candidato conservador propone reducir los impuestos; en cambio, su oponente aboga por aumentar el gasto público.
examples.en: The conservative candidate proposes cutting taxes; in contrast, his opponent advocates for increasing public spending.

cards.example_sentence: The first group completed the task quickly; in contrast, the second group needed more time to finish.
cards.example_es: El primer grupo completó la tarea rápidamente; en cambio, el segundo grupo necesitó más tiempo para terminar.
cards.example_en: The first group completed the task quickly; in contrast, the second group needed more time to finish.
cards.mnemonic_en: In contrast, picture a light switch saying 'en cambio' as it flips to the opposite side.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce an example.

examples.es: Las economías emergentes enfrentan diversos desafíos estructurales; por ejemplo, la falta de infraestructura básica limita el crecimiento comercial.
examples.en: Emerging economies face various structural challenges; for instance, the lack of basic infrastructure limits commercial growth.

examples.es: Existen varias fuentes de energía renovable; por ejemplo, la energía solar es muy eficiente.
examples.en: There are several sources of renewable energy; for instance, solar power is very efficient.

examples.es: Muchos mamíferos viven en climas extremos; por ejemplo, los osos polares habitan en el Ártico.
examples.en: Many mammals live in extreme climates; for instance, polar bears inhabit the Arctic.

cards.example_sentence: Emerging economies face various structural challenges; for instance, the lack of basic infrastructure limits commercial growth.
cards.example_es: Las economías emergentes enfrentan diversos desafíos estructurales; por ejemplo, la falta de infraestructura básica limita el crecimiento comercial.
cards.example_en: Emerging economies face various structural challenges; for instance, the lack of basic infrastructure limits commercial growth.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a poor man holding a four as an example.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a clarification or restatement of something previously said.
collocations: that is to say, the project is delayed
that is to say, in other words
that is to say, we need to act

examples.es: No me gustó la película; es decir, la encontré aburrida.
examples.en: I didn't like the movie; that is to say, I found it boring.

examples.es: El candidato no cumple con los requisitos mínimos; es decir, no tiene la experiencia necesaria para el puesto.
examples.en: The candidate does not meet the minimum requirements; that is to say, he lacks the necessary experience for the position.

examples.es: La empresa ha decidido reducir sus operaciones en el extranjero; es decir, cerrará todas sus sucursales en Europa el próximo mes.
examples.en: The company has decided to scale back its overseas operations; that is to say, it will close all of its European branches next month.

cards.example_sentence: I didn't like the movie; that is to say, I found it boring.
cards.example_es: No me gustó la película; es decir, la encontré aburrida.
cards.example_en: I didn't like the movie; that is to say, I found it boring.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a teacher holding a sign that reads ES and saying, 'That is to say'.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a general summary or overview of a topic.
collocations: Broadly speaking, the results are positive.
Broadly speaking, we can conclude that...
Broadly speaking, it's a good approach.

examples.es: A grandes rasgos, el proyecto consiste en desarrollar una aplicación móvil para la gestión de tareas.
examples.en: Broadly speaking, the project involves developing a mobile app for task management.

examples.es: A grandes rasgos, los resultados de la encuesta indican que la mayoría de los estudiantes prefiere el aprendizaje híbrido.
examples.en: Broadly speaking, the survey results indicate that the majority of students prefer hybrid learning.

examples.es: A grandes rasgos, la nueva política económica busca reducir la inflación mediante el control del gasto público.
examples.en: Broadly speaking, the new economic policy aims to reduce inflation by controlling public spending.

cards.example_sentence: Broadly speaking, the project involves developing a mobile app for task management.
cards.example_es: A grandes rasgos, el proyecto consiste en desarrollar una aplicación móvil para la gestión de tareas.
cards.example_en: Broadly speaking, the project involves developing a mobile app for task management.
cards.mnemonic_en: A broad map with a raspy goose speaking about big features.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce the first reason or point in a discussion.
collocations: Why did you do that in the first place?
In the first place, we should consider the costs.
It wasn't necessary in the first place.

examples.es: En primer lugar, debemos revisar los documentos antes de proceder con la auditoría.
examples.en: In the first place, we must review the documents before proceeding with the audit.

examples.es: En primer lugar, el autor argumenta que el cambio climático es una amenaza inminente para la biodiversidad.
examples.en: In the first place, the author argues that climate change is an imminent threat to biodiversity.

examples.es: En primer lugar, los investigadores deben establecer una metodología clara para garantizar la validez de los resultados.
examples.en: In the first place, researchers must establish a clear methodology to ensure the validity of the results.

cards.example_sentence: In the first place, we must review the documents before proceeding with the audit.
cards.example_es: En primer lugar, debemos revisar los documentos antes de proceder con la auditoría.
cards.example_en: In the first place, we must review the documents before proceeding with the audit.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a prime (primer) athlete stepping into the first place on the podium.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce the second item in a list or sequence of points.

examples.es: En segundo lugar, debemos considerar el presupuesto.
examples.en: Secondly, we must consider the budget.

examples.es: En segundo lugar, el informe analiza el impacto ambiental del proyecto.
examples.en: Secondly, the report analyzes the environmental impact of the project.

examples.es: En segundo lugar, el orador presentó los datos estadísticos sobre el crecimiento demográfico.
examples.en: Secondly, the speaker presented the statistical data regarding population growth.

cards.example_sentence: Secondly, we must consider the budget.
cards.example_es: En segundo lugar, debemos considerar el presupuesto.
cards.example_en: Secondly, we must consider the budget.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a runner in second place yelling 'Secondly!' as if saying Spanish 'segundo'.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something applies broadly or in a general sense.
collocations: to put it in general terms
in general terms, the result is
in general terms, we can say

examples.es: En términos generales, el proyecto será completado a finales de año.
examples.en: In general terms, the project will be completed by the end of the year.

examples.es: En términos generales, los resultados de la encuesta indican una mayor satisfacción entre los empleados.
examples.en: In general terms, the survey results indicate higher satisfaction among employees.

examples.es: En términos generales, la nueva política educativa busca reducir la brecha de desigualdad en las escuelas rurales.
examples.en: In general terms, the new education policy seeks to reduce the inequality gap in rural schools.

cards.example_sentence: In general terms, the project will be completed by the end of the year.
cards.example_es: En términos generales, el proyecto será completado a finales de año.
cards.example_en: In general terms, the project will be completed by the end of the year.
cards.mnemonic_en: A general signs a contract titled In general terms on a big board.

cards.definition_en: In the end, after all other considerations or events have taken place.

examples.es: En última instancia, el éxito de la investigación depende de la precisión de los datos recolectados.
examples.en: Ultimately, the success of the research depends on the accuracy of the collected data.

examples.es: Aunque discutimos varias alternativas, en última instancia elegimos el modelo más rentable para la empresa.
examples.en: Although we discussed several alternatives, ultimately we chose the most cost-effective model for the company.

examples.es: En última instancia, es responsabilidad del comité académico aprobar o rechazar la tesis doctoral.
examples.en: Ultimately, it is the responsibility of the academic committee to approve or reject the doctoral thesis.

cards.example_sentence: Ultimately, the success of the research depends on the accuracy of the collected data.
cards.example_es: En última instancia, el éxito de la investigación depende de la precisión de los datos recolectados.
cards.example_en: Ultimately, the success of the research depends on the accuracy of the collected data.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine the last 'última' chapter flipping open, revealing the ultimate outcome.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a statement that the speaker wants to emphasize or draw attention to.
collocations: It should be noted that the data...
It should be noted that this is...

examples.es: Cabe señalar que el informe será presentado mañana.
examples.en: It should be noted that the report will be presented tomorrow.

examples.es: Cabe señalar que los resultados del experimento no fueron concluyentes.
examples.en: It should be noted that the results of the experiment were not conclusive.

examples.es: Cabe señalar que la tasa de desempleo ha disminuido significativamente este trimestre.
examples.en: It should be noted that the unemployment rate has decreased significantly this quarter.

cards.example_sentence: It should be noted that the report will be presented tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Cabe señalar que el informe será presentado mañana.
cards.example_en: It should be noted that the report will be presented tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a note on a signal flag, reminding you it should be noted that.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a statement that may be debated or supported with reasoning.
collocations: It can be argued that the data supports this
It can be argued that this is the case
It can be argued that there are alternatives
It can be argued that the policy is ineffective

examples.es: Se puede sostener que la implementación de esta nueva política educativa mejorará las tasas de graduación a largo plazo.
examples.en: It can be argued that the implementation of this new educational policy will improve graduation rates in the long term.

examples.es: Se puede sostener que el aumento en las temperaturas globales es la causa principal de la reciente inestabilidad climática.
examples.en: It can be argued that the rise in global temperatures is the primary cause of recent climate instability.

examples.es: Se puede sostener que los avances en inteligencia artificial transformarán radicalmente el mercado laboral en la próxima década.
examples.en: It can be argued that advancements in artificial intelligence will radically transform the labor market over the next decade.

cards.example_sentence: It can be argued that the implementation of this new educational policy will improve graduation rates in the long term.
cards.example_es: Se puede sostener que la implementación de esta nueva política educativa mejorará las tasas de graduación a largo plazo.
cards.example_en: It can be argued that the implementation of this new educational policy will improve graduation rates in the long term.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a sturdy 'sostener' shelf holding a debate sign, reminding you 'It can be argued that'.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something appears clear or obvious.
collocations: It seems evident that the data show...
It seems evident that we need to act
It seems evident that this is the case

examples.es: Parece evidente que la evidencia apoya su teoría.
examples.en: It seems evident that the evidence supports his theory.

examples.es: Parece evidente que los resultados del experimento son concluyentes.
examples.en: It seems evident that the results of the experiment are conclusive.

examples.es: Parece evidente que el cambio climático está afectando la biodiversidad global.
examples.en: It seems evident that climate change is affecting global biodiversity.

cards.example_sentence: It seems evident that the evidence supports his theory.
cards.example_es: Parece evidente que la evidencia apoya su teoría.
cards.example_en: It seems evident that the evidence supports his theory.
cards.mnemonic_en: It seems evident that a pair of evidence makes it obvious.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that something is generally approved or recognized by many people.

examples.es: Está ampliamente aceptado que el aumento de las temperaturas globales se debe a la actividad humana.
examples.en: It is widely accepted that the rise in global temperatures is due to human activity.

examples.es: Está ampliamente aceptado en la comunidad científica que la vacunación reduce significativamente la propagación de enfermedades.
examples.en: It is widely accepted in the scientific community that vaccination significantly reduces the spread of diseases.

examples.es: Está ampliamente aceptado que la educación bilingüe mejora las capacidades cognitivas de los estudiantes jóvenes.
examples.en: It is widely accepted that bilingual education improves the cognitive abilities of young students.

cards.example_sentence: It is widely accepted that the rise in global temperatures is due to human activity.
cards.example_es: Está ampliamente aceptado que el aumento de las temperaturas globales se debe a la actividad humana.
cards.example_en: It is widely accepted that the rise in global temperatures is due to human activity.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a wide (wide) hallway where everyone nods, saying 'ampliamente aceptado'.

cards.definition_en: To stay in a state of uncertainty.

examples.es: El resultado del juicio seguirá permaneciendo incierto.
examples.en: The outcome of the trial will remain uncertain.

examples.es: Debido a la falta de datos, la viabilidad del proyecto debe permanecer incierto.
examples.en: Due to the lack of data, the project's viability must remain uncertain.

examples.es: Aunque los expertos analizaron las pruebas, el origen del fenómeno parece permanecer incierto.
examples.en: Although experts analyzed the evidence, the origin of the phenomenon seems to remain uncertain.

cards.example_sentence: The outcome of the trial will remain uncertain.
cards.example_es: El resultado del juicio seguirá permaneciendo incierto.
cards.example_en: The outcome of the trial will remain uncertain.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a RE-MAINING sign that reads IN-CERTAIN, swaying like an unsure flag.

cards.definition_en: To consider something carefully when making a decision.

examples.es: Debes tener en cuenta las recomendaciones del médico antes de iniciar el tratamiento.
examples.en: You should take into consideration the doctor's recommendations before starting the treatment.

examples.es: El comité debe tener en cuenta los posibles riesgos financieros antes de aprobar el nuevo proyecto de inversión.
examples.en: The committee must take into consideration the potential financial risks before approving the new investment project.

examples.es: Al redactar el ensayo, es fundamental tener en cuenta las diversas perspectivas culturales sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: When writing the essay, it is essential to take into consideration the diverse cultural perspectives on climate change.

cards.example_sentence: You should take into consideration the doctor's recommendations before starting the treatment.
cards.example_es: Debes tener en cuenta las recomendaciones del médico antes de iniciar el tratamiento.
cards.example_en: You should take into consideration the doctor's recommendations before starting the treatment.
cards.mnemonic_en: Take a ten, then consider it carefully.

cards.definition_en: to form a judgment or inference based on evidence
collocations: draw a conclusion from the data
draw a conclusion based on the evidence

examples.es: Basándose en los resultados del experimento, los investigadores pudieron sacar una conclusión sobre la eficacia del nuevo fármaco.
examples.en: Based on the experiment results, the researchers were able to draw a conclusion regarding the efficacy of the new drug.

examples.es: El comité necesita analizar todas las pruebas antes de sacar una conclusión sobre el incidente.
examples.en: The committee needs to analyze all the evidence before they draw a conclusion about the incident.

examples.es: Es prematuro sacar una conclusión sin haber escuchado los argumentos de ambas partes.
examples.en: It is premature to draw a conclusion without having heard the arguments from both sides.

cards.example_sentence: Based on the experiment results, the researchers were able to draw a conclusion regarding the efficacy of the new drug.
cards.example_es: Basándose en los resultados del experimento, los investigadores pudieron sacar una conclusión sobre la eficacia del nuevo fármaco.
cards.example_en: Based on the experiment results, the researchers were able to draw a conclusion regarding the efficacy of the new drug.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture yourself drawing a final line that pulls out the answer, like you 'sacar' a conclusion

cards.definition_en: to arrive at a decision or judgment after considering the evidence

examples.es: Tras evaluar los resultados del experimento, los investigadores pudieron llegar a una conclusión sobre la eficacia del fármaco.
examples.en: After evaluating the experiment results, the researchers were able to reach a conclusion regarding the drug's efficacy.

examples.es: El tribunal escuchó todos los testimonios antes de llegar a una conclusión sobre el caso.
examples.en: The court heard all the testimonies before they could reach a conclusion about the case.

examples.es: Los expertos debatieron durante horas antes de llegar a una conclusión unánime sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: The experts debated for hours before they could reach a conclusion on climate change.

cards.example_sentence: After evaluating the experiment results, the researchers were able to reach a conclusion regarding the drug's efficacy.
cards.example_es: Tras evaluar los resultados del experimento, los investigadores pudieron llegar a una conclusión sobre la eficacia del fármaco.
cards.example_en: After evaluating the experiment results, the researchers were able to reach a conclusion regarding the drug's efficacy.
cards.mnemonic_en: I reach, I 'llegar' at the conclusion podium.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a final statement or summary.
collocations: By way of conclusion, we can see that...
By way of conclusion, the study suggests...
By way of conclusion, it is evident that...
By way of conclusion, I recommend...

examples.es: A modo de conclusión, el informe destaca la necesidad urgente de implementar políticas de sostenibilidad más estrictas.
examples.en: By way of conclusion, the report highlights the urgent need to implement stricter sustainability policies.

examples.es: A modo de conclusión, los investigadores sugieren que se realicen estudios adicionales para verificar estos resultados preliminares.
examples.en: By way of conclusion, the researchers suggest that further studies be conducted to verify these preliminary results.

examples.es: A modo de conclusión, la presentación resumió los principales desafíos económicos que enfrentará la empresa durante el próximo año fiscal.
examples.en: By way of conclusion, the presentation summarized the main economic challenges the company will face during the next fiscal year.

cards.example_sentence: By way of conclusion, the report highlights the urgent need to implement stricter sustainability policies.
cards.example_es: A modo de conclusión, el informe destaca la necesidad urgente de implementar políticas de sostenibilidad más estrictas.
cards.example_en: By way of conclusion, the report highlights the urgent need to implement stricter sustainability policies.
cards.mnemonic_en: Press the mode button to end, by way of conclusion.

cards.definition_en: A written document presenting the author's research and findings, typically submitted for an academic degree.

examples.es: Estoy trabajando en mi tesis de doctorado.
examples.en: I am working on my doctoral thesis.

examples.es: El estudiante debe defender su tesis ante un comité de expertos.
examples.en: The student must defend his thesis before a committee of experts.

examples.es: La tesis principal del artículo sostiene que la tecnología mejora el aprendizaje.
examples.en: The main thesis of the article argues that technology improves learning.

cards.example_sentence: I am working on my doctoral thesis.
cards.example_es: Estoy trabajando en mi tesis de doctorado.
cards.example_en: I am working on my doctoral thesis.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a student holding a thick test paper that says teh-sis as the thesis

cards.definition_en: A statement or set of reasons presented in opposition to another argument.

examples.es: El abogado presentó una antítesis detallada para invalidar la evidencia presentada por la fiscalía.
examples.en: The lawyer presented a detailed counterargument to invalidate the evidence provided by the prosecution.

examples.es: El autor presentó una antítesis sólida para cuestionar los hallazgos del estudio.
examples.en: The author presented a solid counterargument to challenge the study's findings.

examples.es: Debes incluir una antítesis en tu ensayo para demostrar que has considerado perspectivas opuestas.
examples.en: You must include a counterargument in your essay to show that you have considered opposing perspectives.

cards.example_sentence: The lawyer presented a detailed counterargument to invalidate the evidence provided by the prosecution.
cards.example_es: El abogado presentó una antítesis detallada para invalidar la evidencia presentada por la fiscalía.
cards.example_en: The lawyer presented a detailed counterargument to invalidate the evidence provided by the prosecution.
cards.mnemonic_en: On a kitchen counter, the antítesis debates the recipe, a perfect counterargument.

cards.definition_en: a person's attitude or position on a particular issue

examples.es: Su postura frente al cambio fue muy clara.
examples.en: His stance on the change was very clear.

examples.es: La universidad adoptó una postura firme contra el plagio académico.
examples.en: The university adopted a firm stance against academic plagiarism.

examples.es: El autor defiende su postura mediante un análisis detallado de los datos.
examples.en: The author defends his stance through a detailed analysis of the data.

cards.example_sentence: His stance on the change was very clear.
cards.example_es: Su postura frente al cambio fue muy clara.
cards.example_en: His stance on the change was very clear.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a solid stance, like a perfect postura on a mountain ledge.

cards.definition_en: The mental process of drawing conclusions or making inferences based on evidence or logical principles.

examples.es: Su razonamiento fue impecable durante el debate.
examples.en: His reasoning was impeccable during the debate.

examples.es: El comité cuestionó el razonamiento detrás de la nueva propuesta presupuestaria.
examples.en: The committee questioned the reasoning behind the new budget proposal.

examples.es: Debes explicar el razonamiento lógico que te llevó a esa conclusión científica.
examples.en: You must explain the logical reasoning that led you to that scientific conclusion.

cards.example_sentence: His reasoning was impeccable during the debate.
cards.example_es: Su razonamiento fue impecable durante el debate.
cards.example_en: His reasoning was impeccable during the debate.
cards.mnemonic_en: A razor sharp mind slices a puzzle, revealing the reasoning.

cards.definition_en: A statement or proposition that serves as a basis for reasoning or argument.

examples.es: La premisa del argumento es que todos los seres humanos buscan la felicidad.
examples.en: The premise of the argument is that all human beings seek happiness.

examples.es: El estudio científico se basa en la premisa de que el clima está cambiando rápidamente.
examples.en: The scientific study is based on the premise that the climate is changing rapidly.

examples.es: Debemos cuestionar la premisa fundamental sobre la que se construyó esta teoría económica.
examples.en: We must question the fundamental premise upon which this economic theory was built.

cards.example_sentence: The premise of the argument is that all human beings seek happiness.
cards.example_es: La premisa del argumento es que todos los seres humanos buscan la felicidad.
cards.example_en: The premise of the argument is that all human beings seek happiness.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a pre‑meeting where you set the premise, hearing pre‑me‑sa echo in the room.

cards.definition_en: A conclusion drawn from evidence and reasoning.

examples.es: A partir de los datos, hizo una inferencia sobre el comportamiento futuro del mercado.
examples.en: Based on the data, he made an inference about the future behavior of the market.

examples.es: El detective llegó a una inferencia lógica tras analizar todas las pistas encontradas en la escena del crimen.
examples.en: The detective reached a logical inference after analyzing all the clues found at the crime scene.

examples.es: Los estudiantes deben extraer una inferencia válida del texto científico para completar su ensayo de investigación.
examples.en: Students must draw a valid inference from the scientific text to complete their research essay.

cards.example_sentence: Based on the data, he made an inference about the future behavior of the market.
cards.example_es: A partir de los datos, hizo una inferencia sobre el comportamiento futuro del mercado.
cards.example_en: Based on the data, he made an inference about the future behavior of the market.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a detective reading the 'infer' in Inferencia to uncover hidden clues.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being logically sound or legally acceptable.

examples.es: El investigador cuestionó la validez de los datos recopilados durante el experimento.
examples.en: The researcher questioned the validity of the data collected during the experiment.

examples.es: Para publicar el estudio, debemos demostrar la validez de nuestra metodología ante el comité académico.
examples.en: To publish the study, we must demonstrate the validity of our methodology to the academic committee.

examples.es: Los resultados de la encuesta perdieron su validez debido a un error en el diseño de las preguntas.
examples.en: The survey results lost their validity due to a flaw in the design of the questions.

cards.example_sentence: The researcher questioned the validity of the data collected during the experiment.
cards.example_es: El investigador cuestionó la validez de los datos recopilados durante el experimento.
cards.example_en: The researcher questioned the validity of the data collected during the experiment.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a valid ID stamped validez that opens the door to the party.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being trustworthy or dependable.

examples.es: La fiabilidad de los datos recopilados durante el experimento fue cuestionada por los revisores.
examples.en: The reliability of the data collected during the experiment was questioned by the reviewers.

examples.es: Para publicar un estudio científico, es necesario demostrar la fiabilidad de la metodología empleada.
examples.en: To publish a scientific study, it is necessary to demonstrate the reliability of the methodology employed.

examples.es: El informe destaca la fiabilidad de las fuentes primarias utilizadas para el análisis histórico.
examples.en: The report highlights the reliability of the primary sources used for the historical analysis.

cards.example_sentence: The reliability of the data collected during the experiment was questioned by the reviewers.
cards.example_es: La fiabilidad de los datos recopilados durante el experimento fue cuestionada por los revisores.
cards.example_en: The reliability of the data collected during the experiment was questioned by the reviewers.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a trusty robot labeled Fiabilidad that never fails, proving reliability.

cards.definition_en: A tendency to favor or prejudice a particular perspective, group, or outcome.

examples.es: El sesgo de la encuesta hizo que los resultados no fueran representativos.
examples.en: The bias of the survey made the results unrepresentative.

examples.es: Los investigadores deben reconocer cualquier sesgo potencial en su metodología para garantizar la objetividad del estudio.
examples.en: Researchers must acknowledge any potential bias in their methodology to ensure the objectivity of the study.

examples.es: Es fundamental analizar los datos con cuidado para evitar que el sesgo del observador afecte las conclusiones finales.
examples.en: It is essential to analyze the data carefully to prevent observer bias from affecting the final conclusions.

cards.example_sentence: The bias of the survey made the results unrepresentative.
cards.example_es: El sesgo de la encuesta hizo que los resultados no fueran representativos.
cards.example_en: The bias of the survey made the results unrepresentative.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tilted scale labeled sesgo to remember bias.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being objective, or the ability to consider facts without personal bias or emotion.

examples.es: El científico debe mantener la objetividad al analizar los datos.
examples.en: The scientist must maintain objectivity when analyzing the data.

examples.es: El comité editorial exige total objetividad en los artículos de investigación publicados.
examples.en: The editorial board demands total objectivity in the published research papers.

examples.es: Es difícil conservar la objetividad cuando se evalúa un proyecto en el que uno mismo ha trabajado.
examples.en: It is difficult to preserve objectivity when evaluating a project on which you have worked yourself.

cards.example_sentence: The scientist must maintain objectivity when analyzing the data.
cards.example_es: El científico debe mantener la objetividad al analizar los datos.
cards.example_en: The scientist must maintain objectivity when analyzing the data.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a plain object wearing glasses that read OBJETIVIDAD, showing pure objectivity.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being based on personal feelings, tastes, or opinions rather than objective facts.

examples.es: La subjetividad del autor se refleja en cada capítulo del libro.
examples.en: The subjectivity of the author is reflected in every chapter of the book.

examples.es: Debido a la subjetividad inherente a las entrevistas cualitativas, los investigadores deben ser muy cuidadosos al analizar los datos.
examples.en: Due to the subjectivity inherent in qualitative interviews, researchers must be very careful when analyzing the data.

examples.es: El comité académico criticó el ensayo por su excesiva subjetividad al tratar temas que requieren un análisis objetivo.
examples.en: The academic committee criticized the essay for its excessive subjectivity when addressing topics that require objective analysis.

cards.example_sentence: The subjectivity of the author is reflected in every chapter of the book.
cards.example_es: La subjetividad del autor se refleja en cada capítulo del libro.
cards.example_en: The subjectivity of the author is reflected in every chapter of the book.
cards.mnemonic_en: A subject flies a jet, seeing the world from his own perspective.

cards.definition_en: A statistical measure that describes the extent to which two variables change together.

examples.es: Existe una fuerte correlación entre el consumo de azúcar y la incidencia de diabetes.
examples.en: There is a strong correlation between sugar consumption and the incidence of diabetes.

examples.es: El estudio demostró una correlación directa entre las horas de estudio y las calificaciones finales de los alumnos.
examples.en: The study demonstrated a direct correlation between study hours and students' final grades.

examples.es: Los investigadores no pudieron establecer ninguna correlación significativa entre el clima y el comportamiento del mercado bursátil.
examples.en: The researchers could not establish any significant correlation between the weather and stock market behavior.

cards.example_sentence: There is a strong correlation between sugar consumption and the incidence of diabetes.
cards.example_es: Existe una fuerte correlación entre el consumo de azúcar y la incidencia de diabetes.
cards.example_en: There is a strong correlation between sugar consumption and the incidence of diabetes.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a coral reef where fish swim in perfect correlation, echoing the Spanish correlación.

cards.definition_en: The relationship in which one event or action causes another to occur.

examples.es: La causalidad entre fumar y el cáncer de pulmón está bien establecida.
examples.en: The causation between smoking and lung cancer is well established.

examples.es: Los investigadores tuvieron dificultades para demostrar la causalidad directa entre el nuevo fármaco y los efectos secundarios observados.
examples.en: The researchers struggled to demonstrate a direct causation between the new drug and the observed side effects.

examples.es: Es fundamental distinguir entre una simple correlación estadística y una verdadera causalidad en los estudios sociológicos.
examples.en: It is essential to distinguish between a mere statistical correlation and true causation in sociological studies.

cards.example_sentence: The causation between smoking and lung cancer is well established.
cards.example_es: La causalidad entre fumar y el cáncer de pulmón está bien establecida.
cards.example_en: The causation between smoking and lung cancer is well established.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cause chain labeled causa linking events, reminding you that causalidad means causation.

cards.definition_en: The act of giving out or spreading something, or the way in which something is shared or dispersed.

examples.es: La distribución de los recursos se realizó de manera equitativa.
examples.en: The distribution of the resources was carried out equitably.

examples.es: El estudio analiza la distribución geográfica de las especies en peligro de extinción.
examples.en: The study analyzes the geographic distribution of endangered species.

examples.es: La empresa optimizó la distribución de sus productos a través de una nueva red logística.
examples.en: The company optimized the distribution of its products through a new logistics network.

cards.example_sentence: The distribution of the resources was carried out equitably.
cards.example_es: La distribución de los recursos se realizó de manera equitativa.
cards.example_en: The distribution of the resources was carried out equitably.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a distributor handing out pizza slices, a vivid distribución of food across the table.

cards.definition_en: A departure from a standard, norm, or expected course.

examples.es: La desviación de los resultados experimentales respecto a la hipótesis inicial sugiere un error en el procedimiento.
examples.en: The deviation of the experimental results from the initial hypothesis suggests an error in the procedure.

examples.es: El informe estadístico analiza la desviación estándar de los ingresos familiares durante la última década.
examples.en: The statistical report analyzes the standard deviation of household income over the last decade.

examples.es: Cualquier desviación significativa del protocolo de seguridad debe ser reportada inmediatamente al comité de ética.
examples.en: Any significant deviation from the safety protocol must be reported immediately to the ethics committee.

cards.example_sentence: The deviation of the experimental results from the initial hypothesis suggests an error in the procedure.
cards.example_es: La desviación de los resultados experimentales respecto a la hipótesis inicial sugiere un error en el procedimiento.
cards.example_en: The deviation of the experimental results from the initial hypothesis suggests an error in the procedure.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a road that deviates like a sign saying Desviación pointing off the main path.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being consistent, or the ability to remain the same over time or across situations.

examples.es: La consistencia de los datos en el informe es fundamental para garantizar la validez de los resultados.
examples.en: The consistency of the data in the report is fundamental to ensure the validity of the results.

examples.es: El profesor destacó la falta de consistencia en los argumentos presentados durante el debate académico.
examples.en: The professor highlighted the lack of consistency in the arguments presented during the academic debate.

examples.es: Para lograr un éxito duradero, es necesario mantener una consistencia rigurosa en la metodología de investigación.
examples.en: To achieve lasting success, it is necessary to maintain rigorous consistency in the research methodology.

cards.example_sentence: The consistency of the data in the report is fundamental to ensure the validity of the results.
cards.example_es: La consistencia de los datos en el informe es fundamental para garantizar la validez de los resultados.
cards.example_en: The consistency of the data in the report is fundamental to ensure the validity of the results.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a solid brick wall labeled consistencia that never wavers, teaching consistency.

cards.definition_en: The quality or state of being correct, exact, or precise.

examples.es: La precisión del reloj suizo es impresionante.
examples.en: The accuracy of the Swiss watch is impressive.

examples.es: El científico destacó la precisión de los datos obtenidos durante el experimento.
examples.en: The scientist highlighted the accuracy of the data obtained during the experiment.

examples.es: Se requiere una gran precisión al realizar mediciones en el laboratorio de química.
examples.en: Great accuracy is required when taking measurements in the chemistry laboratory.

cards.example_sentence: The accuracy of the Swiss watch is impressive.
cards.example_es: La precisión del reloj suizo es impresionante.
cards.example_en: The accuracy of the Swiss watch is impressive.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a precise city (precisión) where every arrow hits the bullseye, showing perfect accuracy.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being relevant or applicable to a particular matter.

examples.es: La relevancia de los datos científicos es fundamental para tomar decisiones informadas.
examples.en: The relevance of scientific data is essential for making informed decisions.

examples.es: El comité cuestionó la relevancia de incluir estadísticas históricas en un informe sobre tendencias futuras.
examples.en: The committee questioned the relevance of including historical statistics in a report on future trends.

examples.es: Debemos evaluar la relevancia de cada fuente bibliográfica antes de integrarla en nuestra tesis doctoral.
examples.en: We must evaluate the relevance of each bibliographic source before integrating it into our doctoral thesis.

cards.example_sentence: The relevance of scientific data is essential for making informed decisions.
cards.example_es: La relevancia de los datos científicos es fundamental para tomar decisiones informadas.
cards.example_en: The relevance of scientific data is essential for making informed decisions.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bright badge labeled relevancia that marks the most relevant piece of information.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being suitable or fitting for a particular purpose, situation, or person.

examples.es: La pertinencia de sus comentarios mejoró la discusión del grupo.
examples.en: The appropriateness of his remarks improved the group's discussion.

examples.es: El comité evaluó la pertinencia de los datos presentados en el informe financiero.
examples.en: The committee evaluated the appropriateness of the data presented in the financial report.

examples.es: Debemos cuestionar la pertinencia de incluir este gráfico complejo en una presentación tan breve.
examples.en: We must question the appropriateness of including this complex chart in such a brief presentation.

cards.example_sentence: The appropriateness of his remarks improved the group's discussion.
cards.example_es: La pertinencia de sus comentarios mejoró la discusión del grupo.
cards.example_en: The appropriateness of his remarks improved the group's discussion.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a proper teen sailing a sea, perfectly fitting the situation—like pertinencia.

cards.definition_en: A subset of a population that accurately reflects the characteristics of the whole group.
collocations: a representative sample of the population

examples.es: Para el estudio, tomamos una muestra representativa de la población.
examples.en: For the study, we took a representative sample of the population.

examples.es: El investigador seleccionó una muestra representativa de los estudiantes para analizar sus hábitos de lectura.
examples.en: The researcher selected a representative sample of the students to analyze their reading habits.

examples.es: Los resultados de la encuesta no son válidos porque no se utilizó una muestra representativa de los consumidores.
examples.en: The survey results are not valid because they did not use a representative sample of the consumers.

cards.example_sentence: For the study, we took a representative sample of the population.
cards.example_es: Para el estudio, tomamos una muestra representativa de la población.
cards.example_en: For the study, we took a representative sample of the population.
cards.mnemonic_en: A rep hands out a sample of muestra, showing the perfect representative sample

cards.definition_en: Selection criteria are the standards or requirements used to choose among alternatives.

examples.es: El comité estableció un criterio de selección riguroso para filtrar a los candidatos al puesto de investigador.
examples.en: The committee established a rigorous selection criteria to filter the candidates for the research position.

examples.es: Debemos revisar el criterio de selección antes de proceder con la compra de los nuevos equipos de laboratorio.
examples.en: We must review the selection criteria before proceeding with the purchase of the new laboratory equipment.

examples.es: El criterio de selección para publicar en esta revista científica exige una metodología experimental impecable.
examples.en: The selection criteria for publishing in this scientific journal requires an impeccable experimental methodology.

cards.example_sentence: The committee established a rigorous selection criteria to filter the candidates for the research position.
cards.example_es: El comité estableció un criterio de selección riguroso para filtrar a los candidatos al puesto de investigador.
cards.example_en: The committee established a rigorous selection criteria to filter the candidates for the research position.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a judge ticking a 'selection criteria' list titled 'Criterio de selección'.

cards.definition_en: To gather or obtain information, typically for analysis or record-keeping.

examples.es: El equipo necesita recopilar datos antes de presentar el informe.
examples.en: The team needs to collect data before presenting the report.

examples.es: Los investigadores deben recopilar datos mediante encuestas para validar su hipótesis.
examples.en: Researchers must collect data through surveys to validate their hypothesis.

examples.es: Durante el experimento, es fundamental recopilar datos precisos sobre la temperatura del sistema.
examples.en: During the experiment, it is essential to collect data regarding the system temperature.

cards.example_sentence: The team needs to collect data before presenting the report.
cards.example_es: El equipo necesita recopilar datos antes de presentar el informe.
cards.example_en: The team needs to collect data before presenting the report.
cards.mnemonic_en: I collect data, re‑cop all the facts like a copycat scientist.

cards.definition_en: To manipulate or handle data in order to extract information or perform operations on it.

examples.es: Necesitamos procesar datos antes de generar el informe.
examples.en: We need to process data before generating the report.

examples.es: El departamento de finanzas utiliza algoritmos avanzados para procesar datos financieros de miles de transacciones diarias.
examples.en: The finance department uses advanced algorithms to process data from thousands of daily transactions.

examples.es: El equipo de investigación tardará varias semanas en procesar datos provenientes de los experimentos.
examples.en: The research team will take several weeks to process data gathered from the experiments.

cards.example_sentence: We need to process data before generating the report.
cards.example_es: Necesitamos procesar datos antes de generar el informe.
cards.example_en: We need to process data before generating the report.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a processor blending raw numbers like a kitchen blender, turning data into smooth info.

cards.definition_en: Based on or derived from evidence.

examples.es: El nuevo tratamiento está respaldado por evidencia científica.
examples.en: The new treatment is evidence-based, relying on rigorous clinical trials.

examples.es: La política educativa debe ser respaldado por evidencia para mejorar el rendimiento escolar.
examples.en: The school board insists that all curriculum changes must be evidence-based to ensure student success.

examples.es: Las políticas de salud pública deben ser respaldadas por evidencia para garantizar que las intervenciones sean realmente efectivas.
examples.en: Public health policies must be evidence-based to ensure that interventions are truly effective.

cards.example_sentence: The new treatment is evidence-based, relying on rigorous clinical trials.
cards.example_es: El nuevo tratamiento está respaldado por evidencia científica.
cards.example_en: The new treatment is evidence-based, relying on rigorous clinical trials.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sturdy back supporting piles of evidence like a bookshelf.

cards.definition_en: Considerable in amount, size, or importance; significant.

examples.es: El aumento de la inversión fue sustancial este año.
examples.en: The increase in investment was substantial this year.

examples.es: El equipo de investigación encontró una diferencia sustancial en los resultados del experimento.
examples.en: The research team found a substantial difference in the experiment results.

examples.es: La nueva normativa ha provocado un cambio sustancial en la gestión de los residuos industriales.
examples.en: The new regulation has caused a substantial change in the management of industrial waste.

cards.example_sentence: The increase in investment was substantial this year.
cards.example_es: El aumento de la inversión fue sustancial este año.
cards.example_en: The increase in investment was substantial this year.
cards.mnemonic_en: A SUBstantial pillar sustains the building, showing how big and essential it is.

cards.definition_en: Existing or situated at the edge or margin, or being only barely sufficient.

examples.es: El aumento marginal del precio no afectará a la mayoría de los consumidores.
examples.en: The marginal increase in price will not affect most consumers.

cards.example_sentence: The marginal increase in price will not affect most consumers.
cards.example_es: El aumento marginal del precio no afectará a la mayoría de los consumidores.
cards.example_en: The marginal increase in price will not affect most consumers.

cards.definition_en: Important or notable enough to be worthy of attention.

examples.es: Este hallazgo es significativo para la investigación.
examples.en: This finding is significant for the research.

examples.es: Hubo un cambio significativo en los resultados después de ajustar las variables.
examples.en: There was a significant change in the results after adjusting the variables.

examples.es: La empresa reportó un incremento significativo en sus beneficios trimestrales.
examples.en: The company reported a significant increase in its quarterly profits.

cards.example_sentence: This finding is significant for the research.
cards.example_es: Este hallazgo es significativo para la investigación.
cards.example_en: This finding is significant for the research.
cards.mnemonic_en: A bright sign on the hill marks a significant moment, just like significativo means important.

cards.definition_en: so small or unimportant that it is not worth considering

examples.es: El impacto de ese error es insignificante en el resultado final.
examples.en: The impact of that mistake is negligible on the final result.

examples.es: La diferencia de temperatura entre las dos muestras resultó ser insignificante para el experimento.
examples.en: The temperature difference between the two samples proved to be negligible for the experiment.

examples.es: Debido a la insignificante cantidad de residuos químicos, no se requiere un protocolo de limpieza especial.
examples.en: Due to the negligible amount of chemical residue, no special cleaning protocol is required.

cards.example_sentence: The impact of that mistake is negligible on the final result.
cards.example_es: El impacto de ese error es insignificante en el resultado final.
cards.example_en: The impact of that mistake is negligible on the final result.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a negligible speck of dust that the word insignificant (insignificante) can’t even notice.

cards.definition_en: strictness or exactness in standards, procedures, or analysis

examples.es: La rigurosidad del método científico garantiza resultados confiables.
examples.en: The rigor of the scientific method ensures reliable results.

examples.es: El comité editorial elogió la rigurosidad con la que se analizaron los datos estadísticos.
examples.en: The editorial committee praised the rigor with which the statistical data were analyzed.

examples.es: Se requiere mayor rigurosidad en la revisión de las fuentes bibliográficas para evitar errores de citación.
examples.en: Greater rigor is required in the review of bibliographic sources to avoid citation errors.

cards.example_sentence: The rigor of the scientific method ensures reliable results.
cards.example_es: La rigurosidad del método científico garantiza resultados confiables.
cards.example_en: The rigor of the scientific method ensures reliable results.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a strict ruler measuring with rigor, echoing the Spanish riguroso in every precise line.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being logical, consistent, and forming a unified whole.

examples.es: La coherencia del argumento hizo que todos lo aceptaran.
examples.en: The coherence of the argument made everyone accept it.

examples.es: El profesor señaló que al ensayo le faltaba coherencia entre los párrafos introductorios y la conclusión.
examples.en: The professor pointed out that the essay lacked coherence between the introductory paragraphs and the conclusion.

examples.es: Para mejorar la coherencia de su presentación, el estudiante utilizó conectores lógicos entre las diapositivas.
examples.en: To improve the coherence of his presentation, the student used logical connectors between the slides.

cards.example_sentence: The coherence of the argument made everyone accept it.
cards.example_es: La coherencia del argumento hizo que todos lo aceptaran.
cards.example_en: The coherence of the argument made everyone accept it.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a coherent choir chanting coherencia in perfect harmony.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being clear, easily understood, or transparent.

examples.es: La claridad del agua del lago era impresionante.
examples.en: The clarity of the lake's water was impressive.

examples.es: El profesor insistió en la claridad de los argumentos presentados en el ensayo.
examples.en: The professor insisted on the clarity of the arguments presented in the essay.

examples.es: El orador se esforzó por mantener la claridad durante toda su presentación técnica.
examples.en: The speaker strived to maintain clarity throughout his technical presentation.

cards.example_sentence: The clarity of the lake's water was impressive.
cards.example_es: La claridad del agua del lago era impresionante.
cards.example_en: The clarity of the lake's water was impressive.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a crystal clear lake called Claridad reflecting perfect clarity

cards.definition_en: The quality of being brief and to the point in speech or writing.

examples.es: El profesor enfatizó que la concisión es fundamental para que los lectores comprendan rápidamente los argumentos principales de un ensayo.
examples.en: The professor emphasized that conciseness is essential for readers to quickly grasp the main arguments of an essay.

examples.es: Para mejorar la calidad de su informe técnico, debe priorizar la concisión y eliminar cualquier detalle irrelevante que distraiga al lector.
examples.en: To improve the quality of your technical report, you must prioritize conciseness and remove any irrelevant details that distract the reader.

examples.es: Durante la presentación, el orador fue elogiado por su notable concisión al explicar conceptos complejos en muy poco tiempo.
examples.en: During the presentation, the speaker was praised for his remarkable conciseness in explaining complex concepts in very little time.

cards.example_sentence: The professor emphasized that conciseness is essential for readers to quickly grasp the main arguments of an essay.
cards.example_es: El profesor enfatizó que la concisión es fundamental para que los lectores comprendan rápidamente los argumentos principales de un ensayo.
cards.example_en: The professor emphasized that conciseness is essential for readers to quickly grasp the main arguments of an essay.
cards.mnemonic_en: A tiny cactus says concisión while staying short and sharp.

cards.definition_en: To give more detail or expand upon something.

examples.es: El profesor pidió al estudiante que ampliara su argumento sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: The professor asked the student to elaborate on his argument regarding climate change.

examples.es: Durante la entrevista, el candidato tuvo que ampliar su experiencia previa en gestión de proyectos.
examples.en: During the interview, the candidate had to elaborate on his previous experience in project management.

examples.es: Es necesario ampliar la metodología utilizada en este estudio para entender mejor los resultados.
examples.en: It is necessary to elaborate on the methodology used in this study to better understand the results.

cards.example_sentence: The professor asked the student to elaborate on his argument regarding climate change.
cards.example_es: El profesor pidió al estudiante que ampliara su argumento sobre el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The professor asked the student to elaborate on his argument regarding climate change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lab that expands—ampliar the lab—to elaborate on the experiment.

cards.definition_en: To give a brief statement of the main points of something.

examples.es: Por favor, resume el artículo en tres frases.
examples.en: Please summarize the article in three sentences.

examples.es: El profesor pidió a los estudiantes que resuman los hallazgos principales de su investigación.
examples.en: The professor asked the students to summarize the main findings of their research.

examples.es: Es necesario resumir los datos estadísticos para que la audiencia comprenda las tendencias actuales.
examples.en: It is necessary to summarize the statistical data so that the audience understands the current trends.

cards.example_sentence: Please summarize the article in three sentences.
cards.example_es: Por favor, resume el artículo en tres frases.
cards.example_en: Please summarize the article in three sentences.
cards.mnemonic_en: Summarize feels like a résumé being trimmed, just as the Spanish Resumir means to condense.

cards.definition_en: A restatement of a text or passage using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity.

examples.es: ¿Puedes parafrasear lo que dije para que sea más claro?
examples.en: Can you paraphrase what I said to make it clearer?

examples.es: Es necesario parafrasear las citas directas para evitar el plagio en tu ensayo académico.
examples.en: It is necessary to paraphrase direct quotes to avoid plagiarism in your academic essay.

examples.es: El profesor nos pidió parafrasear el texto original usando nuestras propias palabras.
examples.en: The professor asked us to paraphrase the original text using our own words.

cards.example_sentence: Can you paraphrase what I said to make it clearer?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes parafrasear lo que dije para que sea más claro?
cards.example_en: Can you paraphrase what I said to make it clearer?
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a pair of phrase books dancing, each saying para-phrase as they paraphrase.

cards.definition_en: To quote or refer to a source as evidence or support.

examples.es: El artículo cita a varios expertos sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: The article cites several experts on climate change.

examples.es: Es obligatorio citar todas las fuentes bibliográficas al final del ensayo académico.
examples.en: It is mandatory to cite all bibliographic sources at the end of the academic essay.

examples.es: El abogado decidió citar un precedente legal para fortalecer su argumento ante el juez.
examples.en: The lawyer decided to cite a legal precedent to strengthen his argument before the judge.

cards.example_sentence: The article cites several experts on climate change.
cards.example_es: El artículo cita a varios expertos sobre el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The article cites several experts on climate change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chef sprinkling citations like a chef saying citar to flavor a dish

cards.definition_en: A source of information that is cited or consulted.

examples.es: Es importante referenciar todas las fuentes en tu trabajo de investigación.
examples.en: It is important to reference all sources in your research paper.

examples.es: El profesor pidió a los estudiantes referenciar cada cita directa en sus ensayos académicos.
examples.en: The professor asked the students to reference every direct quote in their academic essays.

examples.es: Debes referenciar los datos estadísticos que utilizaste para respaldar tu argumento principal.
examples.en: You must reference the statistical data you used to support your main argument.

cards.example_sentence: It is important to reference all sources in your research paper.
cards.example_es: Es importante referenciar todas las fuentes en tu trabajo de investigación.
cards.example_en: It is important to reference all sources in your research paper.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a referee handing out a reference sheet, just like you referenciar a source.

cards.definition_en: Notes placed at the bottom of a page that give additional information or citations.

examples.es: Las notas a pie de página explican los términos técnicos del artículo.
examples.en: The footnotes explain the technical terms of the article.

examples.es: El profesor pidió que incluyéramos las referencias bibliográficas en las notas a pie de página.
examples.en: The professor requested that we include the bibliographic references in the footnotes.

examples.es: Debes revisar las notas a pie de página para verificar la fuente de esta cita histórica.
examples.en: You must check the footnotes to verify the source of this historical quote.

cards.example_sentence: The footnotes explain the technical terms of the article.
cards.example_es: Las notas a pie de página explican los términos técnicos del artículo.
cards.example_en: The footnotes explain the technical terms of the article.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine tiny feet writing notes at the bottom of a page.

cards.definition_en: A supplementary section at the end of a book or document, or a small organ attached to the large intestine.

examples.es: El apéndice del libro contiene información adicional sobre la historia.
examples.en: The appendix of the book contains additional information about the history.

examples.es: Por favor, consulte el apéndice para ver las tablas estadísticas detalladas del estudio.
examples.en: Please consult the appendix to view the detailed statistical tables from the study.

examples.es: El autor incluyó un apéndice con los documentos originales para respaldar sus argumentos.
examples.en: The author included an appendix with the original documents to support his arguments.

cards.example_sentence: The appendix of the book contains additional information about the history.
cards.example_es: El apéndice del libro contiene información adicional sobre la historia.
cards.example_en: The appendix of the book contains additional information about the history.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tiny appendix attached to a book, just like the Spanish apéndice sticks onto the page.

cards.definition_en: A brief overview of a document's main points, usually placed at the beginning.

examples.es: El consultor redactó un resumen ejecutivo para presentar los hallazgos principales del informe a la junta directiva.
examples.en: The consultant drafted an executive summary to present the report's key findings to the board of directors.

examples.es: Antes de leer el documento completo de cincuenta páginas, los inversores revisaron el resumen ejecutivo para evaluar la viabilidad del proyecto.
examples.en: Before reading the full fifty-page document, the investors reviewed the executive summary to assess the project's viability.

examples.es: La directora solicitó que el resumen ejecutivo fuera conciso y destacara únicamente las recomendaciones estratégicas más importantes.
examples.en: The director requested that the executive summary be concise and highlight only the most important strategic recommendations.

cards.example_sentence: The consultant drafted an executive summary to present the report's key findings to the board of directors.
cards.example_es: El consultor redactó un resumen ejecutivo para presentar los hallazgos principales del informe a la junta directiva.
cards.example_en: The consultant drafted an executive summary to present the report's key findings to the board of directors.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a boss (executive) giving a quick sum—'re-sum-en' as the executive summary.

cards.definition_en: A question that guides the focus and direction of a research study.

examples.es: La pregunta de investigación de mi tesis es cómo afecta el cambio climático a la biodiversidad.
examples.en: The research question of my thesis is how climate change affects biodiversity.

examples.es: Antes de comenzar el experimento, el profesor nos pidió que formuláramos una pregunta de investigación clara y concisa.
examples.en: Before starting the experiment, the professor asked us to formulate a clear and concise research question.

examples.es: El comité evaluador rechazó el proyecto porque la pregunta de investigación no estaba suficientemente delimitada.
examples.en: The review committee rejected the project because the research question was not sufficiently defined.

cards.example_sentence: The research question of my thesis is how climate change affects biodiversity.
cards.example_es: La pregunta de investigación de mi tesis es cómo afecta el cambio climático a la biodiversidad.
cards.example_en: The research question of my thesis is how climate change affects biodiversity.
cards.mnemonic_en: A researcher knocks on a door marked PRETTY GUNTA, demanding the answer to his question.

cards.definition_en: The range or extent of topics covered by the study.
collocations: the scope of the study includes
within the scope of the study
the scope of the study was limited

examples.es: El ámbito de estudio de la investigación es la biología molecular.
examples.en: The scope of the study is molecular biology.

examples.es: Debemos definir claramente el ámbito de estudio para evitar resultados irrelevantes en el informe.
examples.en: We must clearly define the scope of the study to avoid irrelevant results in the report.

examples.es: El comité académico decidió ampliar el ámbito de estudio para incluir datos demográficos adicionales.
examples.en: The academic committee decided to expand the scope of the study to include additional demographic data.

cards.example_sentence: The scope of the study is molecular biology.
cards.example_es: El ámbito de estudio de la investigación es la biología molecular.
cards.example_en: The scope of the study is molecular biology.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a scope zooming over the study's ambient field.

cards.definition_en: A specific area or focus within a broader field of study or investigation.

examples.es: Mi línea de investigación se centra en la inteligencia artificial.
examples.en: My research line focuses on artificial intelligence.

examples.es: El departamento ha definido una nueva línea de investigación sobre el cambio climático en zonas costeras.
examples.en: The department has defined a new research line regarding climate change in coastal areas.

examples.es: Para su tesis doctoral, ella eligió una línea de investigación poco explorada en la literatura económica.
examples.en: For her doctoral thesis, she chose a research line that is rarely explored in economic literature.

cards.example_sentence: My research line focuses on artificial intelligence.
cards.example_es: Mi línea de investigación se centra en la inteligencia artificial.
cards.example_en: My research line focuses on artificial intelligence.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a scientist drawing a bright red research line across a map, like a Spanish línea

cards.definition_en: A set of ideas and concepts that provides a structure for understanding or analyzing something.

examples.es: El investigador presentó un nuevo marco conceptual para entender la migración urbana.
examples.en: The researcher presented a new conceptual framework to understand urban migration.

examples.es: La tesis doctoral propone un marco conceptual sólido para analizar el impacto de la inteligencia artificial en el mercado laboral.
examples.en: The doctoral thesis proposes a solid conceptual framework to analyze the impact of artificial intelligence on the job market.

examples.es: Antes de iniciar el experimento, el equipo estableció un marco conceptual que define las variables clave del estudio.
examples.en: Before starting the experiment, the team established a conceptual framework that defines the key variables of the study.

cards.example_sentence: The researcher presented a new conceptual framework to understand urban migration.
cards.example_es: El investigador presentó un nuevo marco conceptual para entender la migración urbana.
cards.example_en: The researcher presented a new conceptual framework to understand urban migration.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a FRAME (marco) that holds a CONCEPT, a conceptual framework.

cards.definition_en: A research gap is an area or topic that has not yet been studied or is insufficiently explored in existing literature.

examples.es: El estudio identifica un vacío de investigación en la relación entre la dieta y la salud mental.
examples.en: The study identifies a research gap in the relationship between diet and mental health.

examples.es: La tesis doctoral aborda un vacío de investigación sobre el impacto de la inteligencia artificial en el mercado laboral rural.
examples.en: The doctoral thesis addresses a research gap regarding the impact of artificial intelligence on the rural labor market.

examples.es: Los autores señalan un vacío de investigación importante al no existir datos suficientes sobre el comportamiento de los consumidores en mercados emergentes.
examples.en: The authors point out a significant research gap due to the lack of sufficient data on consumer behavior in emerging markets.

cards.example_sentence: The study identifies a research gap in the relationship between diet and mental health.
cards.example_es: El estudio identifica un vacío de investigación en la relación entre la dieta y la salud mental.
cards.example_en: The study identifies a research gap in the relationship between diet and mental health.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a vacant (vacío) research lab with a big hole (gap) in the data.

cards.definition_en: A significant or important result discovered in a study or investigation.

examples.es: El hallazgo clave del estudio fue la relación entre la dieta y la salud cardiovascular.
examples.en: The key finding of the study was the relationship between diet and cardiovascular health.

examples.es: Tras analizar los datos, los investigadores presentaron el hallazgo clave que explica el descenso en las ventas.
examples.en: After analyzing the data, the researchers presented the key finding that explains the decline in sales.

examples.es: El informe final destaca un hallazgo clave sobre la eficacia de las nuevas políticas educativas en zonas rurales.
examples.en: The final report highlights a key finding regarding the effectiveness of the new educational policies in rural areas.

cards.example_sentence: The key finding of the study was the relationship between diet and cardiovascular health.
cards.example_es: El hallazgo clave del estudio fue la relación entre la dieta y la salud cardiovascular.
cards.example_en: The key finding of the study was the relationship between diet and cardiovascular health.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a golden key finding a hidden hallazgo in a dusty chest

cards.definition_en: A highly influential piece of research or writing that forms the basis for later developments.

examples.es: El tratado de Adam Smith sobre la riqueza de las naciones se considera un trabajo seminal que estableció los principios fundamentales de la economía moderna.
examples.en: Adam Smith's treatise on the wealth of nations is considered a seminal work that established the fundamental principles of modern economics.

examples.es: La profesora citó el trabajo seminal de Keynes para explicar los fundamentos de la macroeconomía moderna.
examples.en: The professor cited the seminal work of Keynes to explain the foundations of modern macroeconomics.

examples.es: Su tesis doctoral se convirtió en un trabajo seminal que transformó nuestra comprensión sobre la inteligencia artificial.
examples.en: Her doctoral thesis became a seminal work that transformed our understanding of artificial intelligence.

cards.example_sentence: Adam Smith's treatise on the wealth of nations is considered a seminal work that established the fundamental principles of modern economics.
cards.example_es: El tratado de Adam Smith sobre la riqueza de las naciones se considera un trabajo seminal que estableció los principios fundamentales de la economía moderna.
cards.example_en: Adam Smith's treatise on the wealth of nations is considered a seminal work that established the fundamental principles of modern economics.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a seed (seminal) sprouting a giant work of art.

cards.definition_en: A contribution that is new, unique, or created for the first time.

examples.es: Su contribución original al proyecto fue fundamental para obtener la aprobación del comité.
examples.en: His original contribution to the project was essential for gaining the committee's approval.

examples.es: La tesis doctoral destaca por ser una contribución original al campo de la astrofísica moderna.
examples.en: The doctoral thesis stands out for being an original contribution to the field of modern astrophysics.

examples.es: El autor busca realizar una contribución original que desafíe las teorías económicas establecidas.
examples.en: The author seeks to make an original contribution that challenges established economic theories.

cards.example_sentence: His original contribution to the project was essential for gaining the committee's approval.
cards.example_es: Su contribución original al proyecto fue fundamental para obtener la aprobación del comité.
cards.example_en: His original contribution to the project was essential for gaining the committee's approval.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of an original piece as a 'contribución' to the exhibit.

cards.definition_en: Additional possible paths or options for exploration.

examples.es: El estudio concluye sugiriendo líneas futuras de investigación sobre el impacto del cambio climático en la agricultura.
examples.en: The study concludes by suggesting further avenues of research regarding the impact of climate change on agriculture.

examples.es: Tras analizar los datos actuales, el comité propuso líneas futuras para expandir la presencia de la empresa en mercados internacionales.
examples.en: After analyzing the current data, the committee proposed further avenues to expand the company's presence in international markets.

examples.es: La tesis identifica varias líneas futuras que permitirían aplicar este modelo matemático a problemas de ingeniería civil.
examples.en: The thesis identifies several further avenues that would allow for the application of this mathematical model to civil engineering problems.

cards.example_sentence: The study concludes by suggesting further avenues of research regarding the impact of climate change on agriculture.
cards.example_es: El estudio concluye sugiriendo líneas futuras de investigación sobre el impacto del cambio climático en la agricultura.
cards.example_en: The study concludes by suggesting further avenues of research regarding the impact of climate change on agriculture.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a road of future lines, a further avenue stretching into tomorrow.

title: News and Society
description: Advanced vocabulary for current affairs, social issues, media, technology, and global events.

cards.definition_en: A headline is a brief, attention‑grabbing title that appears at the top of a news article or other written piece.

examples.es: El titular del periódico anunciaba la victoria del equipo.
examples.en: The headline in the newspaper announced the team's victory.

examples.es: El periodista escribió un titular impactante para captar la atención de los lectores.
examples.en: The journalist wrote a shocking headline to grab the readers' attention.

examples.es: Todo el mundo comentaba el titular principal sobre la nueva ley económica.
examples.en: Everyone was discussing the main headline about the new economic law.

cards.example_sentence: The headline in the newspaper announced the team's victory.
cards.example_es: El titular del periódico anunciaba la victoria del equipo.
cards.example_en: The headline in the newspaper announced the team's victory.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a head with a line of headlines stitched across its crown like a title.

cards.definition_en: The reporting of an event or topic by various media outlets.

examples.es: La cobertura mediática del accidente fue extensa y constante.
examples.en: The media coverage of the accident was extensive and continuous.

examples.es: El candidato se quejó de que la cobertura mediática estaba sesgada en su contra.
examples.en: The candidate complained that the media coverage was biased against him.

examples.es: La organización benéfica espera obtener más cobertura mediática para su campaña de recaudación de fondos.
