examples.en: The charity hopes to gain more media coverage for its fundraising campaign.

cards.example_sentence: The media coverage of the accident was extensive and continuous.
cards.example_es: La cobertura mediática del accidente fue extensa y constante.
cards.example_en: The media coverage of the accident was extensive and continuous.
cards.mnemonic_en: The media spreads a blanket (cobertura) over the story.

cards.definition_en: A feature article is a detailed, in‑depth newspaper or magazine story that explores a topic more comprehensively than a news report.

examples.es: Leí un artículo de fondo sobre la crisis climática en el periódico.
examples.en: I read a feature article about the climate crisis in the newspaper.

examples.es: La revista publicó un artículo de fondo analizando el impacto de la inteligencia artificial en el empleo.
examples.en: The magazine published a feature article analyzing the impact of artificial intelligence on employment.

examples.es: El periodista pasó tres meses investigando para escribir su artículo de fondo sobre la pobreza urbana.
examples.en: The journalist spent three months investigating to write his feature article about urban poverty.

cards.example_sentence: I read a feature article about the climate crisis in the newspaper.
cards.example_es: Leí un artículo de fondo sobre la crisis climática en el periódico.
cards.example_en: I read a feature article about the climate crisis in the newspaper.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a feature article perched on the fondo of a desk like a prized painting.

cards.definition_en: An article expressing the opinion of the editor or the publication.

examples.es: Leí la editorial del periódico sobre la crisis climática.
examples.en: I read the editorial in the newspaper about the climate crisis.

cards.example_sentence: I read the editorial in the newspaper about the climate crisis.
cards.example_es: Leí la editorial del periódico sobre la crisis climática.
cards.example_en: I read the editorial in the newspaper about the climate crisis.

cards.definition_en: A source that can be trusted to provide accurate and trustworthy information.

examples.es: Este artículo es una fuente fiable para estudiar la historia.
examples.en: This article is a reliable source for studying history.

examples.es: El periodista siempre verifica la información con una fuente fiable antes de publicar la noticia.
examples.en: The journalist always verifies the information with a reliable source before publishing the news.

examples.es: Es difícil encontrar una fuente fiable sobre este tema en las redes sociales.
examples.en: It is difficult to find a reliable source on this topic on social media.

cards.example_sentence: This article is a reliable source for studying history.
cards.example_es: Este artículo es una fuente fiable para estudiar la historia.
cards.example_en: This article is a reliable source for studying history.
cards.mnemonic_en: A reliable source is like a steady fountain (fuente) that never stops flowing truth.

cards.definition_en: False or inaccurate information that is spread deliberately or unintentionally.

examples.es: La desinformación sobre la vacuna está generando miedo.
examples.en: Misinformation about the vaccine is causing fear.

examples.es: Las redes sociales facilitan la rápida propagación de la desinformación durante las elecciones.
examples.en: Social media facilitates the rapid spread of misinformation during elections.

examples.es: El gobierno lanzó una campaña para combatir la desinformación en los medios digitales.
examples.en: The government launched a campaign to combat misinformation in digital media.

cards.example_sentence: Misinformation about the vaccine is causing fear.
cards.example_es: La desinformación sobre la vacuna está generando miedo.
cards.example_en: Misinformation about the vaccine is causing fear.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a missed info sign exploding, spreading desinformación everywhere.

cards.definition_en: Information that is incorrect or misleading.

examples.es: El gobierno lanzó una campaña para educar a los ciudadanos sobre cómo identificar información falsa en las noticias.
examples.en: The government launched a campaign to educate citizens on how to identify false information in the news.

examples.es: Es preocupante ver cómo se propaga información falsa durante los procesos electorales en el país.
examples.en: It is worrying to see how false information spreads during the country's electoral processes.

examples.es: La empresa tecnológica fue multada por no eliminar la información falsa que circulaba en su plataforma.
examples.en: The tech company was fined for failing to remove the false information circulating on its platform.

cards.example_sentence: The government launched a campaign to educate citizens on how to identify false information in the news.
cards.example_es: El gobierno lanzó una campaña para educar a los ciudadanos sobre cómo identificar información falsa en las noticias.
cards.example_en: The government launched a campaign to educate citizens on how to identify false information in the news.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a newspaper headline stamped with the Spanish word falso, showing false information.

cards.definition_en: Urgent or newly received news that is reported immediately.

examples.es: ¡Noticia de última hora! El presidente ha anunciado su renuncia.
examples.en: Breaking news! The president has announced his resignation.

examples.es: Interrumpimos la programación habitual para presentar una noticia de última hora sobre el incendio forestal.
examples.en: We are interrupting our regular programming to bring you breaking news about the forest fire.

examples.es: Los periodistas corrieron hacia el lugar tras recibir una noticia de última hora sobre el accidente ferroviario.
examples.en: Journalists rushed to the scene after receiving breaking news regarding the train accident.

cards.example_sentence: Breaking news! The president has announced his resignation.
cards.example_es: ¡Noticia de última hora! El presidente ha anunciado su renuncia.
cards.example_en: Breaking news! The president has announced his resignation.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a broken clock at the hour, shouting the latest breaking news.

cards.definition_en: Live coverage is the real-time reporting or broadcasting of an event as it happens.

examples.es: La cobertura en directo del concierto se transmitirá en nuestro sitio web.
examples.en: The live coverage of the concert will be streamed on our website.

examples.es: El canal de noticias interrumpió su programación habitual para ofrecer una cobertura en directo del desastre natural.
examples.en: The news channel interrupted its regular programming to provide live coverage of the natural disaster.

examples.es: Los periodistas deportivos están preparando una cobertura en directo desde el estadio antes del inicio del partido.
examples.en: The sports journalists are preparing live coverage from the stadium before the match begins.

cards.example_sentence: The live coverage of the concert will be streamed on our website.
cards.example_es: La cobertura en directo del concierto se transmitirá en nuestro sitio web.
cards.example_en: The live coverage of the concert will be streamed on our website.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cover (cobertura) being broadcast live, like a TV show unfolding in real time.

cards.definition_en: A change that adds new information or improves something to make it current.

examples.es: La actualización del software mejoró el rendimiento del dispositivo.
examples.en: The update of the software improved the device's performance.

examples.es: El periodista publicó una actualización sobre la situación en la zona de conflicto.
examples.en: The journalist published an update regarding the situation in the conflict zone.

examples.es: Recibimos una actualización constante sobre los resultados de las elecciones nacionales.
examples.en: We received a constant update on the results of the national elections.

cards.example_sentence: The update of the software improved the device's performance.
cards.example_es: La actualización del software mejoró el rendimiento del dispositivo.
cards.example_en: The update of the software improved the device's performance.
cards.mnemonic_en: Update feels like saying actual, so picture installing the latest actual news as an actualización.

cards.definition_en: A survey that asks people for their views on a particular topic.

examples.es: La encuesta de opinión mostró que la mayoría apoya la medida.
examples.en: The opinion poll showed that the majority supports the measure.

examples.es: El candidato lidera la última encuesta de opinión antes de las elecciones presidenciales.
examples.en: The candidate is leading the latest opinion poll before the presidential election.

examples.es: Los medios publicaron una encuesta de opinión sobre los hábitos de consumo de los jóvenes.
examples.en: The media published an opinion poll regarding the consumption habits of young people.

cards.example_sentence: The opinion poll showed that the majority supports the measure.
cards.example_es: La encuesta de opinión mostró que la mayoría apoya la medida.
cards.example_en: The opinion poll showed that the majority supports the measure.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a poll where everyone shouts their opinión like a noisy encuesta

cards.definition_en: The percentage or number of eligible voters who actually cast a ballot in an election.

examples.es: La participación electoral en las últimas elecciones fue del 70%.
examples.en: Voter turnout in the last election was 70%.

examples.es: El gobierno está preocupado por la baja participación electoral entre los ciudadanos más jóvenes.
examples.en: The government is concerned about the low voter turnout among younger citizens.

examples.es: Se espera que una mayor participación electoral cambie el resultado de la votación este domingo.
examples.en: A higher voter turnout is expected to change the outcome of the vote this Sunday.

cards.example_sentence: Voter turnout in the last election was 70%.
cards.example_es: La participación electoral en las últimas elecciones fue del 70%.
cards.example_en: Voter turnout in the last election was 70%.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine voters turning out, the same as participación electoral in a bustling poll.

cards.definition_en: A coordinated effort by a candidate or political party to win votes in an upcoming election.

examples.es: La campaña electoral comenzó la semana pasada y ya se están escuchando los debates.
examples.en: The election campaign began last week and the debates are already being heard.

examples.es: Los candidatos recorren todo el país durante la campaña electoral para ganar votos.
examples.en: The candidates travel across the country during the election campaign to win votes.

examples.es: El partido político gastó millones de dólares en publicidad para su campaña electoral.
examples.en: The political party spent millions of dollars on advertising for its election campaign.

cards.example_sentence: The election campaign began last week and the debates are already being heard.
cards.example_es: La campaña electoral comenzó la semana pasada y ya se están escuchando los debates.
cards.example_en: The election campaign began last week and the debates are already being heard.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a field (campo) full of voters electing a leader during the campaign.

cards.definition_en: A public debate is an open discussion where participants exchange differing views on a topic in front of an audience.

examples.es: El debate público sobre el cambio climático atrajo a miles de espectadores.
examples.en: The public debate on climate change attracted thousands of viewers.

examples.es: La nueva ley de educación ha generado un intenso debate público en todo el país.
examples.en: The new education law has sparked an intense public debate across the country.

examples.es: Los candidatos presidenciales se preparan para participar en un debate público televisado esta noche.
examples.en: The presidential candidates are preparing to participate in a televised public debate tonight.

cards.example_sentence: The public debate on climate change attracted thousands of viewers.
cards.example_es: El debate público sobre el cambio climático atrajo a miles de espectadores.
cards.example_en: The public debate on climate change attracted thousands of viewers.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a public square where a de-bate of speakers shout the word public.

cards.definition_en: The process of dividing something into opposing groups or of aligning electric charges in opposite directions.

examples.es: La polarización política en el país ha aumentado en los últimos años.
examples.en: Political polarization in the country has increased in recent years.

examples.es: Las redes sociales han acelerado la polarización de la opinión pública sobre temas controvertidos.
examples.en: Social media has accelerated the polarization of public opinion on controversial topics.

examples.es: El debate electoral reflejó una profunda polarización entre los votantes de ambos partidos.
examples.en: The electoral debate reflected a deep polarization between the voters of both parties.

cards.example_sentence: Political polarization in the country has increased in recent years.
cards.example_es: La polarización política en el país ha aumentado en los últimos años.
cards.example_en: Political polarization in the country has increased in recent years.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a pole emitting a bright beam that polarizes everything, echoing the Spanish polarización.

cards.definition_en: A political approach that seeks to appeal to ordinary people who feel their concerns are ignored by established elites.

examples.es: El populismo ha ganado terreno en varios países de América Latina.
examples.en: Populism has gained ground in several Latin American countries.

examples.es: Los analistas políticos debaten sobre cómo el populismo influye en las elecciones modernas.
examples.en: Political analysts debate how populism influences modern elections.

examples.es: El discurso del candidato se basó principalmente en el populismo para atraer a los votantes descontentos.
examples.en: The candidate's speech relied heavily on populism to appeal to disgruntled voters.

cards.example_sentence: Populism has gained ground in several Latin American countries.
cards.example_es: El populismo ha ganado terreno en varios países de América Latina.
cards.example_en: Populism has gained ground in several Latin American countries.
cards.mnemonic_en: Populism sounds like Spanish 'populismo', picture a crowd popping popcorn for a charismatic leader.

cards.definition_en: The act or system of governing, or the way an organization or society is managed and controlled.

examples.es: La gobernanza de la empresa se basa en la transparencia y la participación de los empleados.
examples.en: The governance of the company is based on transparency and employee participation.

examples.es: El nuevo tratado internacional busca mejorar la gobernanza global sobre el uso de la inteligencia artificial.
examples.en: The new international treaty seeks to improve global governance regarding the use of artificial intelligence.

examples.es: Los ciudadanos exigieron una mejor gobernanza en la gestión de los recursos hídricos durante la sequía.
examples.en: Citizens demanded better governance in the management of water resources during the drought.

cards.example_sentence: The governance of the company is based on transparency and employee participation.
cards.example_es: La gobernanza de la empresa se basa en la transparencia y la participación de los empleados.
cards.example_en: The governance of the company is based on transparency and employee participation.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a governor handing a SA map, showing his governance

cards.definition_en: The quality of being clear, open, or easily understood, or the condition of allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be seen.

examples.es: La transparencia del gobierno es esencial para la confianza de los ciudadanos.
examples.en: Government transparency is essential for citizens' trust.

examples.es: La empresa prometió mayor transparencia en sus procesos de contratación para evitar el favoritismo.
examples.en: The company promised greater transparency in its hiring processes to avoid favoritism.

examples.es: Los periodistas exigen transparencia sobre el uso de los fondos públicos durante la crisis.
examples.en: Journalists are demanding transparency regarding the use of public funds during the crisis.

cards.example_sentence: Government transparency is essential for citizens' trust.
cards.example_es: La transparencia del gobierno es esencial para la confianza de los ciudadanos.
cards.example_en: Government transparency is essential for citizens' trust.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a clear glass that lets all transparencia shine through, showing everything inside.

cards.definition_en: The obligation to accept responsibility for one's actions and decisions.

examples.es: La rendición de cuentas es fundamental para la transparencia en el gobierno.
examples.en: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.

examples.es: Los ciudadanos exigen mayor rendición de cuentas a las empresas que contaminan el medio ambiente.
examples.en: Citizens are demanding greater accountability from companies that pollute the environment.

examples.es: El nuevo director implementó un sistema de rendición de cuentas para evaluar el desempeño de cada departamento.
examples.en: The new director implemented an accountability system to evaluate the performance of each department.

cards.example_sentence: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.
cards.example_es: La rendición de cuentas es fundamental para la transparencia en el gobierno.
cards.example_en: Accountability is essential for transparency in government.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a ledger where each cuenta is an account you must hand over, making you fully accountable

cards.definition_en: Dishonest or fraudulent conduct by those in power, often involving bribery or the misuse of authority for personal gain.

examples.es: La corrupción en el gobierno ha disminuido gracias a las nuevas leyes.
examples.en: Corruption in the government has decreased thanks to the new laws.

examples.es: El periodista publicó un informe detallado sobre la corrupción dentro de la empresa constructora.
examples.en: The journalist published a detailed report about the corruption within the construction company.

examples.es: Muchos ciudadanos protestaron en las calles para exigir el fin de la corrupción en el sistema judicial.
examples.en: Many citizens protested in the streets to demand an end to the corruption in the judicial system.

cards.example_sentence: Corruption in the government has decreased thanks to the new laws.
cards.example_es: La corrupción en el gobierno ha disminuido gracias a las nuevas leyes.
cards.example_en: Corruption in the government has decreased thanks to the new laws.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a corrupt ion sneaking into the core of power.

cards.definition_en: A condition of being unequal, especially in status, rights, or opportunities.

examples.es: La desigualdad salarial entre hombres y mujeres sigue siendo un problema grave.
examples.en: Salary inequality between men and women remains a serious problem.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas políticas para reducir la desigualdad en el acceso a la educación.
examples.en: The government implemented new policies to reduce inequality in access to education.

examples.es: Los manifestantes salieron a las calles para protestar contra la creciente desigualdad económica en el país.
examples.en: Protesters took to the streets to demonstrate against the growing economic inequality in the country.

cards.example_sentence: Salary inequality between men and women remains a serious problem.
cards.example_es: La desigualdad salarial entre hombres y mujeres sigue siendo un problema grave.
cards.example_en: Salary inequality between men and women remains a serious problem.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a broken scale labeled Desigualdad, its pans uneven, showing inequality.

cards.definition_en: The movement of individuals or groups within or between social classes in a society.

examples.es: La movilidad social es un indicador clave de la igualdad de oportunidades en una sociedad.
examples.en: Social mobility is a key indicator of equal opportunity in a society.

examples.es: El acceso a una educación de calidad es fundamental para fomentar la movilidad social en las comunidades desfavorecidas.
examples.en: Access to quality education is essential to foster social mobility in disadvantaged communities.

examples.es: Muchos economistas argumentan que el estancamiento de los salarios está limitando la movilidad social de la clase trabajadora.
examples.en: Many economists argue that stagnant wages are limiting social mobility for the working class.

cards.example_sentence: Social mobility is a key indicator of equal opportunity in a society.
cards.example_es: La movilidad social es un indicador clave de la igualdad de oportunidades en una sociedad.
cards.example_en: Social mobility is a key indicator of equal opportunity in a society.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a social ladder on a moving bus called movilidad climbing upward

cards.definition_en: The amount of money required to cover basic expenses such as housing, food, taxes, and healthcare in a specific location.

examples.es: El aumento constante en el costo de vida dificulta que muchas familias lleguen a fin de mes.
examples.en: The constant increase in the cost of living makes it difficult for many families to make ends meet.

examples.es: Debido al alto costo de vida en la ciudad, muchos jóvenes deciden mudarse a las afueras.
examples.en: Due to the high cost of living in the city, many young people decide to move to the suburbs.

examples.es: El gobierno anunció nuevas medidas para frenar el impacto del costo de vida en los sectores más vulnerables.
examples.en: The government announced new measures to curb the impact of the cost of living on the most vulnerable sectors.

cards.example_sentence: The constant increase in the cost of living makes it difficult for many families to make ends meet.
cards.example_es: El aumento constante en el costo de vida dificulta que muchas familias lleguen a fin de mes.
cards.example_en: The constant increase in the cost of living makes it difficult for many families to make ends meet.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine paying the cost (costo) of a lively (living) party.

cards.definition_en: A general increase in prices and a corresponding decline in the purchasing power of money.

examples.es: La inflación ha aumentado el costo de los alimentos.
examples.en: Inflation has increased the cost of food.

examples.es: El banco central subió las tasas de interés para frenar la inflación.
examples.en: The central bank raised interest rates to curb inflation.

examples.es: Muchos ciudadanos están preocupados por cómo la inflación afecta sus ahorros.
examples.en: Many citizens are worried about how inflation affects their savings.

cards.example_sentence: Inflation has increased the cost of food.
cards.example_es: La inflación ha aumentado el costo de los alimentos.
cards.example_en: Inflation has increased the cost of food.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a balloon inflating as 'inflación' lifts prices.

cards.definition_en: A period of economic decline marked by reduced trade, production, and rising unemployment.

examples.es: La recesión ha provocado un aumento del desempleo en el país.
examples.en: The recession has caused an increase in unemployment in the country.

examples.es: Muchos pequeños negocios cerraron sus puertas debido a la grave recesión económica.
examples.en: Many small businesses closed their doors due to the severe economic recession.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas medidas fiscales para intentar salir de la recesión.
examples.en: The government implemented new fiscal measures to try to emerge from the recession.

cards.example_sentence: The recession has caused an increase in unemployment in the country.
cards.example_es: La recesión ha provocado un aumento del desempleo en el país.
cards.example_en: The recession has caused an increase in unemployment in the country.
cards.mnemonic_en: When the economy goes into recession it takes a recess like a broken tooth, and recesión sounds just like recess.

cards.definition_en: A period when an economy regains growth and improves after a downturn.

examples.es: La recuperación económica del país ha sido más rápida de lo esperado.
examples.en: The country's economic recovery has been faster than expected.

examples.es: El gobierno anunció nuevas medidas para fomentar la recuperación económica tras la crisis financiera.
examples.en: The government announced new measures to foster economic recovery after the financial crisis.

examples.es: Muchos analistas coinciden en que la recuperación económica depende de la estabilidad del mercado laboral.
examples.en: Many analysts agree that economic recovery depends on the stability of the labor market.

cards.example_sentence: The country's economic recovery has been faster than expected.
cards.example_es: La recuperación económica del país ha sido más rápida de lo esperado.
cards.example_en: The country's economic recovery has been faster than expected.
cards.mnemonic_en: Eco‑nomics recovered: picture a shattered piggy bank being glued back together.

cards.definition_en: The supply of workers and the demand for jobs in an economy, encompassing employment conditions and opportunities.

examples.es: El mercado laboral está experimentando una alta demanda de profesionales de tecnología.
examples.en: The labor market is experiencing a high demand for technology professionals.

examples.es: La crisis económica ha provocado una gran inestabilidad en el mercado laboral actual.
examples.en: The economic crisis has caused great instability in the current labor market.

examples.es: El gobierno ha implementado nuevas reformas para fomentar la flexibilidad en el mercado laboral.
examples.en: The government has implemented new reforms to encourage flexibility in the labor market.

cards.example_sentence: The labor market is experiencing a high demand for technology professionals.
cards.example_es: El mercado laboral está experimentando una alta demanda de profesionales de tecnología.
cards.example_en: The labor market is experiencing a high demand for technology professionals.
cards.mnemonic_en: A lab rat in the mercado sells job offers.

cards.definition_en: A situation in which the supply of affordable housing is insufficient to meet demand, causing widespread difficulty in obtaining suitable homes.

examples.es: La crisis habitacional está obligando a muchas familias a buscar refugio en viviendas temporales.
examples.en: The housing crisis is forcing many families to seek shelter in temporary homes.

examples.es: El gobierno ha anunciado nuevas medidas para frenar la crisis habitacional en las grandes ciudades.
examples.en: The government has announced new measures to curb the housing crisis in major cities.

examples.es: Los jóvenes tienen dificultades para independizarse debido a la grave crisis habitacional que afecta al país.
examples.en: Young people struggle to move out on their own due to the severe housing crisis affecting the country.

cards.example_sentence: The housing crisis is forcing many families to seek shelter in temporary homes.
cards.example_es: La crisis habitacional está obligando a muchas familias a buscar refugio en viviendas temporales.
cards.example_en: The housing crisis is forcing many families to seek shelter in temporary homes.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a house collapsing in a crisis, like a broken habit‑home emergency.

cards.definition_en: A person who lacks a permanent residence or home.

examples.es: El ayuntamiento ha lanzado un programa de asistencia para ayudar a las personas sin techo durante el invierno.
examples.en: The city council has launched an assistance program to help homeless people during the winter.

examples.es: Muchos voluntarios reparten comida caliente en la plaza principal a la población sin techo cada noche.
examples.en: Many volunteers distribute hot food in the main square to the homeless population every night.

examples.es: La organización benéfica construyó nuevos albergues para ofrecer un lugar seguro a quienes se encuentran sin techo.
examples.en: The charity built new shelters to offer a safe place to those who are homeless.

cards.example_sentence: The city council has launched an assistance program to help homeless people during the winter.
cards.example_es: El ayuntamiento ha lanzado un programa de asistencia para ayudar a las personas sin techo durante el invierno.
cards.example_en: The city council has launched an assistance program to help homeless people during the winter.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a homeless person under the sky, sin techo, with no roof over their head.

cards.definition_en: The organized provision of medical services to maintain or improve people's health.

examples.es: El acceso a la atención sanitaria es un derecho fundamental.
examples.en: Access to healthcare is a fundamental right.

examples.es: El gobierno anunció un aumento en el presupuesto destinado a la atención sanitaria pública.
examples.en: The government announced an increase in the budget allocated for public healthcare.

examples.es: Las nuevas reformas buscan mejorar la calidad de la atención sanitaria en las zonas rurales.
examples.en: The new reforms aim to improve the quality of healthcare in rural areas.

cards.example_sentence: Access to healthcare is a fundamental right.
cards.example_es: El acceso a la atención sanitaria es un derecho fundamental.
cards.example_en: Access to healthcare is a fundamental right.
cards.mnemonic_en: Healthcare sounds like heal and care, picture a doctor healing a patient with a sanitary mask.

cards.definition_en: A state of psychological well‑being that enables a person to handle daily stresses, work productively, and contribute to society.

examples.es: La salud mental es tan importante como la salud física.
examples.en: Mental health is as important as physical health.

examples.es: Las empresas modernas ofrecen programas de apoyo para mejorar la salud mental de sus empleados.
examples.en: Modern companies offer support programs to improve the mental health of their employees.

examples.es: El gobierno ha lanzado una campaña nacional para reducir el estigma asociado a la salud mental.
examples.en: The government has launched a national campaign to reduce the stigma associated with mental health.

cards.example_sentence: Mental health is as important as physical health.
cards.example_es: La salud mental es tan importante como la salud física.
cards.example_en: Mental health is as important as physical health.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a therapist serving a giant salad to boost mental health.

cards.definition_en: A state of being comfortable, healthy, or happy.

examples.es: El programa de ejercicios mejora el bienestar de los participantes.
examples.en: The exercise program improves the participants' well-being.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas políticas para proteger el bienestar de los ciudadanos.
examples.en: The government implemented new policies to protect the well-being of the citizens.

examples.es: Los expertos advierten que el estrés laboral afecta negativamente el bienestar de los empleados.
examples.en: Experts warn that work stress negatively affects the well-being of employees.

cards.example_sentence: The exercise program improves the participants' well-being.
cards.example_es: El programa de ejercicios mejora el bienestar de los participantes.
cards.example_en: The exercise program improves the participants' well-being.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a well that says bien and a statue that stands estar, together they form well-being.

cards.definition_en: A demographic situation in which a large proportion of the population is elderly.

examples.es: El envejecimiento de la población es un desafío para los sistemas de salud.
examples.en: The aging population is a challenge for health systems.

examples.es: Muchos países europeos están implementando reformas en las pensiones debido al envejecimiento de la población.
examples.en: Many European countries are implementing pension reforms due to the aging population.

examples.es: La escasez de mano de obra joven es una consecuencia directa del envejecimiento de la población en esta región.
examples.en: The shortage of young labor is a direct consequence of the aging population in this region.

cards.example_sentence: The aging population is a challenge for health systems.
cards.example_es: El envejecimiento de la población es un desafío para los sistemas de salud.
cards.example_en: The aging population is a challenge for health systems.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an aging crowd, a senior población marching together.

cards.definition_en: The movement of people or groups from one location to another, typically for settlement, work, or environmental reasons.

examples.es: La migración de aves hacia el sur comienza en otoño.
examples.en: The migration of birds to the south begins in autumn.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas políticas para gestionar la migración en la frontera.
examples.en: The government implemented new policies to manage migration at the border.

examples.es: Muchos expertos analizan las causas económicas que provocan la migración masiva de trabajadores.
examples.en: Many experts analyze the economic causes that trigger the mass migration of workers.

cards.example_sentence: The migration of birds to the south begins in autumn.
cards.example_es: La migración de aves hacia el sur comienza en otoño.
cards.example_en: The migration of birds to the south begins in autumn.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a flock of birds on a 'migra'tion, flying south together.

cards.definition_en: A person who has been forced to leave their country to escape war, persecution, or disaster.

examples.es: El refugiado encontró un nuevo hogar en la ciudad.
examples.en: The refugee found a new home in the city.

examples.es: La organización humanitaria proporcionó comida y mantas a cada refugiado en el campamento.
examples.en: The humanitarian organization provided food and blankets to every refugee in the camp.

examples.es: El gobierno aprobó una nueva ley para facilitar la integración de cualquier refugiado que llegue al país.
examples.en: The government passed a new law to facilitate the integration of any refugee who arrives in the country.

cards.example_sentence: The refugee found a new home in the city.
cards.example_es: El refugiado encontró un nuevo hogar en la ciudad.
cards.example_en: The refugee found a new home in the city.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a refugee clutching a sign that reads refugio, the Spanish word that sounds just like refugee.

cards.definition_en: The process of combining separate parts into a unified whole.

examples.es: La integración de los nuevos empleados en la empresa fue muy rápida.
examples.en: The integration of the new employees into the company was very quick.

examples.es: El gobierno busca fomentar la integración de los inmigrantes en la comunidad local.
examples.en: The government seeks to foster the integration of immigrants into the local community.

examples.es: La integración de las nuevas tecnologías en el aula mejora el aprendizaje de los estudiantes.
examples.en: The integration of new technologies into the classroom improves student learning.

cards.example_sentence: The integration of the new employees into the company was very quick.
cards.example_es: La integración de los nuevos empleados en la empresa fue muy rápida.
cards.example_en: The integration of the new employees into the company was very quick.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a giant gear labeled integral joining smoothly, symbolizing the Spanish integración.

cards.definition_en: Unfair or prejudicial treatment of individuals or groups based on characteristics such as race, gender, age, or other attributes.

examples.es: La discriminación en el lugar de trabajo es inaceptable.
examples.en: Discrimination in the workplace is unacceptable.

examples.es: El gobierno aprobó nuevas leyes para combatir la discriminación racial en las escuelas.
examples.en: The government passed new laws to combat racial discrimination in schools.

examples.es: Muchos activistas luchan diariamente contra la discriminación de género en el acceso a puestos directivos.
examples.en: Many activists fight daily against gender discrimination in access to management positions.

cards.example_sentence: Discrimination in the workplace is unacceptable.
cards.example_es: La discriminación en el lugar de trabajo es inaceptable.
cards.example_en: Discrimination in the workplace is unacceptable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a disc ringing a nation, sorting people into groups.

cards.definition_en: The act or state of including someone or something, or the condition of being included.

examples.es: La inclusión de estudiantes con necesidades especiales en el aula beneficia a todos los alumnos.
examples.en: The inclusion of students with special needs in the classroom benefits all students.

examples.es: La empresa implementó nuevas políticas para promover la inclusión de personas de diversos orígenes en el equipo de trabajo.
examples.en: The company implemented new policies to promote the inclusion of people from diverse backgrounds in the work team.

examples.es: El ayuntamiento aprobó un plan de urbanismo centrado en la inclusión de rampas y ascensores en todos los edificios públicos.
examples.en: The city council approved an urban planning project focused on the inclusion of ramps and elevators in all public buildings.

cards.example_sentence: The inclusion of students with special needs in the classroom benefits all students.
cards.example_es: La inclusión de estudiantes con necesidades especiales en el aula beneficia a todos los alumnos.
cards.example_en: The inclusion of students with special needs in the classroom benefits all students.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a club door open for all, the sign reads INCLUE SION, showing inclusion.

cards.definition_en: The quality of being easy to reach, use, understand, or obtain, especially for people with disabilities.

examples.es: La accesibilidad del sitio web es fundamental para los usuarios con discapacidades.
examples.en: The accessibility of the website is essential for users with disabilities.

examples.es: El ayuntamiento aprobó nuevas normas para mejorar la accesibilidad en el transporte público de la ciudad.
examples.en: The city council passed new regulations to improve accessibility in public transportation.

examples.es: El diseño de este edificio histórico limita la accesibilidad para las personas que utilizan sillas de ruedas.
examples.en: The design of this historic building limits accessibility for people who use wheelchairs.

cards.example_sentence: The accessibility of the website is essential for users with disabilities.
cards.example_es: La accesibilidad del sitio web es fundamental para los usuarios con discapacidades.
cards.example_en: The accessibility of the website is essential for users with disabilities.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a wide ramp labeled Acceso letting anyone easily access the building, showing its accessibility.

cards.definition_en: The gap between individuals, households, or regions in access to and use of information and communication technologies.

examples.es: La brecha digital afecta a las comunidades rurales.
examples.en: The digital divide affects rural communities.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevos programas educativos para reducir la brecha digital en las escuelas públicas.
examples.en: The government implemented new educational programs to reduce the digital divide in public schools.

examples.es: Muchos adultos mayores se sienten excluidos debido a la creciente brecha digital en los servicios bancarios.
examples.en: Many senior citizens feel excluded due to the growing digital divide in banking services.

cards.example_sentence: The digital divide affects rural communities.
cards.example_es: La brecha digital afecta a las comunidades rurales.
cards.example_en: The digital divide affects rural communities.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a digital gap, a broken bridge where the brecha splits the internet.

cards.definition_en: The protection of personal information from unauthorized access or disclosure.

examples.es: La privacidad de datos es fundamental para proteger la información personal.
examples.en: Data privacy is essential to protect personal information.

examples.es: Las nuevas leyes europeas imponen sanciones estrictas a las empresas que no respetan la privacidad de datos.
examples.en: New European laws impose strict penalties on companies that do not respect data privacy.

examples.es: El usuario decidió cerrar su cuenta en la red social debido a sus constantes preocupaciones sobre la privacidad de datos.
examples.en: The user decided to close their social media account due to their constant concerns about data privacy.

cards.example_sentence: Data privacy is essential to protect personal information.
cards.example_es: La privacidad de datos es fundamental para proteger la información personal.
cards.example_en: Data privacy is essential to protect personal information.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a data file locked with a privacy shield stamped datos.

cards.definition_en: The practice of protecting computers, networks, and data from unauthorized access, attacks, or damage.

examples.es: La ciberseguridad es esencial para proteger los datos de la empresa.
examples.en: Cybersecurity is essential to protect the company's data.

examples.es: El gobierno anunció nuevas medidas de ciberseguridad para evitar ataques a los sistemas eléctricos.
examples.en: The government announced new cybersecurity measures to prevent attacks on power grids.

examples.es: Muchos estudiantes universitarios deciden especializarse en ciberseguridad debido a la alta demanda laboral.
examples.en: Many university students decide to specialize in cybersecurity due to the high job demand.

cards.example_sentence: Cybersecurity is essential to protect the company's data.
cards.example_es: La ciberseguridad es esencial para proteger los datos de la empresa.
cards.example_en: Cybersecurity is essential to protect the company's data.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cyber guard protecting a giant ciber shield over the network

cards.definition_en: The close monitoring of people, places, or activities, especially for security or intelligence purposes.

examples.es: La vigilancia del parque se intensificó después del robo.
examples.en: Surveillance of the park was intensified after the robbery.

examples.es: Las cámaras de seguridad proporcionan vigilancia constante en los pasillos del banco.
examples.en: Security cameras provide constant surveillance in the bank hallways.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó un nuevo sistema de vigilancia para monitorear el tráfico en la ciudad.
examples.en: The government implemented a new surveillance system to monitor traffic in the city.

cards.example_sentence: Surveillance of the park was intensified after the robbery.
cards.example_es: La vigilancia del parque se intensificó después del robo.
cards.example_en: Surveillance of the park was intensified after the robbery.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a siren overseeing a vigil, that’s surveillance.

cards.definition_en: A step-by-step procedure or set of rules for solving a problem or performing a computation.

examples.es: El algoritmo de búsqueda encontró la información rápidamente.
examples.en: The algorithm found the information quickly.

examples.es: La red social utiliza un algoritmo complejo para personalizar los anuncios que vemos.
examples.en: The social network uses a complex algorithm to personalize the ads we see.

examples.es: Los expertos analizan cómo el algoritmo influye en la difusión de noticias falsas en internet.
examples.en: Experts analyze how the algorithm influences the spread of fake news on the internet.

cards.example_sentence: The algorithm found the information quickly.
cards.example_es: El algoritmo de búsqueda encontró la información rápidamente.
cards.example_en: The algorithm found the information quickly.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a robot named Algo sorting a massive rhythm of numbers, an algorithm.

cards.definition_en: Artificial intelligence is the branch of computer science that develops systems capable of performing tasks that normally require human intelligence.

examples.es: La inteligencia artificial está transformando la industria médica.
examples.en: Artificial intelligence is transforming the medical industry.

examples.es: Muchas empresas utilizan la inteligencia artificial para automatizar el servicio al cliente.
examples.en: Many companies use artificial intelligence to automate customer service.

examples.es: El gobierno debate nuevas leyes para regular el desarrollo de la inteligencia artificial.
examples.en: The government is debating new laws to regulate the development of artificial intelligence.

cards.example_sentence: Artificial intelligence is transforming the medical industry.
cards.example_es: La inteligencia artificial está transformando la industria médica.
cards.example_en: Artificial intelligence is transforming the medical industry.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a robot artist feeding a brain, because artificial art fuels inteligencia.

cards.definition_en: The use of technology or machines to perform tasks without human intervention.

examples.es: La automatización de los procesos de fabricación ha aumentado la eficiencia.
examples.en: Automation of manufacturing processes has increased efficiency.

examples.es: Muchos trabajadores temen que la automatización reemplace sus puestos en la fábrica.
examples.en: Many workers fear that automation will replace their jobs at the factory.

examples.es: La empresa implementó la automatización para gestionar mejor el inventario en sus almacenes.
examples.en: The company implemented automation to better manage inventory in its warehouses.

cards.example_sentence: Automation of manufacturing processes has increased efficiency.
cards.example_es: La automatización de los procesos de fabricación ha aumentado la eficiencia.
cards.example_en: Automation of manufacturing processes has increased efficiency.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an auto that drives the factory itself, showing automation.

cards.definition_en: False or misleading information that is generated, spread, or amplified by algorithms.

examples.es: La desinformación algorítmica se propaga rápidamente en las redes sociales.
examples.en: Algorithmic misinformation spreads quickly on social media.

examples.es: Los expertos advierten que la desinformación algorítmica puede manipular la opinión pública durante las elecciones.
examples.en: Experts warn that algorithmic misinformation can manipulate public opinion during elections.

examples.es: Las plataformas digitales deben implementar mejores filtros para combatir la desinformación algorítmica en sus feeds de noticias.
examples.en: Digital platforms must implement better filters to combat algorithmic misinformation in their news feeds.

cards.example_sentence: Algorithmic misinformation spreads quickly on social media.
cards.example_es: La desinformación algorítmica se propaga rápidamente en las redes sociales.
cards.example_en: Algorithmic misinformation spreads quickly on social media.
cards.mnemonic_en: A sneaky algorithm robot spreads fake news like a gossiping machine

cards.definition_en: A long-term alteration in Earth's average weather patterns, primarily caused by human activities that increase greenhouse gases.

examples.es: El cambio climático está afectando a los glaciares.
examples.en: Climate change is affecting the glaciers.

examples.es: Muchos países han firmado acuerdos internacionales para frenar el cambio climático.
examples.en: Many countries have signed international agreements to curb climate change.

examples.es: Los científicos advierten que el aumento de las temperaturas es una consecuencia directa del cambio climático.
examples.en: Scientists warn that rising temperatures are a direct consequence of climate change.

cards.example_sentence: Climate change is affecting the glaciers.
cards.example_es: El cambio climático está afectando a los glaciares.
cards.example_en: Climate change is affecting the glaciers.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture the climate (clima) turning a huge cambio lever.

cards.definition_en: A long-term rise in Earth's average atmospheric temperature caused primarily by human activities that increase greenhouse gas concentrations.
collocations: global warming is a serious issue

examples.es: El calentamiento global está provocando fenómenos climáticos extremos.
examples.en: Global warming is causing extreme weather events.

examples.es: Los científicos advierten que el calentamiento global acelera el derretimiento de los glaciares.
examples.en: Scientists warn that global warming is accelerating the melting of glaciers.

examples.es: Muchos países han firmado tratados para reducir las emisiones que causan el calentamiento global.
examples.en: Many countries have signed treaties to reduce the emissions that cause global warming.

cards.example_sentence: Global warming is causing extreme weather events.
cards.example_es: El calentamiento global está provocando fenómenos climáticos extremos.
cards.example_en: Global warming is causing extreme weather events.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine the globe heating up like a global sweater, a calentamiento for the whole planet.

cards.definition_en: Substances, especially gases or particles, released into the atmosphere, typically as a result of industrial processes or combustion.

examples.es: Las emisiones de CO₂ de la fábrica han aumentado este año.
examples.en: The CO₂ emissions from the factory have increased this year.

examples.es: El gobierno ha implementado nuevas leyes para reducir las emisiones de los vehículos.
examples.en: The government has implemented new laws to reduce vehicle emissions.

examples.es: La cumbre climática se centró en cómo limitar las emisiones globales de gases de efecto invernadero.
examples.en: The climate summit focused on how to limit global greenhouse gas emissions.

cards.example_sentence: The CO₂ emissions from the factory have increased this year.
cards.example_es: Las emisiones de CO₂ de la fábrica han aumentado este año.
cards.example_en: The CO₂ emissions from the factory have increased this year.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a factory puffing out clouds, its emissions echo the Spanish word emisiones.

cards.definition_en: The total amount of greenhouse gases emitted directly or indirectly by a person, organization, event, or product.

examples.es: Reducir nuestra huella de carbono es esencial para combatir el cambio climático.
examples.en: Reducing our carbon footprint is essential to combat climate change.

examples.es: La empresa publicó un informe detallado sobre cómo planean minimizar su huella de carbono durante el próximo año.
examples.en: The company published a detailed report on how they plan to minimize their carbon footprint over the next year.

examples.es: Muchos consumidores eligen productos locales para disminuir su huella de carbono al evitar el transporte de larga distancia.
examples.en: Many consumers choose local products to lower their carbon footprint by avoiding long-distance transportation.

cards.example_sentence: Reducing our carbon footprint is essential to combat climate change.
cards.example_es: Reducir nuestra huella de carbono es esencial para combatir el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: Reducing our carbon footprint is essential to combat climate change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a coal shoe leaving a huella on the ground.

cards.definition_en: Energy obtained from naturally replenished sources such as sunlight, wind, rain, tides, waves, and geothermal heat.

examples.es: La energía renovable reduce la dependencia de los combustibles fósiles.
examples.en: Renewable energy reduces dependence on fossil fuels.

examples.es: El gobierno ha prometido invertir millones en proyectos de energía renovable para combatir el cambio climático.
examples.en: The government has promised to invest millions in renewable energy projects to combat climate change.

examples.es: Muchos países están instalando parques eólicos y solares para aumentar su capacidad de energía renovable.
examples.en: Many countries are installing wind and solar farms to increase their renewable energy capacity.

cards.example_sentence: Renewable energy reduces dependence on fossil fuels.
cards.example_es: La energía renovable reduce la dependencia de los combustibles fósiles.
cards.example_en: Renewable energy reduces dependence on fossil fuels.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a wind turbine being renewed, spilling fresh energía like endless renewable energy

cards.definition_en: Natural resources such as coal, oil, and natural gas formed from ancient organic matter and used as energy sources.

examples.es: Los combustibles fósiles son la principal fuente de energía en el mundo.
examples.en: Fossil fuels are the main source of energy in the world.

examples.es: Muchos países están intentando reducir su dependencia de los combustibles fósiles para combatir el cambio climático.
examples.en: Many countries are trying to reduce their dependence on fossil fuels to combat climate change.

examples.es: La industria automotriz está abandonando gradualmente los combustibles fósiles en favor de los vehículos eléctricos.
examples.en: The automotive industry is gradually moving away from fossil fuels in favor of electric vehicles.

cards.example_sentence: Fossil fuels are the main source of energy in the world.
cards.example_es: Los combustibles fósiles son la principal fuente de energía en el mundo.
cards.example_en: Fossil fuels are the main source of energy in the world.
cards.mnemonic_en: The dinosaur fossil says fósil as it fuels the car with fossil fuels.

cards.definition_en: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall that results in a shortage of water.

examples.es: La sequía ha reducido la cosecha de maíz este año.
examples.en: The drought has reduced the corn harvest this year.

examples.es: El gobierno declaró el estado de emergencia debido a la sequía prolongada en la región.
examples.en: The government declared a state of emergency due to the prolonged drought in the region.

examples.es: Los agricultores locales están sufriendo pérdidas económicas graves por culpa de la sequía.
examples.en: Local farmers are suffering severe economic losses because of the drought.

cards.example_sentence: The drought has reduced the corn harvest this year.
cards.example_es: La sequía ha reducido la cosecha de maíz este año.
cards.example_en: The drought has reduced the corn harvest this year.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cracked earth where the rain has fled, a drought like a Spanish sequía.

cards.definition_en: A large amount of water that covers land which is normally dry.

examples.es: La inundación destruyó varias casas en el pueblo.
examples.en: The flood destroyed several houses in the town.

examples.es: El gobierno envió ayuda humanitaria tras la gran inundación.
examples.en: The government sent humanitarian aid after the major flood.

examples.es: Debido a la inundación, las autoridades cerraron todas las carreteras principales.
examples.en: Due to the flood, the authorities closed all the main roads.

cards.example_sentence: The flood destroyed several houses in the town.
cards.example_es: La inundación destruyó varias casas en el pueblo.
cards.example_en: The flood destroyed several houses in the town.
cards.mnemonic_en: A flood is a huge water flod that inundates everything.

cards.definition_en: A prolonged period of excessively hot weather.

examples.es: Esta semana hubo una ola de calor que alcanzó los 40 grados.
examples.en: This week there was a heatwave that reached 40 degrees.

examples.es: Las autoridades emitieron una alerta roja debido a la intensa ola de calor en la ciudad.
examples.en: Authorities issued a red alert due to the intense heatwave in the city.

examples.es: El consumo de electricidad aumentó considerablemente durante la reciente ola de calor.
examples.en: Electricity consumption increased significantly during the recent heatwave.

cards.example_sentence: This week there was a heatwave that reached 40 degrees.
cards.example_es: Esta semana hubo una ola de calor que alcanzó los 40 grados.
cards.example_en: This week there was a heatwave that reached 40 degrees.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a scorching wave, an 'ola' of heat rolling over the city.

cards.definition_en: The protection, preservation, or careful management of natural resources and the environment.

examples.es: La conservación de los bosques es esencial para combatir el cambio climático.
examples.en: The conservation of forests is essential to combat climate change.

examples.es: El museo recibió una subvención para la conservación de sus pinturas antiguas.
examples.en: The museum received a grant for the conservation of its ancient paintings.

examples.es: El gobierno aprobó nuevas leyes para la conservación de las especies en peligro de extinción.
examples.en: The government passed new laws for the conservation of endangered species.

cards.example_sentence: The conservation of forests is essential to combat climate change.
cards.example_es: La conservación de los bosques es esencial para combatir el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The conservation of forests is essential to combat climate change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a guard preserving a jar of conserva to protect the forest

cards.definition_en: The variety of living organisms and their ecological interactions within a given area or across the planet.

examples.es: La biodiversidad es esencial para el equilibrio del ecosistema.
examples.en: Biodiversity is essential for the balance of the ecosystem.

examples.es: La deforestación en la selva amazónica amenaza gravemente la biodiversidad local.
examples.en: Deforestation in the Amazon rainforest severely threatens local biodiversity.

examples.es: El gobierno aprobó nuevas leyes para proteger la biodiversidad en los parques nacionales.
examples.en: The government passed new laws to protect biodiversity in national parks.

cards.example_sentence: Biodiversity is essential for the balance of the ecosystem.
cards.example_es: La biodiversidad es esencial para el equilibrio del ecosistema.
cards.example_en: Biodiversity is essential for the balance of the ecosystem.
cards.mnemonic_en: A bio diver explores a sea of many species, reminding you of biodiversidad.

cards.definition_en: The removal or clearing of trees and forested land, typically for human use such as agriculture or development.

examples.es: La deforestación está provocando la pérdida de hábitats en la Amazonía.
examples.en: Deforestation is causing habitat loss in the Amazon.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas leyes para frenar la deforestación en las zonas protegidas.
examples.en: The government implemented new laws to curb deforestation in protected areas.

examples.es: Muchos científicos advierten que la deforestación acelera el cambio climático global.
examples.en: Many scientists warn that deforestation accelerates global climate change.

cards.example_sentence: Deforestation is causing habitat loss in the Amazon.
cards.example_es: La deforestación está provocando la pérdida de hábitats en la Amazonía.
cards.example_en: Deforestation is causing habitat loss in the Amazon.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a forest disappearing and you chant de-forest-ación as the trees vanish

cards.definition_en: Pollution is the introduction of harmful substances or energy into the environment.

examples.es: La contaminación del aire está empeorando la salud de la población.
examples.en: Air pollution is worsening the health of the population.

examples.es: El vertido de petróleo en el océano ha causado una grave contaminación en la costa.
examples.en: The oil spill in the ocean has caused severe pollution along the coast.

examples.es: Las nuevas regulaciones industriales buscan reducir la contaminación en los ríos cercanos.
examples.en: New industrial regulations aim to reduce pollution in the nearby rivers.

cards.example_sentence: Air pollution is worsening the health of the population.
cards.example_es: La contaminación del aire está empeorando la salud de la población.
cards.example_en: Air pollution is worsening the health of the population.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a poll of dirty water that contaminates the town, reminding you of pollution.

cards.definition_en: Waste is material, resources, or energy that is discarded, unused, or considered useless.

examples.es: El desperdicio de alimentos es un problema grave.
examples.en: Food waste is a serious problem.

examples.es: Las empresas deben reducir el desperdicio de plástico en sus procesos de fabricación.
examples.en: Companies must reduce plastic waste in their manufacturing processes.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas políticas para controlar el desperdicio de agua durante la sequía.
examples.en: The government implemented new policies to control water waste during the drought.

cards.example_sentence: Food waste is a serious problem.
cards.example_es: El desperdicio de alimentos es un problema grave.
cards.example_en: Food waste is a serious problem.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a waste dump with a big sign that reads Desperdicio.

cards.definition_en: The process of collecting and converting waste materials into new, usable products.

examples.es: El reciclaje ayuda a proteger el medio ambiente.
examples.en: Recycling helps protect the environment.

examples.es: Debemos separar el plástico y el papel para facilitar el reciclaje en nuestra comunidad.
examples.en: We must separate plastic and paper to facilitate recycling in our community.

examples.es: La nueva ley municipal obliga a todos los vecinos a participar en el programa de reciclaje.
examples.en: The new municipal law requires all residents to participate in the recycling program.

cards.example_sentence: Recycling helps protect the environment.
cards.example_es: El reciclaje ayuda a proteger el medio ambiente.
cards.example_en: Recycling helps protect the environment.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a recycling robot chanting reciclaje as it turns trash into treasure.

cards.definition_en: An economic system that aims to minimize waste and maximize resource use by reusing, repairing, refurbishing, and recycling materials and products.

examples.es: La empresa ha adoptado la economía circular para reducir sus residuos.
examples.en: The company has adopted the circular economy to reduce its waste.

examples.es: El gobierno está incentivando a las fábricas locales a implementar un modelo de economía circular para reciclar materiales.
examples.en: The government is incentivizing local factories to implement a circular economy model to recycle materials.

examples.es: Muchos expertos sostienen que la economía circular es fundamental para combatir el cambio climático a largo plazo.
examples.en: Many experts argue that the circular economy is essential for combating climate change in the long term.

cards.example_sentence: The company has adopted the circular economy to reduce its waste.
cards.example_es: La empresa ha adoptado la economía circular para reducir sus residuos.
cards.example_en: The company has adopted the circular economy to reduce its waste.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a round wheel of money turning, because circular economy sounds like a circular wheel of cash

cards.definition_en: Food security is the condition in which all people have reliable access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food.

examples.es: La seguridad alimentaria es esencial para el desarrollo sostenible.
examples.en: Food security is essential for sustainable development.

examples.es: La sequía prolongada ha puesto en grave riesgo la seguridad alimentaria de toda la región.
examples.en: The prolonged drought has put the food security of the entire region at serious risk.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas políticas agrícolas para fortalecer la seguridad alimentaria del país.
examples.en: The government implemented new agricultural policies to strengthen the country's food security.

cards.example_sentence: Food security is essential for sustainable development.
cards.example_es: La seguridad alimentaria es esencial para el desarrollo sostenible.
cards.example_en: Food security is essential for sustainable development.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a locked fridge: security seal keeps the food safe, like seguridad alimentaria.

cards.definition_en: A condition in which there is insufficient available water to meet the needs of a region or population.

examples.es: La escasez de agua está afectando a millones de personas en todo el mundo.
examples.en: Water scarcity is affecting millions of people worldwide.

examples.es: Los agricultores de la región temen que la escasez de agua arruine sus cosechas este verano.
examples.en: Farmers in the region fear that water scarcity will ruin their crops this summer.

examples.es: El gobierno ha implementado nuevas restricciones debido a la creciente escasez de agua en las ciudades.
examples.en: The government has implemented new restrictions due to the growing water scarcity in cities.

cards.example_sentence: Water scarcity is affecting millions of people worldwide.
cards.example_es: La escasez de agua está afectando a millones de personas en todo el mundo.
cards.example_en: Water scarcity is affecting millions of people worldwide.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a river that escapes and leaves a cracked water scarcity desert.

cards.definition_en: A network of organizations, activities, and resources that moves a product or service from supplier to customer.

examples.es: La empresa está optimizando su cadena de suministro para reducir costos.
examples.en: The company is optimizing its supply chain to reduce costs.

examples.es: La escasez de microchips ha provocado graves interrupciones en la cadena de suministro global.
examples.en: The microchip shortage has caused severe disruptions in the global supply chain.

examples.es: El gobierno busca diversificar la cadena de suministro de medicamentos esenciales para evitar futuras crisis.
examples.en: The government is seeking to diversify the essential medicine supply chain to avoid future crises.

cards.example_sentence: The company is optimizing its supply chain to reduce costs.
cards.example_es: La empresa está optimizando su cadena de suministro para reducir costos.
cards.example_en: The company is optimizing its supply chain to reduce costs.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chain of crates delivering supplies, linking the Spanish 'cadena' to the English supply chain.

cards.definition_en: A disturbance or interruption that prevents something from continuing smoothly.

examples.es: La interrupción del suministro eléctrico dejó a la ciudad sin luz.
examples.en: The disruption of the power supply left the city without electricity.

examples.es: La huelga de transporte causó una gran interrupción en los servicios de autobús.
examples.en: The transport strike caused a major disruption to bus services.

examples.es: La nueva tecnología digital provocó una interrupción en el modelo de negocio tradicional.
examples.en: The new digital technology caused a disruption in the traditional business model.

cards.example_sentence: The disruption of the power supply left the city without electricity.
cards.example_es: La interrupción del suministro eléctrico dejó a la ciudad sin luz.
cards.example_en: The disruption of the power supply left the city without electricity.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a loud crash disrupting a quiet room, like an Interrupción breaking the silence.

cards.definition_en: A rapid increase or intensification, especially of a conflict, problem, or situation.

examples.es: La escalada del conflicto sorprendió a todos.
examples.en: The escalation of the conflict surprised everyone.

examples.es: El gobierno teme una escalada en los precios de la energía este invierno.
examples.en: The government fears an escalation in energy prices this winter.

examples.es: La escalada de la violencia en la frontera obligó a cerrar las rutas comerciales.
examples.en: The escalation of violence at the border forced the closure of trade routes.

cards.example_sentence: The escalation of the conflict surprised everyone.
cards.example_es: La escalada del conflicto sorprendió a todos.
cards.example_en: The escalation of the conflict surprised everyone.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a climber on an escalada rope that suddenly becomes a rising chart, showing escalation.

cards.definition_en: A temporary suspension of fighting between opposing sides.

examples.es: Las partes en conflicto han acordado un alto el fuego para permitir la entrada de ayuda humanitaria.
examples.en: The warring parties have agreed to a ceasefire to allow humanitarian aid to enter.

examples.es: El consejo de seguridad de la ONU exige un alto el fuego inmediato en la zona de guerra.
examples.en: The UN Security Council is demanding an immediate ceasefire in the war zone.

examples.es: Tras semanas de intensos bombardeos, finalmente se ha declarado un alto el fuego en la región.
examples.en: After weeks of heavy shelling, a ceasefire has finally been declared in the region.

cards.example_sentence: The warring parties have agreed to a ceasefire to allow humanitarian aid to enter.
cards.example_es: Las partes en conflicto han acordado un alto el fuego para permitir la entrada de ayuda humanitaria.
cards.example_en: The warring parties have agreed to a ceasefire to allow humanitarian aid to enter.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a ceasefire where commanders yell 'Alto el fuego!' to halt the fire.

cards.definition_en: A penalty imposed for violating a law, rule, or regulation.

examples.es: El comité decidió imponer una sanción de diez mil euros al club por incumplir las normas.
examples.en: The committee decided to impose a sanction of ten thousand euros on the club for violating the rules.

examples.es: El gobierno anunció una nueva sanción económica contra el país vecino debido a sus políticas nucleares.
examples.en: The government announced a new economic sanction against the neighboring country due to its nuclear policies.

examples.es: El jugador recibió una sanción de tres partidos tras agredir a un rival durante el encuentro.
examples.en: The player received a three-match sanction after assaulting an opponent during the game.

cards.example_sentence: The committee decided to impose a sanction of ten thousand euros on the club for violating the rules.
cards.example_es: El comité decidió imponer una sanción de diez mil euros al club por incumplir las normas.
cards.example_en: The committee decided to impose a sanction of ten thousand euros on the club for violating the rules.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a judge stamping a document with a red sanction stamp that reads Sanción

cards.definition_en: Assistance given to people in need, especially during crises or disasters, to alleviate suffering and protect life and health.

examples.es: La ayuda humanitaria llegó a la zona afectada por el terremoto.
examples.en: Humanitarian aid arrived in the area affected by the earthquake.

examples.es: Varias organizaciones internacionales enviaron ayuda humanitaria para los refugiados que huyen del conflicto.
examples.en: Several international organizations sent humanitarian aid for the refugees fleeing the conflict.

examples.es: El gobierno autorizó el paso de camiones con ayuda humanitaria hacia las regiones más pobres del país.
examples.en: The government authorized the passage of trucks with humanitarian aid to the country's poorest regions.

cards.example_sentence: Humanitarian aid arrived in the area affected by the earthquake.
cards.example_es: La ayuda humanitaria llegó a la zona afectada por el terremoto.
cards.example_en: Humanitarian aid arrived in the area affected by the earthquake.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a human handing out aid while yelling Ayuda to the crowd.

cards.definition_en: The involuntary movement of people from their homes or habitual residence, usually caused by conflict, persecution, or natural disasters.

examples.es: El desplazamiento forzado de miles de familias ha creado una crisis humanitaria.
examples.en: The forced displacement of thousands of families has created a humanitarian crisis.

examples.es: Las organizaciones internacionales denuncian el desplazamiento forzado provocado por el conflicto armado en la región.
examples.en: International organizations denounce the forced displacement caused by the armed conflict in the region.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevas políticas para asistir a las víctimas del desplazamiento forzado en las zonas rurales.
examples.en: The government implemented new policies to assist victims of forced displacement in rural areas.

cards.example_sentence: The forced displacement of thousands of families has created a humanitarian crisis.
cards.example_es: El desplazamiento forzado de miles de familias ha creado una crisis humanitaria.
cards.example_en: The forced displacement of thousands of families has created a humanitarian crisis.
cards.mnemonic_en: A force shoves the word des off a map, creating forced displacement.

cards.definition_en: Fundamental rights and freedoms that belong to every person simply because they are human.

examples.es: Los derechos humanos deben ser respetados en todo el mundo.
examples.en: Human rights must be respected worldwide.

examples.es: La organización presentó un informe detallado sobre las violaciones a los derechos humanos en la región.
examples.en: The organization presented a detailed report on the violations of human rights in the region.

examples.es: El activista dedicó toda su vida a la defensa de los derechos humanos frente a la opresión gubernamental.
examples.en: The activist dedicated his entire life to the defense of human rights against government oppression.

cards.example_sentence: Human rights must be respected worldwide.
cards.example_es: Los derechos humanos deben ser respetados en todo el mundo.
cards.example_en: Human rights must be respected worldwide.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a human holding a shield labeled derechos to guard his rights.

cards.definition_en: The right to express one's opinions publicly without censorship or restraint.

examples.es: La libertad de expresión es un derecho fundamental en una democracia.
examples.en: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right in a democracy.

examples.es: El periodista defendió la libertad de expresión durante su entrevista en televisión.
examples.en: The journalist defended freedom of speech during his television interview.

examples.es: Las nuevas leyes del país restringen la libertad de expresión en las redes sociales.
examples.en: The country's new laws restrict freedom of speech on social media.

cards.example_sentence: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right in a democracy.
cards.example_es: La libertad de expresión es un derecho fundamental en una democracia.
cards.example_en: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right in a democracy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a free dome where a liberty flag waves and everyone shouts Libertad

cards.definition_en: The principle that all individuals and institutions, including the government, are bound by and accountable to publicly known laws.

examples.es: El fortalecimiento del Estado de derecho es fundamental para garantizar la estabilidad democrática del país.
examples.en: Strengthening the rule of law is essential to guarantee the country's democratic stability.

examples.es: La comunidad internacional exige que el gobierno respete el Estado de derecho para evitar abusos de poder.
examples.en: The international community demands that the government respect the rule of law to prevent abuses of power.

examples.es: Sin un Estado de derecho sólido, los inversores extranjeros temen que sus contratos no sean protegidos por los tribunales.
examples.en: Without a solid rule of law, foreign investors fear that their contracts will not be protected by the courts.

cards.example_sentence: Strengthening the rule of law is essential to guarantee the country's democratic stability.
cards.example_es: El fortalecimiento del Estado de derecho es fundamental para garantizar la estabilidad democrática del país.
cards.example_en: Strengthening the rule of law is essential to guarantee the country's democratic stability.
cards.mnemonic_en: A ruler sits on a law book inside a grand Estado, showing the rule of law.

cards.definition_en: A change made to improve a system, institution, or practice.

examples.es: La reforma del sistema educativo será aprobada el próximo mes.
examples.en: The reform of the education system will be approved next month.

examples.es: El gobierno anunció una reforma profunda en las leyes laborales para reducir el desempleo.
examples.en: The government announced a major reform of labor laws to reduce unemployment.

examples.es: Los ciudadanos exigen una reforma urgente del sistema de salud pública.
examples.en: Citizens are demanding an urgent reform of the public health system.

cards.example_sentence: The reform of the education system will be approved next month.
cards.example_es: La reforma del sistema educativo será aprobada el próximo mes.
cards.example_en: The reform of the education system will be approved next month.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a broken wall being re‑form‑ed during the Reforma, like rebuilding after a quake.

cards.definition_en: An expression of objection or disapproval, often manifested in a public demonstration.

examples.es: Los estudiantes organizaron una protesta contra la política escolar.
examples.en: The students organized a protest against the school policy.

examples.es: La policía bloqueó la calle principal durante la protesta de los trabajadores.
examples.en: The police blocked the main street during the protest by the workers.

examples.es: Miles de ciudadanos se unieron a la protesta frente al edificio del gobierno.
examples.en: Thousands of citizens joined the protest in front of the government building.

cards.example_sentence: The students organized a protest against the school policy.
cards.example_es: Los estudiantes organizaron una protesta contra la política escolar.
cards.example_en: The students organized a protest against the school policy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a crowd holding signs shouting Pro test! as the Spanish protesta erupts.

cards.definition_en: A public protest or a presentation that shows how something works.

examples.es: La manifestación contra la reforma laboral atrajo a miles de personas.
examples.en: The demonstration against the labor reform attracted thousands of people.

examples.es: Los estudiantes organizaron una manifestación pacífica frente al ayuntamiento para pedir más becas.
examples.en: The students organized a peaceful demonstration in front of city hall to demand more scholarships.

examples.es: La policía tuvo que cortar el tráfico en el centro debido a una manifestación masiva por el cambio climático.
