examples.en: The police had to block traffic in the city center due to a massive demonstration for climate change.

cards.example_sentence: The demonstration against the labor reform attracted thousands of people.
cards.example_es: La manifestación contra la reforma laboral atrajo a miles de personas.
cards.example_en: The demonstration against the labor reform attracted thousands of people.
cards.mnemonic_en: A MANI shows a DEMO, so it becomes a DEMONSTRATION.

cards.definition_en: Activism is the practice of organized efforts to promote or oppose social, political, or environmental change.

examples.es: El activismo juvenil ha impulsado cambios significativos en la política ambiental.
examples.en: Youth activism has driven significant changes in environmental policy.

examples.es: Muchos ciudadanos participan en el activismo local para mejorar la seguridad de sus vecindarios.
examples.en: Many citizens participate in local activism to improve the safety of their neighborhoods.

examples.es: La organización utiliza el activismo digital para recaudar fondos destinados a la protección de especies en peligro.
examples.en: The organization uses digital activism to raise funds for the protection of endangered species.

cards.example_sentence: Youth activism has driven significant changes in environmental policy.
cards.example_es: El activismo juvenil ha impulsado cambios significativos en la política ambiental.
cards.example_en: Youth activism has driven significant changes in environmental policy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an activist shouting activo while marching, showing activism is the active movement.

cards.definition_en: The act of assembling and preparing troops or resources for active service.

examples.es: La movilización de los recursos fue clave para el éxito del proyecto.
examples.en: The mobilization of resources was key to the project's success.

examples.es: El sindicato convocó una movilización masiva para protestar contra los recortes salariales.
examples.en: The union called for a massive mobilization to protest against the salary cuts.

examples.es: El gobierno ordenó la movilización de las tropas hacia la frontera tras el conflicto diplomático.
examples.en: The government ordered the mobilization of troops to the border following the diplomatic conflict.

cards.example_sentence: The mobilization of resources was key to the project's success.
cards.example_es: La movilización de los recursos fue clave para el éxito del proyecto.
cards.example_en: The mobilization of resources was key to the project's success.
cards.mnemonic_en: A mobile van leads the parade, showing the power of mobilization.

cards.definition_en: the state of being informed or conscious about a particular issue among the general public

examples.es: La campaña aumentó la conciencia pública sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: The campaign raised public awareness about climate change.

examples.es: El gobierno lanzó un anuncio para fomentar la conciencia pública sobre la importancia de reciclar.
examples.en: The government launched an advertisement to foster public awareness about the importance of recycling.

examples.es: Los activistas organizaron una marcha para generar conciencia pública acerca de la desigualdad social.
examples.en: The activists organized a march to generate public awareness regarding social inequality.

cards.example_sentence: The campaign raised public awareness about climate change.
cards.example_es: La campaña aumentó la conciencia pública sobre el cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The campaign raised public awareness about climate change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a public plaza with a giant 'aware-ness' billboard that reads 'conciencia' for everyone to see.

cards.definition_en: A spoken or written account of connected events; a story.

examples.es: La narrativa de la novela captura la esencia de la época.
examples.en: The narrative of the novel captures the essence of the era.

examples.es: El candidato cambió su narrativa para atraer a más votantes durante el debate.
examples.en: The candidate changed his narrative to appeal to more voters during the debate.

examples.es: Los medios de comunicación suelen construir una narrativa específica sobre los conflictos internacionales.
examples.en: The media often constructs a specific narrative about international conflicts.

cards.example_sentence: The narrative of the novel captures the essence of the era.
cards.example_es: La narrativa de la novela captura la esencia de la época.
cards.example_en: The narrative of the novel captures the essence of the era.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a narrator writing a story, his narrativa becomes the narrative.

cards.definition_en: A tendency of journalists or news outlets to present information in a way that favors a particular perspective or agenda.

examples.es: El sesgo mediático afecta la forma en que percibimos las noticias.
examples.en: Media bias affects the way we perceive the news.

examples.es: Muchos lectores critican el sesgo mediático en la cobertura de las elecciones presidenciales.
examples.en: Many readers criticize media bias in the coverage of the presidential elections.

examples.es: Es fundamental analizar las fuentes para identificar cualquier posible sesgo mediático en los artículos de opinión.
examples.en: It is essential to analyze the sources to identify any potential media bias in opinion pieces.

cards.example_sentence: Media bias affects the way we perceive the news.
cards.example_es: El sesgo mediático afecta la forma en que percibimos las noticias.
cards.example_en: Media bias affects the way we perceive the news.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a TV news tilt, a media bias – a slanted sesgo on the screen.

cards.definition_en: The process of verifying the accuracy of information or claims.

examples.es: La verificación de hechos es esencial para combatir la desinformación.
examples.en: Fact-checking is essential to combat misinformation.

examples.es: El periódico implementó un riguroso proceso de verificación de hechos antes de publicar el reportaje sobre el escándalo político.
examples.en: The newspaper implemented a rigorous process of fact-checking before publishing the report on the political scandal.

examples.es: Las plataformas digitales han contratado a más periodistas para realizar la verificación de hechos de los contenidos virales.
examples.en: Digital platforms have hired more journalists to perform fact-checking on viral content.

cards.example_sentence: Fact-checking is essential to combat misinformation.
cards.example_es: La verificación de hechos es esencial para combatir la desinformación.
cards.example_en: Fact-checking is essential to combat misinformation.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a detective checking facts and stamping each hecho with a green check.

cards.definition_en: To become widely known or distributed quickly.

examples.es: El rumor se difundió rápidamente por todo el vecindario.
examples.en: The rumor spread rapidly throughout the neighborhood.

examples.es: La noticia sobre la nueva ley se difundió rápidamente en las redes sociales.
examples.en: The news about the new law spread rapidly on social media.

examples.es: El virus se difundió rápidamente entre los trabajadores de la fábrica durante el invierno.
examples.en: The virus spread rapidly among the factory workers during the winter.

cards.example_sentence: The rumor spread rapidly throughout the neighborhood.
cards.example_es: El rumor se difundió rápidamente por todo el vecindario.
cards.example_en: The rumor spread rapidly throughout the neighborhood.
cards.mnemonic_en: A fast‑spreading fire is a 'difund' of flames racing across the forest.

cards.definition_en: to become widely circulated on the internet, especially through rapid sharing.

examples.es: El video de la protesta pacífica se volvió viral en cuestión de horas.
examples.en: The video of the peaceful protest started to go viral within hours.

examples.es: La noticia sobre el nuevo impuesto empezó a volverse viral en todas las redes sociales.
examples.en: The news about the new tax started to go viral across all social media platforms.

examples.es: Es increíble lo rápido que puede volverse viral una campaña de concienciación sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: It is incredible how quickly a climate change awareness campaign can go viral.

cards.example_sentence: The video of the peaceful protest started to go viral within hours.
cards.example_es: El video de la protesta pacífica se volvió viral en cuestión de horas.
cards.example_en: The video of the peaceful protest started to go viral within hours.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a goat (go) turning into a virus, spreading wildly like a viral video.

cards.definition_en: Unintended or indirect consequences of an action or event, often negative.

examples.es: Las repercusiones de la decisión se sentirán durante años.
examples.en: The repercussions of the decision will be felt for years.

examples.es: El escándalo político tuvo graves repercusiones en la popularidad del gobierno.
examples.en: The political scandal had serious repercussions on the government's popularity.

examples.es: Debemos analizar las repercusiones económicas de esta nueva ley antes de aprobarla.
examples.en: We must analyze the economic repercussions of this new law before passing it.

cards.example_sentence: The repercussions of the decision will be felt for years.
cards.example_es: Las repercusiones de la decisión se sentirán durante años.
cards.example_en: The repercussions of the decision will be felt for years.
cards.mnemonic_en: Hear re-per-cus-iones echo and picture the fallout spreading like ripples.

cards.definition_en: An outcome that was not intended or expected.

examples.es: La consecuencia imprevista de su política fue un aumento inesperado de la inflación.
examples.en: The unintended consequence of his policy was an unexpected rise in inflation.

examples.es: El uso excesivo de pesticidas tuvo como consecuencia imprevista la desaparición de las abejas locales.
examples.en: The excessive use of pesticides had the unintended consequence of the disappearance of local bees.

examples.es: La nueva ley de tráfico tuvo la consecuencia imprevista de colapsar las rutas alternativas de la ciudad.
examples.en: The new traffic law had the unintended consequence of clogging the city's alternative routes.

cards.example_sentence: The unintended consequence of his policy was an unexpected rise in inflation.
cards.example_es: La consecuencia imprevista de su política fue un aumento inesperado de la inflación.
cards.example_en: The unintended consequence of his policy was an unexpected rise in inflation.
cards.mnemonic_en: An unexpected accident creates a "con-sequence" chain, like a surprise domino effect.

cards.definition_en: The ability to recover quickly from difficulties or to adapt in the face of adversity.
collocations: resilience in the face of adversity

examples.es: La resiliencia de la comunidad le permitió recuperarse rápidamente después del terremoto.
examples.en: The community's resilience allowed it to recover quickly after the earthquake.

examples.es: El atleta demostró una gran resiliencia al volver a competir tras su grave lesión.
examples.en: The athlete showed great resilience by returning to competition after his serious injury.

examples.es: La economía del país ha mostrado una notable resiliencia frente a la crisis financiera global.
examples.en: The country's economy has shown remarkable resilience in the face of the global financial crisis.

cards.example_sentence: The community's resilience allowed it to recover quickly after the earthquake.
cards.example_es: La resiliencia de la comunidad le permitió recuperarse rápidamente después del terremoto.
cards.example_en: The community's resilience allowed it to recover quickly after the earthquake.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a resilient rubber band snapping back, echoing resiliencia in a storm.

cards.definition_en: The ability to adjust to new conditions or changes.

examples.es: Su capacidad de adaptación le permitió superar los cambios en el trabajo.
examples.en: His adaptability allowed him to overcome the changes at work.

examples.es: En un mercado laboral tan cambiante, la capacidad de adaptación es una habilidad fundamental para los jóvenes.
examples.en: In such a shifting job market, adaptability is a fundamental skill for young people.

examples.es: La empresa sobrevivió a la crisis económica gracias a la gran capacidad de adaptación de su equipo directivo.
examples.en: The company survived the economic crisis thanks to the great adaptability of its management team.

cards.example_sentence: His adaptability allowed him to overcome the changes at work.
cards.example_es: Su capacidad de adaptación le permitió superar los cambios en el trabajo.
cards.example_en: His adaptability allowed him to overcome the changes at work.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a flexible cap that adapts to any shape, showing the adaptability of capacidad.

cards.definition_en: the mutual reliance between two or more entities, where each depends on the others.

examples.es: La interdependencia entre los países latinoamericanos se refleja en sus acuerdos comerciales.
examples.en: The interdependence among Latin American countries is reflected in their trade agreements.

examples.es: El cambio climático demuestra la interdependencia de todas las naciones frente a una crisis global.
examples.en: Climate change demonstrates the interdependence of all nations in the face of a global crisis.

examples.es: La economía moderna se basa en la interdependencia de los mercados financieros internacionales.
examples.en: The modern economy is based on the interdependence of international financial markets.

cards.example_sentence: The interdependence among Latin American countries is reflected in their trade agreements.
cards.example_es: La interdependencia entre los países latinoamericanos se refleja en sus acuerdos comerciales.
cards.example_en: The interdependence among Latin American countries is reflected in their trade agreements.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture two friends leaning on each other, their bond is interdependence like inter and depend together.

cards.definition_en: General agreement among scientists about a particular scientific finding or theory.

examples.es: El consenso científico indica que el cambio climático es causado principalmente por la actividad humana.
examples.en: The scientific consensus indicates that climate change is primarily caused by human activity.

examples.es: Existe un consenso científico sobre la eficacia de las vacunas para prevenir enfermedades graves.
examples.en: There is a scientific consensus regarding the effectiveness of vaccines in preventing serious diseases.

examples.es: Los expertos buscan llegar a un consenso científico antes de publicar las nuevas directrices de salud pública.
examples.en: Experts aim to reach a scientific consensus before publishing the new public health guidelines.

cards.example_sentence: The scientific consensus indicates that climate change is primarily caused by human activity.
cards.example_es: El consenso científico indica que el cambio climático es causado principalmente por la actividad humana.
cards.example_en: The scientific consensus indicates that climate change is primarily caused by human activity.
cards.mnemonic_en: Scientists sit (siéntate) together, forming a scientific consensus.

cards.definition_en: A discussion that examines moral principles and values.

examples.es: El debate ético sobre la edición genética es muy controvertido.
examples.en: The ethical debate about genetic editing is very controversial.

examples.es: La inteligencia artificial ha generado un intenso debate ético en las universidades.
examples.en: Artificial intelligence has sparked an intense ethical debate in universities.

examples.es: Los médicos mantienen un debate ético constante sobre el uso de tratamientos experimentales.
examples.en: Doctors maintain a constant ethical debate regarding the use of experimental treatments.

cards.example_sentence: The ethical debate about genetic editing is very controversial.
cards.example_es: El debate ético sobre la edición genética es muy controvertido.
cards.example_en: The ethical debate about genetic editing is very controversial.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize an ethical debate with participants holding big signs that read ético.

cards.definition_en: A difference in attitudes, values, and experiences between people of different generations.

examples.es: La brecha generacional entre los jóvenes y sus padres se hace evidente en el uso de la tecnología.
examples.en: The generation gap between young people and their parents is evident in their use of technology.

examples.es: Los expertos debaten cómo cerrar la brecha generacional en el lugar de trabajo para mejorar la colaboración.
examples.en: Experts are debating how to bridge the generation gap in the workplace to improve collaboration.

examples.es: Las diferencias políticas entre abuelos y nietos suelen ser un claro ejemplo de la brecha generacional.
examples.en: The political differences between grandparents and grandchildren are often a clear example of the generation gap.

cards.example_sentence: The generation gap between young people and their parents is evident in their use of technology.
cards.example_es: La brecha generacional entre los jóvenes y sus padres se hace evidente en el uso de la tecnología.
cards.example_en: The generation gap between young people and their parents is evident in their use of technology.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a huge gap where a generation falls into a brecha, a crack separating the old from the young.

cards.definition_en: The influence exerted by a group or society on an individual's behavior or decisions.

examples.es: La presión social lo llevó a dejar su trabajo.
examples.en: Social pressure led him to quit his job.

examples.es: Muchos adolescentes consumen alcohol debido a la presión social en sus grupos de amigos.
examples.en: Many teenagers consume alcohol due to social pressure within their peer groups.

examples.es: Ella decidió no seguir la moda actual para evitar la presión social de encajar en el grupo.
examples.en: She decided not to follow the latest fashion to avoid the social pressure of fitting in.

cards.example_sentence: Social pressure led him to quit his job.
cards.example_es: La presión social lo llevó a dejar su trabajo.
cards.example_en: Social pressure led him to quit his job.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a social crowd pressing ion batteries, showing social pressure.

cards.definition_en: The bonds, shared values, and sense of belonging that hold members of a society together.

examples.es: La cohesión social es fundamental para el desarrollo de la comunidad.
examples.en: Social cohesion is essential for the community's development.

examples.es: El gobierno implementó nuevos programas para fortalecer la cohesión social en los barrios marginados.
examples.en: The government implemented new programs to strengthen social cohesion in marginalized neighborhoods.

examples.es: La desigualdad económica extrema amenaza la cohesión social de toda la nación.
examples.en: Extreme economic inequality threatens the social cohesion of the entire nation.

cards.example_sentence: Social cohesion is essential for the community's development.
cards.example_es: La cohesión social es fundamental para el desarrollo de la comunidad.
cards.example_en: Social cohesion is essential for the community's development.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a social gathering where people tie a rope called 'cohesión' to stay tightly connected.

cards.definition_en: A feeling of being accepted and valued as part of a group or community.

examples.es: El sentimiento de pertenencia que experimenta en su comunidad lo motiva a participar activamente.
examples.en: The sense of belonging he feels in his community motivates him to participate actively.

examples.es: Las empresas modernas buscan fomentar un sentimiento de pertenencia para retener a sus empleados más talentosos.
examples.en: Modern companies seek to foster a sense of belonging to retain their most talented employees.

examples.es: Muchos inmigrantes luchan por encontrar un sentimiento de pertenencia al adaptarse a una cultura completamente nueva.
examples.en: Many immigrants struggle to find a sense of belonging when adapting to a completely new culture.

cards.example_sentence: The sense of belonging he feels in his community motivates him to participate actively.
cards.example_es: El sentimiento de pertenencia que experimenta en su comunidad lo motiva a participar activamente.
cards.example_en: The sense of belonging he feels in his community motivates him to participate actively.
cards.mnemonic_en: I feel a sense of belonging like a censo of people holding hands.

cards.definition_en: The process of treating a person or group as insignificant or peripheral.

examples.es: La marginalización de las comunidades rurales dificulta su acceso a servicios básicos.
examples.en: The marginalization of rural communities hinders their access to basic services.

examples.es: El informe destaca cómo la falta de educación contribuye a la marginalización de los jóvenes en el mercado laboral.
examples.en: The report highlights how the lack of education contributes to the marginalization of young people in the labor market.

examples.es: Las políticas sociales deben enfocarse en reducir la marginalización de las minorías étnicas en las grandes ciudades.
examples.en: Social policies must focus on reducing the marginalization of ethnic minorities in large cities.

cards.example_sentence: The marginalization of rural communities hinders their access to basic services.
cards.example_es: La marginalización de las comunidades rurales dificulta su acceso a servicios básicos.
cards.example_en: The marginalization of rural communities hinders their access to basic services.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a margin line shoving a crowd to the side, showing marginalization.

cards.definition_en: The process of giving someone authority, confidence, or power to act or make decisions.

examples.es: El empoderamiento de las mujeres es fundamental para el desarrollo sostenible.
examples.en: The empowerment of women is essential for sustainable development.

examples.es: Los programas de capacitación técnica buscan el empoderamiento de los jóvenes en comunidades rurales.
examples.en: Technical training programs aim for the empowerment of young people in rural communities.

examples.es: La educación digital es una herramienta clave para el empoderamiento de los ciudadanos en la era de la información.
examples.en: Digital education is a key tool for the empowerment of citizens in the information age.

cards.example_sentence: The empowerment of women is essential for sustainable development.
cards.example_es: El empoderamiento de las mujeres es fundamental para el desarrollo sostenible.
cards.example_en: The empowerment of women is essential for sustainable development.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a glowing power button marked empower‑ment as you shout the Spanish word empoderamiento.

cards.definition_en: Climate justice is the principle of ensuring fair and equitable treatment of all people in the distribution of climate change impacts and responsibilities.

examples.es: La justicia climática exige que los países ricos reduzcan sus emisiones para proteger a los más vulnerables.
examples.en: Climate justice demands that rich countries cut their emissions to protect the most vulnerable.

examples.es: Los manifestantes marcharon por el centro de la ciudad para reclamar justicia climática ante la inacción de los líderes mundiales.
examples.en: Protesters marched through the city center to demand climate justice in response to the inaction of world leaders.

examples.es: El informe destaca que la justicia climática es fundamental para ayudar a las comunidades insulares a adaptarse al aumento del nivel del mar.
examples.en: The report highlights that climate justice is essential to help island communities adapt to rising sea levels.

cards.example_sentence: Climate justice demands that rich countries cut their emissions to protect the most vulnerable.
cards.example_es: La justicia climática exige que los países ricos reduzcan sus emisiones para proteger a los más vulnerables.
cards.example_en: Climate justice demands that rich countries cut their emissions to protect the most vulnerable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a courtroom in a stormy climate where justice rains down on the earth.

cards.definition_en: A principle of fairness that seeks equitable distribution of resources, rights, and opportunities within a society.

examples.es: El movimiento lucha por la justicia social en la comunidad.
examples.en: The movement fights for social justice in the community.

examples.es: La universidad organizó un seminario sobre la importancia de la justicia social en la educación moderna.
examples.en: The university organized a seminar on the importance of social justice in modern education.

examples.es: Muchos activistas consideran que la igualdad de oportunidades es el pilar fundamental de la justicia social.
examples.en: Many activists consider equal opportunity to be the fundamental pillar of social justice.

cards.example_sentence: The movement fights for social justice in the community.
cards.example_es: El movimiento lucha por la justicia social en la comunidad.
cards.example_en: The movement fights for social justice in the community.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a just sea where all people share social waves.

cards.definition_en: A situation in which two or more parties are jointly accountable for an outcome.

examples.es: La responsabilidad compartida entre los países es fundamental para combatir el cambio climático global.
examples.en: Shared responsibility among nations is fundamental to combat global climate change.

examples.es: El acuerdo de paz enfatiza la responsabilidad compartida de todas las partes involucradas en el conflicto.
examples.en: The peace agreement emphasizes the shared responsibility of all parties involved in the conflict.

examples.es: La seguridad en el lugar de trabajo es una responsabilidad compartida entre los empleados y la gerencia.
examples.en: Workplace safety is a shared responsibility between employees and management.

cards.example_sentence: Shared responsibility among nations is fundamental to combat global climate change.
cards.example_es: La responsabilidad compartida entre los países es fundamental para combatir el cambio climático global.
cards.example_en: Shared responsibility among nations is fundamental to combat global climate change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine two people sharing a heavy bag of duty—shared responsibility

cards.definition_en: A controversial or emotionally charged topic that provokes strong opinions.

examples.es: El cambio climático se ha convertido en un tema candente en la política internacional.
examples.en: Climate change has become a hot-button issue in international politics.

examples.es: La reforma del sistema educativo es un tema candente que divide a los votantes en las elecciones.
examples.en: Education reform is a hot-button issue that divides voters during the election.

examples.es: El uso de la inteligencia artificial en el trabajo sigue siendo un tema candente en los debates sobre el futuro laboral.
examples.en: The use of artificial intelligence in the workplace remains a hot-button issue in debates about the future of work.

cards.example_sentence: Climate change has become a hot-button issue in international politics.
cards.example_es: El cambio climático se ha convertido en un tema candente en la política internacional.
cards.example_en: Climate change has become a hot-button issue in international politics.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a red button on fire, the hot button that sparks every heated debate

cards.definition_en: A significant or extensive effect or influence.

examples.es: La nueva ley tendrá una amplia repercusión en la economía del país.
examples.en: The new law will have a far-reaching impact on the country's economy.

examples.es: El descubrimiento de esta tecnología de inteligencia artificial ha tenido una amplia repercusión en el mercado laboral global.
examples.en: The discovery of this artificial intelligence technology has had a far-reaching impact on the global labor market.

examples.es: El escándalo de corrupción política ha generado una amplia repercusión en la confianza de los ciudadanos hacia el gobierno.
examples.en: The political corruption scandal has generated a far-reaching impact on the citizens' trust in the government.

cards.example_sentence: The new law will have a far-reaching impact on the country's economy.
cards.example_es: La nueva ley tendrá una amplia repercusión en la economía del país.
cards.example_en: The new law will have a far-reaching impact on the country's economy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a far‑reaching arm amplifying a massive ripple, a repercussion that spreads across the world.

title: Advanced Phrasal Verbs
description: A large deck of phrasal verbs and common advanced uses to improve fluency and natural expression.

cards.definition_en: to mention or raise a topic for discussion

examples.es: Durante la reunión, Juan mencionó el problema del presupuesto.
examples.en: During the meeting, Juan brought up the budget issue.

examples.es: Por favor, no menciones el accidente cuando hables con su madre.
examples.en: Please do not bring up the accident when you talk to his mother.

examples.es: Ella mencionó su ascenso inesperado mientras celebrábamos el cumpleaños.
examples.en: She brought up her unexpected promotion while we were celebrating the birthday.

cards.example_sentence: During the meeting, Juan brought up the budget issue.
cards.example_es: Durante la reunión, Juan mencionó el problema del presupuesto.
cards.example_en: During the meeting, Juan brought up the budget issue.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a man (men) being lifted up, so you bring him up to mention the story.

cards.definition_en: to perform or execute a task or plan

examples.es: El equipo de ingenieros debe llevar a cabo pruebas de seguridad antes de abrir el puente.
examples.en: The team of engineers must carry out safety tests before opening the bridge.

examples.es: La empresa decidió llevar a cabo una encuesta para conocer la opinión de sus clientes.
examples.en: The company decided to carry out a survey to learn the opinion of its customers.

examples.es: El científico se preparó para llevar a cabo un experimento complejo en el laboratorio durante la noche.
examples.en: The scientist prepared to carry out a complex experiment in the laboratory throughout the night.

cards.example_sentence: The team of engineers must carry out safety tests before opening the bridge.
cards.example_es: El equipo de ingenieros debe llevar a cabo pruebas de seguridad antes de abrir el puente.
cards.example_en: The team of engineers must carry out safety tests before opening the bridge.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a carrier delivering a cargo to a cape (cabo) – you carry out the mission.

cards.definition_en: to find or meet something or someone by chance

examples.es: Me encontré con un viejo amigo en el mercado.
examples.en: I came across an old friend at the market.

examples.es: Mientras limpiaba el desván, me encontré con una caja llena de fotografías antiguas de mis abuelos.
examples.en: While cleaning the attic, I came across a box full of old photographs of my grandparents.

examples.es: Me encontré con un error técnico en el informe justo antes de enviarlo a mi jefe.
examples.en: I came across a technical error in the report just before sending it to my boss.

cards.example_sentence: I came across an old friend at the market.
cards.example_es: Me encontré con un viejo amigo en el mercado.
cards.example_en: I came across an old friend at the market.
cards.mnemonic_en: I come across (como cruz) a friend and we meet.

cards.definition_en: To think of or produce an idea, plan, or solution.

examples.es: Necesitamos idear una solución antes de que termine el día.
examples.en: We need to come up with a solution before the day ends.

examples.es: El equipo tuvo que idear un plan de emergencia tras el fallo del sistema.
examples.en: The team had to come up with an emergency plan after the system failure.

examples.es: Ella siempre logra idear excusas creativas cuando llega tarde a clase.
examples.en: She always manages to come up with creative excuses when she arrives late to class.

cards.example_sentence: We need to come up with a solution before the day ends.
cards.example_es: Necesitamos idear una solución antes de que termine el día.
cards.example_en: We need to come up with a solution before the day ends.
cards.mnemonic_en: I see a lightbulb rise as I idear and come up with a new plan.

cards.definition_en: to reduce the amount or frequency of something

examples.es: Necesito reducir el consumo de azúcar.
examples.en: I need to cut down on sugar consumption.

examples.es: El médico me recomendó reducir el consumo de grasas saturadas para mejorar mi salud cardiovascular.
examples.en: The doctor recommended that I cut down on saturated fats to improve my cardiovascular health.

examples.es: Si queremos ahorrar dinero este mes, debemos reducir nuestros gastos en salidas a restaurantes.
examples.en: If we want to save money this month, we must cut down on our spending at restaurants.

cards.example_sentence: I need to cut down on sugar consumption.
cards.example_es: Necesito reducir el consumo de azúcar.
cards.example_en: I need to cut down on sugar consumption.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a red cutter shrinking a pile, so you cut down on it.

cards.definition_en: To eliminate or get rid of something.
collocations: do away with the old system

examples.es: El gobierno decidió eliminar los impuestos a la importación.
examples.en: The government decided to do away with import taxes.

examples.es: La empresa planea eliminar el uso de papel en todas sus oficinas.
examples.en: The company plans to do away with the use of paper in all its offices.

examples.es: Es necesario eliminar las reglas obsoletas para mejorar la eficiencia del equipo.
examples.en: It is necessary to do away with obsolete rules to improve team efficiency.

cards.example_sentence: The government decided to do away with import taxes.
cards.example_es: El gobierno decidió eliminar los impuestos a la importación.
cards.example_en: The government decided to do away with import taxes.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a dough (do) being tossed away, eliminating it.

cards.definition_en: To understand or solve something by thinking about it.

examples.es: Necesito resolver este problema antes de la reunión.
examples.en: I need to figure out this problem before the meeting.

examples.es: Todavía no he podido resolver cómo funciona este nuevo software.
examples.en: I still haven't been able to figure out how this new software works.

examples.es: Es difícil resolver por qué el coche no arranca esta mañana.
examples.en: It is difficult to figure out why the car won't start this morning.

cards.example_sentence: I need to figure out this problem before the meeting.
cards.example_es: Necesito resolver este problema antes de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I need to figure out this problem before the meeting.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a figure wielding a resolver wrench to crack the puzzle.

cards.definition_en: To complete something by adding missing information.

examples.es: Por favor, rellena el formulario antes de la fecha límite.
examples.en: Please fill in the form before the deadline.

examples.es: Si no sabes la respuesta, puedes dejar el espacio en blanco o intentar rellenar los huecos con la información correcta.
examples.en: If you do not know the answer, you can leave the space blank or try to fill in the blanks with the correct information.

examples.es: El candidato tuvo que rellenar una solicitud detallada para aplicar al puesto de trabajo.
examples.en: The candidate had to fill in a detailed application to apply for the job position.

cards.example_sentence: Please fill in the form before the deadline.
cards.example_es: Por favor, rellena el formulario antes de la fecha límite.
cards.example_en: Please fill in the form before the deadline.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a reel near a paper and you fill in the blanks on it.

cards.definition_en: To continue an action or task until it is completed.

examples.es: Es importante cumplir hasta el final con los proyectos que iniciamos.
examples.en: It's important to follow through with the projects we start.

examples.es: Aunque prometió ayudarme con la mudanza, no pudo cumplir hasta el final con su palabra.
examples.en: Although he promised to help me move, he failed to follow through on his word.

examples.es: Si decides inscribirte en el curso de maratón, debes cumplir hasta el final con el entrenamiento diario.
examples.en: If you decide to sign up for the marathon course, you must follow through with the daily training.

cards.example_sentence: It's important to follow through with the projects we start.
cards.example_es: Es importante cumplir hasta el final con los proyectos que iniciamos.
cards.example_en: It's important to follow through with the projects we start.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a ‘full’ line you must ‘through’ to the end, like Cumplir hasta el final.

cards.definition_en: To communicate or convey an idea, feeling, or message so that it is understood.

examples.es: El profesor logró transmitir la idea de manera clara.
examples.en: The teacher managed to get across the idea clearly.

examples.es: Es difícil transmitir mis sentimientos en una carta tan breve.
examples.en: It is difficult to get across my feelings in such a short letter.

examples.es: El político intentó transmitir su mensaje de cambio a los votantes indecisos.
examples.en: The politician tried to get across his message of change to the undecided voters.

cards.example_sentence: The teacher managed to get across the idea clearly.
cards.example_es: El profesor logró transmitir la idea de manera clara.
cards.example_en: The teacher managed to get across the idea clearly.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a goat (get) crossing a bridge, shouting the news across the river like you transmit.

cards.definition_en: To succeed in doing something that is disapproved of or illegal without being punished.

examples.es: Juan intentó robar la tienda, pero la policía lo atrapó antes de que pudiera salirse con la suya.
examples.en: Juan tried to rob the store, but the police caught him before he could get away with it.

examples.es: El estudiante copió en el examen final y, aunque el profesor sospechaba, logró salirse con la suya.
examples.en: The student cheated on the final exam and, even though the teacher was suspicious, he managed to get away with it.

examples.es: Ella rompió el jarrón antiguo de su madre y escondió los trozos para intentar salirse con la suya.
examples.en: She broke her mother's antique vase and hid the pieces to try to get away with it.

cards.example_sentence: Juan tried to rob the store, but the police caught him before he could get away with it.
cards.example_es: Juan intentó robar la tienda, pero la policía lo atrapó antes de que pudiera salirse con la suya.
cards.example_en: Juan tried to rob the store, but the police caught him before he could get away with it.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cat that gets away with the fish, sneaking out (salir) triumphantly.

cards.definition_en: to manage to survive or cope with a situation, often with limited resources.

examples.es: Con tan poco dinero, tengo que arreglármelas.
examples.en: With so little money, I have to get by.

examples.es: Aunque no hablo francés, pude arreglármelas durante mi viaje a París.
examples.en: Even though I do not speak French, I managed to get by during my trip to Paris.

examples.es: Sin un coche, ella se las arregla usando el transporte público todos los días.
examples.en: Without a car, she manages to get by using public transportation every day.

cards.example_sentence: With so little money, I have to get by.
cards.example_es: Con tan poco dinero, tengo que arreglármelas.
cards.example_en: With so little money, I have to get by.
cards.mnemonic_en: A clever guy arranges tools to get by.

cards.definition_en: To recover from or move beyond something, such as an illness, difficulty, or emotional upset.

examples.es: Tuve que superar mi miedo a volar.
examples.en: I had to get over my fear of flying.

examples.es: Le costó mucho superar la ruptura con su pareja de hace años.
examples.en: It took her a long time to get over the breakup with her long-term partner.

examples.es: Es difícil superar el trauma de haber perdido su casa en el incendio.
examples.en: It is difficult to get over the trauma of losing his home in the fire.

cards.example_sentence: I had to get over my fear of flying.
cards.example_es: Tuve que superar mi miedo a volar.
cards.example_en: I had to get over my fear of flying.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a superhero goat leaping over a wall, shouting Get over, to remember Superar

cards.definition_en: to surrender or yield to someone or something

examples.es: Después de mucho discutir, decidió ceder a sus demandas.
examples.en: After a long argument, he decided to give in to his demands.

examples.es: Aunque el niño lloró durante horas, sus padres no quisieron ceder ante sus caprichos.
examples.en: Even though the child cried for hours, his parents refused to give in to his tantrums.

examples.es: La estructura del puente empezó a ceder bajo el peso excesivo de los camiones.
examples.en: The bridge structure started to give in under the excessive weight of the trucks.

cards.example_sentence: After a long argument, he decided to give in to his demands.
cards.example_es: Después de mucho discutir, decidió ceder a sus demandas.
cards.example_en: After a long argument, he decided to give in to his demands.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cedar tree bending, it gives in to the wind.

cards.definition_en: To stop trying to achieve something or to surrender.

examples.es: No voy a rendirme tan fácilmente.
examples.en: I won't give up so easily.

examples.es: Aunque el examen era muy difícil, decidió no rendirse y terminó todos los ejercicios.
examples.en: Even though the exam was very difficult, he decided not to give up and finished all the exercises.

examples.es: Después de intentar arreglar la computadora durante horas, finalmente tuve que rendirme y llamar a un técnico.
examples.en: After trying to fix the computer for hours, I finally had to give up and call a technician.

cards.example_sentence: I won't give up so easily.
cards.example_es: No voy a rendirme tan fácilmente.
cards.example_en: I won't give up so easily.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a torn (rend) rope that finally gives up and falls.

cards.definition_en: To examine or review something carefully.

examples.es: Voy a repasar mis notas antes del examen.
examples.en: I will go over my notes before the exam.

examples.es: El abogado necesita repasar el contrato antes de firmarlo.
examples.en: The lawyer needs to go over the contract before signing it.

examples.es: Vamos a repasar los detalles del viaje durante la cena.
examples.en: We are going to go over the travel details during dinner.

cards.example_sentence: I will go over my notes before the exam.
cards.example_es: Voy a repasar mis notas antes del examen.
cards.example_en: I will go over my notes before the exam.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a goat going over a fence, chanting re-pass ar to recall repasar

cards.definition_en: to develop from a child into an adult

examples.es: Los niños deben crecer en un ambiente seguro.
examples.en: Children should grow up in a safe environment.

examples.es: Ella quiere volver al pueblo donde pudo crecer rodeada de naturaleza.
examples.en: She wants to return to the village where she was able to grow up surrounded by nature.

examples.es: Es difícil ver a tus hijos crecer tan rápido mientras pasan los años.
examples.en: It is hard to watch your children grow up so fast as the years go by.

cards.example_sentence: Children should grow up in a safe environment.
cards.example_es: Los niños deben crecer en un ambiente seguro.
cards.example_en: Children should grow up in a safe environment.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a child growing up, shouting crecer as he reaches the sky

cards.definition_en: to submit something, such as a document or assignment, to the appropriate authority.

examples.es: Tengo que entregar el informe antes de las cinco.
examples.en: I have to hand in the report before five.

examples.es: Los estudiantes deben entregar sus exámenes al terminar la clase.
examples.en: Students must hand in their exams when they finish the class.

examples.es: No olvides entregar la solicitud firmada en la oficina de administración.
examples.en: Do not forget to hand in the signed application at the administration office.

cards.example_sentence: I have to hand in the report before five.
cards.example_es: Tengo que entregar el informe antes de las cinco.
cards.example_en: I have to hand in the report before five.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine handing in a rag as you enter the classroom.

cards.definition_en: To restrain or keep something from progressing or being expressed.

examples.es: No deberías reprimirte y decir lo que sientes.
examples.en: You shouldn't hold back and say what you feel.

examples.es: A pesar del miedo, ella no pudo reprimirse y saltó al agua.
examples.en: Despite her fear, she could not hold back and jumped into the water.

examples.es: Él intentó reprimirse, pero terminó estallando de risa en medio de la reunión.
examples.en: He tried to hold back, but he ended up bursting into laughter in the middle of the meeting.

cards.example_sentence: You shouldn't hold back and say what you feel.
cards.example_es: No deberías reprimirte y decir lo que sientes.
cards.example_en: You shouldn't hold back and say what you feel.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a REPRIMe bottle trying to hold back a flood.

cards.definition_en: To stay at the same level or pace as someone or something.
collocations: keep up with the latest trends

examples.es: Es difícil mantener el ritmo de la clase de baile.
examples.en: It's hard to keep up with the pace of the dance class.

examples.es: No puedo mantener el ritmo de las noticias tecnológicas porque cambian cada día.
examples.en: I cannot keep up with the latest tech news because it changes every day.

examples.es: Ella corre muy rápido y me cuesta mantener el ritmo de su entrenamiento.
examples.en: She runs very fast and I struggle to keep up with her training routine.

cards.example_sentence: It's hard to keep up with the pace of the dance class.
cards.example_es: Es difícil mantener el ritmo de la clase de baile.
cards.example_en: It's hard to keep up with the pace of the dance class.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cup dancing, keeping the beat—keep up with the rhythm.

cards.definition_en: To disappoint someone or fail to meet their expectations.

examples.es: No quería decepcionar a sus padres con sus malas notas.
examples.en: He didn't want to let down his parents with his poor grades.

examples.es: Prometió ayudarme con la mudanza, pero al final decidió decepcionar a todo el equipo.
examples.en: He promised to help me move, but in the end, he decided to let down the whole team.

examples.es: Si no terminas el proyecto a tiempo, vas a decepcionar a tu jefe antes de la reunión.
examples.en: If you don't finish the project on time, you are going to let down your boss before the meeting.

cards.example_sentence: He didn't want to let down his parents with his poor grades.
cards.example_es: No quería decepcionar a sus padres con sus malas notas.
cards.example_en: He didn't want to let down his parents with his poor grades.
cards.mnemonic_en: Let a deceptive kite fall down, you just let down (disappointed) them.

cards.definition_en: to take care of someone or something

examples.es: Tengo que cuidar de mi hermano pequeño mientras mis padres están fuera.
examples.en: I have to look after my little brother while my parents are away.

examples.es: La vecina se ofreció a cuidar de nuestras plantas durante las vacaciones.
examples.en: The neighbor offered to look after our plants while we are on vacation.

examples.es: Es difícil cuidar de un cachorro tan energético sin ayuda.
examples.en: It is difficult to look after such an energetic puppy without any help.

cards.example_sentence: I have to look after my little brother while my parents are away.
cards.example_es: Tengo que cuidar de mi hermano pequeño mientras mis padres están fuera.
cards.example_en: I have to look after my little brother while my parents are away.
cards.mnemonic_en: When you look after someone, you cuidar them like a caring guard.

cards.definition_en: to examine or investigate something in order to learn more about it

examples.es: Voy a investigar el origen del problema.
examples.en: I will look into the origin of the problem.

examples.es: El comité va a investigar las irregularidades financieras detectadas en el informe.
examples.en: The committee is going to look into the financial irregularities detected in the report.

examples.es: Deberíamos investigar las quejas de los clientes antes de lanzar el nuevo producto.
examples.en: We should look into the customer complaints before launching the new product.

cards.example_sentence: I will look into the origin of the problem.
cards.example_es: Voy a investigar el origen del problema.
cards.example_en: I will look into the origin of the problem.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a detective looking into a dark vault, thinking invest I gear as he investigates.

cards.definition_en: to regard someone with admiration and respect
collocations: look up to as an inspiration

examples.es: Siempre he admirado a mi abuelo por su sabiduría.
examples.en: I have always looked up to my grandfather for his wisdom.

examples.es: Los niños pequeños suelen admirar a los atletas profesionales que ven en la televisión.
examples.en: Young children often look up to the professional athletes they see on television.

examples.es: Ella admira a su mentora por la forma en que maneja los conflictos en la oficina.
examples.en: She looks up to her mentor for the way she handles conflicts in the office.

cards.example_sentence: I have always looked up to my grandfather for his wisdom.
cards.example_es: Siempre he admirado a mi abuelo por su sabiduría.
cards.example_en: I have always looked up to my grandfather for his wisdom.
cards.mnemonic_en: I look up to her, I admire her.

cards.definition_en: To compensate for something; to offset or counterbalance it.

examples.es: El aumento de sueldo ayuda a compensar el tiempo extra que trabajó durante el mes.
examples.en: The raise helps to make up for the extra time he worked during the month.

examples.es: Su talento natural compensa la falta de experiencia que tiene sobre el escenario.
examples.en: Her natural talent helps to make up for the lack of experience she has on stage.

examples.es: La excelente calidad de la comida compensa el servicio tan lento que recibimos anoche.
examples.en: The excellent quality of the food serves to make up for the slow service we received last night.

cards.example_sentence: The raise helps to make up for the extra time he worked during the month.
cards.example_es: El aumento de sueldo ayuda a compensar el tiempo extra que trabajó durante el mes.
cards.example_en: The raise helps to make up for the extra time he worked during the month.
cards.mnemonic_en: A compass (compensar) corrects the wrong direction, making up for the mistake.

cards.definition_en: to continue or proceed to the next thing, especially after a difficulty or interruption

examples.es: Después de la ruptura, decidió seguir adelante con su vida.
examples.en: After the breakup, she decided to move on with her life.

examples.es: Aunque el proyecto fracasó, el equipo debe seguir adelante y aprender de los errores.
examples.en: Even though the project failed, the team must move on and learn from their mistakes.

examples.es: Es hora de dejar el pasado atrás y seguir adelante con nuestros planes de viaje.
examples.en: It is time to leave the past behind and move on with our travel plans.

cards.example_sentence: After the breakup, she decided to move on with her life.
cards.example_es: Después de la ruptura, decidió seguir adelante con su vida.
cards.example_en: After the breakup, she decided to move on with her life.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a moving train shouting 'Move on' as it speeds forward, just like seguir adelante.

cards.definition_en: To draw attention to something.

examples.es: El editor señaló un error tipográfico en la última página del manuscrito.
examples.en: The editor had to point out a typo on the last page of the manuscript.

examples.es: Mi colega señaló que el informe financiero contenía datos desactualizados.
examples.en: My colleague was quick to point out that the financial report contained outdated data.

examples.es: El experto señaló las ventajas de invertir en energías renovables a largo plazo.
examples.en: The expert took a moment to point out the long-term benefits of investing in renewable energy.

cards.example_sentence: The editor had to point out a typo on the last page of the manuscript.
cards.example_es: El editor señaló un error tipográfico en la última página del manuscrito.
cards.example_en: The editor had to point out a typo on the last page of the manuscript.
cards.mnemonic_en: I point out the bright señal on the map, like a sign pointing the way.

cards.definition_en: To delay or postpone something.

examples.es: Voy a posponer la reunión hasta mañana.
examples.en: I will put off the meeting until tomorrow.

examples.es: No deberías posponer tu visita al dentista si te duele la muela.
examples.en: You should not put off your visit to the dentist if your tooth hurts.

examples.es: El mal tiempo obligó a los organizadores a posponer el partido de fútbol.
examples.en: The bad weather forced the organizers to put off the soccer match.

cards.example_sentence: I will put off the meeting until tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Voy a posponer la reunión hasta mañana.
cards.example_en: I will put off the meeting until tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: You put off the job, so you pose your plans in a pond, waiting.

cards.definition_en: to tolerate or endure something or someone
collocations: can't put up with his attitude

examples.es: No puedo soportar el ruido constante.
examples.en: I can't put up with the constant noise.

examples.es: Ella decidió que ya no iba a soportar más sus malos tratos.
examples.en: She decided she was not going to put up with his mistreatment anymore.

examples.es: Es difícil soportar el comportamiento grosero de ese cliente durante toda la reunión.
examples.en: It is difficult to put up with that client's rude behavior throughout the entire meeting.

cards.example_sentence: I can't put up with the constant noise.
cards.example_es: No puedo soportar el ruido constante.
cards.example_en: I can't put up with the constant noise.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a supportive porter who puts up with every heavy load, just like the Spanish soportar.

cards.definition_en: To eliminate or exclude something as a possibility.

examples.es: Los médicos deben descartar otras causas antes de confirmar el diagnóstico.
examples.en: Doctors must rule out other causes before confirming the diagnosis.

examples.es: La policía tuvo que descartar al principal sospechoso debido a su coartada sólida.
examples.en: The police had to rule out the main suspect due to his solid alibi.

examples.es: No podemos descartar la posibilidad de que el proyecto se retrase por falta de fondos.
examples.en: We cannot rule out the possibility that the project will be delayed due to lack of funding.

cards.example_sentence: Doctors must rule out other causes before confirming the diagnosis.
cards.example_es: Los médicos deben descartar otras causas antes de confirmar el diagnóstico.
cards.example_en: Doctors must rule out other causes before confirming the diagnosis.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a ruler striking out a card, discarding it—so you rule out the option.

cards.definition_en: To meet or encounter someone or something unexpectedly.

examples.es: Me topé con mi viejo amigo en el supermercado.
examples.en: I ran into my old friend at the supermarket.

examples.es: Casi me topo con el director mientras salía a escondidas de la oficina.
examples.en: I almost ran into the director while sneaking out of the office.

examples.es: Es muy probable que te topes con algunos problemas técnicos durante la instalación.
examples.en: It is very likely that you will run into some technical problems during the installation.

cards.example_sentence: I ran into my old friend at the supermarket.
cards.example_es: Me topé con mi viejo amigo en el supermercado.
cards.example_en: I ran into my old friend at the supermarket.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a spinning top that runs into a wall, reminding you of run into.

cards.definition_en: To reserve something for later use or a specific purpose.

examples.es: Voy a reservar tiempo para estudiar.
examples.en: I will set aside time to study.

examples.es: Debes reservar algo de dinero cada mes para emergencias.
examples.en: You should set aside some money every month for emergencies.

examples.es: El arquitecto decidió reservar una zona del jardín para construir una piscina.
examples.en: The architect decided to set aside an area of the garden to build a pool.

cards.example_sentence: I will set aside time to study.
cards.example_es: Voy a reservar tiempo para estudiar.
cards.example_en: I will set aside time to study.
cards.mnemonic_en: I set aside a seat for the reservation, like reserving a spot.

cards.definition_en: to arrange or establish something, such as equipment, a system, or an event.

examples.es: Vamos a montar la carpa antes de que empiece a llover.
examples.en: Let's set up the tent before it starts raining.

examples.es: El técnico vino a montar el nuevo sistema de seguridad en la oficina.
examples.en: The technician came to set up the new security system in the office.

examples.es: Necesitamos montar una mesa adicional para los invitados que llegarán tarde.
examples.en: We need to set up an extra table for the guests arriving late.

cards.example_sentence: Let's set up the tent before it starts raining.
cards.example_es: Vamos a montar la carpa antes de que empiece a llover.
cards.example_en: Let's set up the tent before it starts raining.
cards.mnemonic_en: Mount a horse, then set up the camp.

cards.definition_en: To organize, resolve, or clarify something.

examples.es: Necesito aclarar los detalles del proyecto antes de la reunión.
examples.en: I need to sort out the project details before the meeting.

examples.es: Tenemos que aclarar este malentendido con el cliente lo antes posible.
examples.en: We have to sort out this misunderstanding with the client as soon as possible.

examples.es: Mi hermano se mudará pronto y todavía tiene que aclarar sus asuntos personales.
examples.en: My brother is moving soon and he still needs to sort out his personal affairs.

cards.example_sentence: I need to sort out the project details before the meeting.
cards.example_es: Necesito aclarar los detalles del proyecto antes de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I need to sort out the project details before the meeting.
cards.mnemonic_en: Sort out the tangled cords until the room looks a clear.

cards.definition_en: To speak louder or more assertively, especially to express an opinion.

examples.es: Es importante hablar con firmeza cuando se defienden los derechos humanos.
examples.en: It's important to speak up when defending human rights.

examples.es: Si no estás de acuerdo con la nueva política de la empresa, deberías hablar con firmeza en la próxima reunión.
examples.en: If you disagree with the new company policy, you should speak up at the next meeting.

examples.es: Ella decidió hablar con firmeza contra el acoso escolar para proteger a sus compañeros más jóvenes.
examples.en: She decided to speak up against bullying to protect her younger classmates.

cards.example_sentence: It's important to speak up when defending human rights.
cards.example_es: Es importante hablar con firmeza cuando se defienden los derechos humanos.
cards.example_en: It's important to speak up when defending human rights.
cards.mnemonic_en: A firm microphone shouts Speak up!

cards.definition_en: To resign from a position or office.

examples.es: El presidente anunció que va a dimitir después del escándalo.
examples.en: The president announced that he will step down after the scandal.

examples.es: La directora decidió dimitir para que un líder más joven tome el mando de la empresa.
examples.en: The CEO decided to step down so that a younger leader could take charge of the company.

examples.es: Tras la derrota electoral, el ministro se vio obligado a dimitir de su cargo.
examples.en: Following the election defeat, the minister was forced to step down from his position.

cards.example_sentence: The president announced that he will step down after the scandal.
cards.example_es: El presidente anunció que va a dimitir después del escándalo.
cards.example_en: The president announced that he will step down after the scandal.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a timid boss taking a step down from the throne.

cards.definition_en: to resemble or be similar to someone, especially a family member

examples.es: Mi hijo tiene los mismos ojos azules que su abuelo, realmente se parece a él.
examples.en: My son has the same blue eyes as his grandfather; he really does take after him.

examples.es: Aunque su madre es muy reservada, la niña es muy extrovertida y no se parece a ella en absoluto.
examples.en: Even though her mother is very reserved, the girl is quite outgoing and does not take after her at all.

examples.es: Todos dicen que mi hermano menor se parece a nuestro padre cuando tenía esa misma edad.
examples.en: Everyone says that my younger brother seems to take after our father when he was that same age.

cards.example_sentence: My son has the same blue eyes as his grandfather; he really does take after him.
cards.example_es: Mi hijo tiene los mismos ojos azules que su abuelo, realmente se parece a él.
cards.example_en: My son has the same blue eyes as his grandfather; he really does take after him.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a pair of tacos you take after, because 'take after' sounds like a 'pair' (pare) of tacos.

cards.definition_en: To accept responsibility for something or to assume a task or role.

examples.es: Decidió asumir la responsabilidad del proyecto.
examples.en: He decided to take on the responsibility of the project.

examples.es: La empresa va a asumir a diez empleados nuevos el próximo mes.
examples.en: The company is going to take on ten new employees next month.

examples.es: No deberías asumir tantos compromisos si ya estás muy ocupado.
examples.en: You should not take on so many commitments if you are already very busy.

cards.example_sentence: He decided to take on the responsibility of the project.
cards.example_es: Decidió asumir la responsabilidad del proyecto.
cards.example_en: He decided to take on the responsibility of the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: Take on sounds like a sum, so picture yourself taking on a huge pile of cash.

cards.definition_en: to assume control or responsibility for something

examples.es: El nuevo CEO decidió tomar el control de la empresa.
examples.en: The new CEO decided to take over the company.

examples.es: El copiloto tuvo que tomar el control cuando el capitán se desmayó.
examples.en: The co-pilot had to take over when the captain fainted.

examples.es: Mi hermano mayor va a tomar el control de la granja familiar el próximo año.
examples.en: My older brother is going to take over the family farm next year.

cards.example_sentence: The new CEO decided to take over the company.
cards.example_es: El nuevo CEO decidió tomar el control de la empresa.
cards.example_en: The new CEO decided to take over the company.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a giant taco taking over the control console.

cards.definition_en: to consider something carefully

examples.es: Antes de aceptar la propuesta de matrimonio, ella decidió que necesitaba pensarlo detenidamente durante el fin de semana.
examples.en: Before accepting the marriage proposal, she decided she needed to think over the decision during the weekend.

examples.es: El comité no dará una respuesta inmediata, ya que prefieren pensarlo detenidamente antes de comprometer el presupuesto anual.
examples.en: The committee will not give an immediate answer, as they prefer to think over the request before committing the annual budget.

examples.es: Si te ofrecen un traslado al extranjero, asegúrate de pensarlo detenidamente antes de empacar todas tus pertenencias.
examples.en: If you are offered a transfer abroad, make sure to think over the implications before packing up all your belongings.

cards.example_sentence: Before accepting the marriage proposal, she decided she needed to think over the decision during the weekend.
cards.example_es: Antes de aceptar la propuesta de matrimonio, ella decidió que necesitaba pensarlo detenidamente durante el fin de semana.
cards.example_en: Before accepting the marriage proposal, she decided she needed to think over the decision during the weekend.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a thinker standing over a pond, whispering 'pensar' as he weighs the idea.

cards.definition_en: To reject or refuse something.

examples.es: Decidió rechazar la oferta de trabajo.
examples.en: He decided to turn down the job offer.

examples.es: Tuve que rechazar su invitación a la fiesta porque tenía mucho trabajo.
examples.en: I had to turn down his invitation to the party because I had too much work.

examples.es: El banco volvió a rechazar nuestra solicitud de préstamo hipotecario.
examples.en: The bank had to turn down our mortgage loan application again.

cards.example_sentence: He decided to turn down the job offer.
cards.example_es: Decidió rechazar la oferta de trabajo.
cards.example_en: He decided to turn down the job offer.
cards.mnemonic_en: I reach a jar, then turn it down, rejecting the offer.

cards.definition_en: to have a particular result or outcome

examples.es: El pastel que horneé para la fiesta resultó ser un desastre porque olvidé ponerle azúcar.
examples.en: The cake I baked for the party turned out to be a disaster because I forgot to add sugar.

examples.es: La entrevista de trabajo resultó ser mucho más difícil de lo que esperaba inicialmente.
examples.en: The job interview turned out to be much more difficult than I had initially expected.

examples.es: Aunque al principio parecían enemigos, los dos vecinos resultaron ser grandes amigos con el paso del tiempo.
examples.en: Although they seemed like enemies at first, the two neighbors turned out to be great friends as time went by.

cards.example_sentence: The cake I baked for the party turned out to be a disaster because I forgot to add sugar.
cards.example_es: El pastel que horneé para la fiesta resultó ser un desastre porque olvidé ponerle azúcar.
cards.example_en: The cake I baked for the party turned out to be a disaster because I forgot to add sugar.
cards.mnemonic_en: The result of the experiment turned out exactly as predicted.

cards.definition_en: to succeed or have a favorable outcome

examples.es: Al final, todo se salió bien.
examples.en: In the end, everything will work out.

examples.es: Aunque tuvimos muchos problemas al principio, la negociación terminó por salir bien.
examples.en: Although we had many problems at the beginning, the negotiation managed to work out.

examples.es: Si ajustamos el presupuesto y reducimos los gastos, el proyecto debería salir bien.
examples.en: If we adjust the budget and cut costs, the project should work out.

cards.example_sentence: In the end, everything will work out.
cards.example_es: Al final, todo se salió bien.
cards.example_en: In the end, everything will work out.
cards.mnemonic_en: Sail out of the gym after a workout, feeling well.

cards.definition_en: to withdraw from an agreement, plan, or commitment
collocations: back out at the last minute

examples.es: Decidió retirarse del proyecto antes de que fuera demasiado tarde.
examples.en: He decided to back out of the project before it was too late.

examples.es: No puedes retirarte ahora que ya hemos firmado el contrato de compraventa.
examples.en: You cannot back out now that we have already signed the sales contract.

examples.es: El candidato se retiró de la competencia a pocos días de las elecciones.
examples.en: The candidate had to back out of the race just days before the election.

cards.example_sentence: He decided to back out of the project before it was too late.
cards.example_es: Decidió retirarse del proyecto antes de que fuera demasiado tarde.
cards.example_en: He decided to back out of the project before it was too late.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a car backing out while shouting retire as it leaves the driveway.

cards.definition_en: To end a relationship with someone.

examples.es: Decidió romper con su novia después de tres años.
examples.en: He decided to break off his relationship with his girlfriend after three years.

examples.es: Las dos naciones han decidido romper con sus acuerdos comerciales debido a las nuevas sanciones.
examples.en: The two nations have decided to break off their trade agreements due to the new sanctions.

examples.es: Durante la tensa reunión, el diplomático se levantó y decidió romper con las negociaciones de paz.
examples.en: During the tense meeting, the diplomat stood up and decided to break off the peace negotiations.

cards.example_sentence: He decided to break off his relationship with his girlfriend after three years.
cards.example_es: Decidió romper con su novia después de tres años.
cards.example_en: He decided to break off his relationship with his girlfriend after three years.
cards.mnemonic_en: I break off the chain, Romper con the lock, ending its hold.

cards.definition_en: To succeed in overcoming an obstacle or to make a way forward.

examples.es: Los pioneros tuvieron que abrirse paso en la selva.
examples.en: The pioneers had to break through the jungle.

examples.es: El sol finalmente logró abrirse paso entre las nubes grises de la mañana.
examples.en: The sun finally managed to break through the gray morning clouds.

examples.es: La policía tuvo que abrirse paso entre la multitud para llegar al lugar del accidente.
examples.en: The police had to break through the crowd to reach the accident scene.

cards.example_sentence: The pioneers had to break through the jungle.
cards.example_es: Los pioneros tuvieron que abrirse paso en la selva.
cards.example_en: The pioneers had to break through the jungle.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a brake breaking a wall, letting people abrirse paso.

cards.definition_en: To interrupt someone while they are speaking.

examples.es: No deberías interrumpir cuando alguien está hablando.
examples.en: You shouldn't cut in when someone is speaking.

examples.es: El coche rojo intentó interrumpir en la fila de tráfico de repente.
examples.en: The red car suddenly tried to cut in the line of traffic.

examples.es: Ella se sintió molesta cuando su colega decidió interrumpir durante su presentación.
examples.en: She felt annoyed when her colleague decided to cut in during her presentation.

cards.example_sentence: You shouldn't cut in when someone is speaking.
cards.example_es: No deberías interrumpir cuando alguien está hablando.
cards.example_en: You shouldn't cut in when someone is speaking.
cards.mnemonic_en: A cutter (corte) jumps in, interrupting the chat.

cards.definition_en: To consume something completely until none remains.

examples.es: El incendio logró agotar todo el suministro de agua de la zona.
examples.en: The fire managed to use up all the water supply in the area.

examples.es: No podemos seguir imprimiendo documentos porque vamos a agotar el tóner de la impresora.
examples.en: We cannot keep printing documents because we are going to use up the printer's toner.

examples.es: Los atletas suelen agotar sus reservas de energía durante la última etapa de la maratón.
examples.en: Athletes tend to use up their energy reserves during the final stage of the marathon.

cards.example_sentence: The fire managed to use up all the water supply in the area.
cards.example_es: El incendio logró agotar todo el suministro de agua de la zona.
cards.example_en: The fire managed to use up all the water supply in the area.
cards.mnemonic_en: When you use up the battery, it goes a‑go‑tar empty.

cards.definition_en: to finally reach a particular place, condition, or result, often unexpectedly

examples.es: Si no estudias, podrías terminar en la calle.
examples.en: If you don't study, you could end up on the street.

examples.es: Después de buscar durante horas, terminamos en un pequeño pueblo pesquero al norte del país.
examples.en: After searching for hours, we end up in a small fishing village in the north of the country.

examples.es: Comenzamos viendo una comedia, pero terminamos en un maratón de películas de terror toda la noche.
examples.en: We started by watching a comedy, but we end up in a horror movie marathon all night long.

cards.example_sentence: If you don't study, you could end up on the street.
cards.example_es: Si no estudias, podrías terminar en la calle.
cards.example_en: If you don't study, you could end up on the street.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture crossing the finish line and yelling 'en!' as you finally end up there.

cards.definition_en: To discover or learn something.

examples.es: Después de buscar durante horas, finalmente di con la solución al problema.
examples.en: After searching for hours, I finally find out the solution to the problem.

examples.es: Revisando los archivos antiguos, espero que podamos dar con la fecha exacta del contrato.
examples.en: While checking the old files, I hope we can find out the exact date of the contract.

examples.es: Si investigas un poco en internet, seguro que das con el nombre del autor de este libro.
examples.en: If you do a little research online, you will surely find out the name of the author of this book.

cards.example_sentence: After searching for hours, I finally find out the solution to the problem.
cards.example_es: Después de buscar durante horas, finalmente di con la solución al problema.
cards.example_en: After searching for hours, I finally find out the solution to the problem.
cards.mnemonic_en: When I find out, I dar con the secret like uncovering a buried chest.

cards.definition_en: To gradually disappear or lose intensity.

examples.es: Con el tiempo, la luz del farol se fue apagando.
examples.en: Over time, the lantern's light began to fade away.

examples.es: El sonido de los pasos en el pasillo se fue apagando hasta quedar en silencio.
examples.en: The sound of footsteps in the hallway began to fade away until it went silent.

examples.es: La esperanza de encontrar a los excursionistas se fue apagando tras varios días de búsqueda sin éxito.
examples.en: The hope of finding the hikers began to fade away after several days of unsuccessful searching.

cards.example_sentence: Over time, the lantern's light began to fade away.
cards.example_es: Con el tiempo, la luz del farol se fue apagando.
cards.example_en: Over time, the lantern's light began to fade away.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a lamp fade away as its light apagando like a ghost slipping into darkness.

cards.definition_en: to take strict or severe measures against something or someone

examples.es: El gobierno decidió aplastar la evasión fiscal.
examples.en: The government decided to crack down on tax evasion.

examples.es: La policía local comenzó a aplastar el consumo de alcohol en las calles durante el festival.
examples.en: The local police started to crack down on street drinking during the festival.

examples.es: La dirección de la empresa prometió aplastar las horas extras no autorizadas a partir del próximo mes.
examples.en: The company management promised to crack down on unauthorized overtime starting next month.

cards.example_sentence: The government decided to crack down on tax evasion.
cards.example_es: El gobierno decidió aplastar la evasión fiscal.
cards.example_en: The government decided to crack down on tax evasion.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a crack hammer slamming down on a wall, crushing it flat like aplastar.

cards.definition_en: To dismiss or reject something or someone in a casual or indifferent manner.

examples.es: Cuando le sugerí que buscara ayuda profesional, él rechazó de plano mi consejo.
examples.en: When I suggested he seek professional help, he decided to brush off my advice entirely.

examples.es: El gerente rechazó de plano las quejas de los clientes sobre el nuevo horario de apertura.
examples.en: The manager chose to brush off the customers' complaints regarding the new opening hours.

examples.es: Aunque ella intentó explicar su versión de los hechos, él rechazó de plano cualquier intento de conversación.
examples.en: Even though she tried to explain her side of the story, he continued to brush off any attempt at conversation.

cards.example_sentence: When I suggested he seek professional help, he decided to brush off my advice entirely.
cards.example_es: Cuando le sugerí que buscara ayuda profesional, él rechazó de plano mi consejo.
cards.example_en: When I suggested he seek professional help, he decided to brush off my advice entirely.
cards.mnemonic_en: Brush off the plain (plano) like dust from a model plane

cards.definition_en: to agree with or support something or someone

examples.es: Decidió acompañar a su hermano en la caminata.
examples.en: He decided to go along with his brother on the hike.

examples.es: Aunque no estaba convencido, aceptó acompañar la propuesta de su colega.
examples.en: Although he was not convinced, he agreed to go along with his colleague's proposal.

examples.es: Si quieres que el proyecto tenga éxito, debes acompañar las nuevas reglas del equipo.
examples.en: If you want the project to succeed, you must go along with the team's new rules.

cards.example_sentence: He decided to go along with his brother on the hike.
cards.example_es: Decidió acompañar a su hermano en la caminata.
cards.example_en: He decided to go along with his brother on the hike.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a companion shouting Go! Along! With! as you both walk.

cards.definition_en: to tolerate or endure something or someone

examples.es: No puedo tolerar el ruido constante de la construcción.
examples.en: I can't put up with the constant construction noise.

examples.es: Ella decidió que ya no iba a tolerar más el comportamiento grosero de su jefe.
examples.en: She decided she was not going to put up with her boss's rude behavior anymore.

examples.es: Es difícil tolerar el frío intenso sin una calefacción adecuada en casa.
examples.en: It is difficult to put up with the intense cold without proper heating at home.

cards.example_sentence: I can't put up with the constant construction noise.
cards.example_es: No puedo tolerar el ruido constante de la construcción.
cards.example_en: I can't put up with the constant construction noise.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a stubborn bull you tolerate, so you literally put up with it.
