cards.definition_en: To intervene or become involved in a situation.
collocations: step in at the last minute

examples.es: El médico tuvo que intervenir rápidamente para salvar al paciente.
examples.en: The doctor had to step in quickly to save the patient.

examples.es: Cuando la discusión se volvió agresiva, el profesor decidió intervenir para calmar los ánimos.
examples.en: When the argument became aggressive, the teacher decided to step in to calm things down.

examples.es: Mi jefe tuvo que intervenir en la negociación porque el cliente estaba muy insatisfecho con el contrato.
examples.en: My boss had to step in during the negotiation because the client was very unhappy with the contract.

cards.example_sentence: The doctor had to step in quickly to save the patient.
cards.example_es: El médico tuvo que intervenir rápidamente para salvar al paciente.
cards.example_en: The doctor had to step in quickly to save the patient.
cards.mnemonic_en: Step in like an intervenir hero entering the scene

cards.definition_en: To gradually stop using or providing something.

examples.es: La fábrica decidió retirar gradualmente el modelo antiguo de lavadoras para lanzar la nueva línea de productos.
examples.en: The factory decided to phase out the old model of washing machines to launch the new product line.

examples.es: El gobierno planea retirarse gradualmente del uso de combustibles fósiles para el año 2040.
examples.en: The government plans to phase out the use of fossil fuels by the year 2040.

examples.es: La universidad va a retirarse gradualmente de los exámenes impresos para adoptar un sistema totalmente digital.
examples.en: The university is going to phase out printed exams to adopt a fully digital system.

cards.example_sentence: The factory decided to phase out the old model of washing machines to launch the new product line.
cards.example_es: La fábrica decidió retirar gradualmente el modelo antiguo de lavadoras para lanzar la nueva línea de productos.
cards.example_en: The factory decided to phase out the old model of washing machines to launch the new product line.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a stage (phase) where you slowly walk out, retiring step by step.

cards.definition_en: To introduce something gradually or in stages.

examples.es: La empresa decidió introducir gradualmente el nuevo software para evitar problemas.
examples.en: The company decided to phase in the new software to avoid problems.

examples.es: El gobierno planea introducir gradualmente los nuevos impuestos sobre el carbono durante los próximos cinco años.
examples.en: The government plans to phase in the new carbon taxes over the next five years.

examples.es: La escuela va a introducir gradualmente el uso de tabletas en todas las aulas a partir del próximo semestre.
examples.en: The school is going to phase in the use of tablets in all classrooms starting next semester.

cards.example_sentence: The company decided to phase in the new software to avoid problems.
cards.example_es: La empresa decidió introducir gradualmente el nuevo software para evitar problemas.
cards.example_en: The company decided to phase in the new software to avoid problems.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine “fases en” – the Spanish for phases in – as you phase in something gradually.

cards.definition_en: to cause something to collapse, fail, or separate into its component parts.

examples.es: El coche empezó a fallar y se detuvo en medio de la carretera.
examples.en: The car started to break down and stopped in the middle of the road.

examples.es: La vieja nevera comenzó a fallar justo después de que terminara la garantía.
examples.en: The old fridge started to break down right after the warranty expired.

examples.es: Si el sistema de calefacción vuelve a fallar durante el invierno, tendremos que llamar a un técnico.
examples.en: If the heating system starts to break down again during the winter, we will have to call a technician.

cards.example_sentence: The car started to break down and stopped in the middle of the road.
cards.example_es: El coche empezó a fallar y se detuvo en medio de la carretera.
cards.example_en: The car started to break down and stopped in the middle of the road.
cards.mnemonic_en: When it falls, it breaks down.

cards.definition_en: To end a romantic relationship.

examples.es: Después de cinco años de relación, decidieron separarse.
examples.en: After five years together, they decided to break up.

examples.es: La banda anunció que iban a separarse tras finalizar su última gira mundial.
examples.en: The band announced that they were going to break up after finishing their final world tour.

examples.es: Si no dejan de discutir por tonterías, al final van a separarse.
examples.en: If you two do not stop arguing over nonsense, you are eventually going to break up.

cards.example_sentence: After five years together, they decided to break up.
cards.example_es: Después de cinco años de relación, decidieron separarse.
cards.example_en: After five years together, they decided to break up.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a broken vase that separates into shards as the couple breaks up

cards.definition_en: to become or cause someone to become less agitated or upset
collocations: Calm down, it's not a big deal

examples.es: Después de la discusión, Juan decidió calmarse y respirar profundamente.
examples.en: After the argument, John decided to calm down and take a deep breath.

examples.es: Por favor, intenta calmarse antes de que el bebé se despierte con tus gritos.
examples.en: Please try to calm down before the baby wakes up from your shouting.

examples.es: El piloto pidió a los pasajeros que se calmaran mientras la turbulencia sacudía el avión.
examples.en: The pilot asked the passengers to calm down while the turbulence shook the plane.

cards.example_sentence: After the argument, John decided to calm down and take a deep breath.
cards.example_es: Después de la discusión, Juan decidió calmarse y respirar profundamente.
cards.example_en: After the argument, John decided to calm down and take a deep breath.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a calm sea whispering calm down as the waves settle.

cards.definition_en: To cancel or stop something that was planned.

examples.es: Debido a la fuerte tormenta, los organizadores decidieron cancelar el partido de fútbol.
examples.en: Due to the heavy storm, the organizers decided to call off the soccer match.

examples.es: El director tuvo que cancelar la reunión de emergencia porque el cliente no pudo llegar a tiempo.
examples.en: The manager had to call off the emergency meeting because the client could not arrive on time.

examples.es: Si el clima no mejora mañana, tendremos que cancelar nuestro picnic en el parque.
examples.en: If the weather does not improve tomorrow, we will have to call off our picnic in the park.

cards.example_sentence: Due to the heavy storm, the organizers decided to call off the soccer match.
cards.example_es: Debido a la fuerte tormenta, los organizadores decidieron cancelar el partido de fútbol.
cards.example_en: Due to the heavy storm, the organizers decided to call off the soccer match.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a phone call saying cancela to end the party.

cards.definition_en: to continue doing something

examples.es: Decidió seguir adelante con el proyecto a pesar de los obstáculos.
examples.en: He decided to carry on with the project despite the obstacles.

examples.es: Aunque el clima empeoró, los excursionistas decidieron seguir adelante con su ascenso a la montaña.
examples.en: Even though the weather worsened, the hikers decided to carry on with their climb up the mountain.

examples.es: La empresa va a seguir adelante con los planes de expansión internacional el próximo año.
examples.en: The company is going to carry on with the international expansion plans next year.

cards.example_sentence: He decided to carry on with the project despite the obstacles.
cards.example_es: Decidió seguir adelante con el proyecto a pesar de los obstáculos.
cards.example_en: He decided to carry on with the project despite the obstacles.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cari (cariño) sprinting forward, carrying on the race.

cards.definition_en: to reach the same level or position as someone or something after falling behind

examples.es: Necesito alcanzar a mis compañeros de clase para no quedarme atrás.
examples.en: I need to catch up with my classmates so I don't fall behind.

examples.es: Me detuve un momento para alcanzar a mi amigo que caminaba más rápido que yo.
examples.en: I stopped for a moment to catch up with my friend who was walking faster than me.

examples.es: Después de estar enfermo toda la semana, tengo mucho trabajo que alcanzar en la oficina.
examples.en: After being sick all week, I have a lot of work to catch up on at the office.

cards.example_sentence: I need to catch up with my classmates so I don't fall behind.
cards.example_es: Necesito alcanzar a mis compañeros de clase para no quedarme atrás.
cards.example_en: I need to catch up with my classmates so I don't fall behind.
cards.mnemonic_en: Al can catch up with the runner

cards.definition_en: to examine or look at something, or to borrow it from a library

examples.es: Voy a comprobar el horario del tren antes de salir.
examples.en: I will check out the train schedule before leaving.

examples.es: Deberías comprobar ese nuevo restaurante italiano que abrieron en el centro.
examples.en: You should check out that new Italian restaurant they opened downtown.

examples.es: Vamos a comprobar los resultados del partido en la página web oficial.
examples.en: Let's check out the match results on the official website.

cards.example_sentence: I will check out the train schedule before leaving.
cards.example_es: Voy a comprobar el horario del tren antes de salir.
cards.example_en: I will check out the train schedule before leaving.
cards.mnemonic_en: I check out the giant compro bar that stamps my library books.

cards.definition_en: to rely on someone or something

examples.es: Puedes contar con mi ayuda para terminar el proyecto.
examples.en: You can count on my help to finish the project.

examples.es: El equipo sabe que puede contar con ella cuando el partido se pone difícil.
examples.en: The team knows they can count on her when the game gets tough.

examples.es: No podemos contar con que el tren llegue a tiempo debido a la tormenta.
examples.en: We cannot count on the train arriving on time due to the storm.

cards.example_sentence: You can count on my help to finish the project.
cards.example_es: Puedes contar con mi ayuda para terminar el proyecto.
cards.example_en: You can count on my help to finish the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: I count on you, picturing me counting coins (con) in my hand.

cards.definition_en: To stop or interrupt something abruptly.

examples.es: El presentador decidió interrumpir abruptamente la entrevista cuando el invitado se puso nervioso.
examples.en: The host decided to cut off the interview abruptly when the guest became nervous.

examples.es: La tormenta logró interrumpir abruptamente nuestra conexión a internet durante la videoconferencia.
examples.en: The storm managed to cut off our internet connection during the video conference.

examples.es: El locutor tuvo que cortar bruscamente la llamada telefónica porque el oyente empezó a insultar.
examples.en: The radio host had to cut off the caller abruptly when he started using offensive language.

cards.example_sentence: The host decided to cut off the interview abruptly when the guest became nervous.
cards.example_es: El presentador decidió interrumpir abruptamente la entrevista cuando el invitado se puso nervioso.
cards.example_en: The host decided to cut off the interview abruptly when the guest became nervous.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a scissors cut off the conversation, a sudden corte that abruptly interrupts.

cards.definition_en: To address, manage, or handle a problem, situation, or person.

examples.es: Tengo que lidiar con muchos problemas en el trabajo.
examples.en: I have to deal with many problems at work.

examples.es: El servicio al cliente debe lidiar con las quejas de los usuarios insatisfechos.
examples.en: Customer service must deal with complaints from dissatisfied users.

examples.es: Es difícil lidiar con la pérdida de un ser querido durante las fiestas.
examples.en: It is difficult to deal with the loss of a loved one during the holidays.

cards.example_sentence: I have to deal with many problems at work.
cards.example_es: Tengo que lidiar con muchos problemas en el trabajo.
cards.example_en: I have to deal with many problems at work.
cards.mnemonic_en: Deal with it like dealing cards while wearing a lid on your ear.

cards.definition_en: To manage or survive without something.

examples.es: Como la tienda cerró por vacaciones, tuvimos que arreglárnoslas sin café durante todo el fin de semana.
examples.en: Since the shop closed for the holidays, we had to do without coffee for the entire weekend.

examples.es: Durante la escasez de suministros, la familia tuvo que hacer un esfuerzo para prescindir de los lujos a los que estaban acostumbrados.
examples.en: During the supply shortage, the family had to make an effort to do without the luxuries they were accustomed to.

examples.es: El presupuesto se agotó por completo, así que tendremos que acabar con los gastos innecesarios este mes.
examples.en: The budget was completely exhausted, so we will have to do without unnecessary expenses this month.

cards.example_sentence: Since the shop closed for the holidays, we had to do without coffee for the entire weekend.
cards.example_es: Como la tienda cerró por vacaciones, tuvimos que arreglárnoslas sin café durante todo el fin de semana.
cards.example_en: Since the shop closed for the holidays, we had to do without coffee for the entire weekend.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a con being finished, so you do without the con.

cards.definition_en: To quit a school, program, or activity before completing it.

examples.es: Decidió abandonar la universidad después del primer semestre.
examples.en: He decided to drop out of college after the first semester.

examples.es: Muchos atletas jóvenes suelen abandonar la carrera profesional debido a las constantes lesiones.
examples.en: Many young athletes tend to drop out of their professional careers due to constant injuries.

examples.es: Si el programa de entrenamiento es demasiado exigente, es probable que la mitad de los participantes decidan abandonar.
examples.en: If the training program is too demanding, it is likely that half of the participants will decide to drop out.

cards.example_sentence: He decided to drop out of college after the first semester.
cards.example_es: Decidió abandonar la universidad después del primer semestre.
cards.example_en: He decided to drop out of college after the first semester.
cards.mnemonic_en: I dropped out, abandoning the game like a broken drop falling away.

cards.definition_en: to consume something completely, especially to finish eating it

examples.es: Voy a comerme todo el pastel.
examples.en: I'm going to eat up the whole cake.

examples.es: Si no te terminas la cena, no podrás salir a jugar.
examples.en: If you don't eat up your dinner, you won't be able to go out and play.

examples.es: El perro tiene tanta hambre que se comió todo el plato de comida en segundos.
examples.en: The dog is so hungry that he managed to eat up his entire bowl of food in seconds.

cards.example_sentence: I'm going to eat up the whole cake.
cards.example_es: Voy a comerme todo el pastel.
cards.example_en: I'm going to eat up the whole cake.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hungry monster that eats up every crumb, just like comerse todo.

cards.definition_en: To dismiss or rationalize something as unimportant or untrue by offering an explanation that reduces its significance.

examples.es: Trató de explicar su error como si no fuera gran cosa.
examples.en: He tried to explain away his mistake as if it were nothing.

examples.es: El político intentó explicar su ausencia en la votación como un problema técnico.
examples.en: The politician attempted to explain away his absence from the vote as a technical glitch.

examples.es: No puedes simplemente explicar tu falta de puntualidad diciendo que el tráfico estaba mal.
examples.en: You cannot simply explain away your lack of punctuality by claiming the traffic was bad.

cards.example_sentence: He tried to explain away his mistake as if it were nothing.
cards.example_es: Trató de explicar su error como si no fuera gran cosa.
cards.example_en: He tried to explain away his mistake as if it were nothing.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an ex‑plane flying away, explaining the mystery.

cards.definition_en: To cause something to become full or to become full oneself.

examples.es: Decidimos parar en la gasolinera para llenar el depósito antes de continuar nuestro viaje por carretera.
examples.en: We decided to stop at the gas station to fill up the tank before continuing our road trip.

examples.es: La sala de espera se llenó de pacientes ansiosos durante la temporada de gripe.
examples.en: The waiting room began to fill up with anxious patients during the flu season.

examples.es: El disco duro de mi computadora se llenó de archivos temporales y fotos antiguas.
examples.en: My computer's hard drive started to fill up with temporary files and old photos.

cards.example_sentence: We decided to stop at the gas station to fill up the tank before continuing our road trip.
cards.example_es: Decidimos parar en la gasolinera para llenar el depósito antes de continuar nuestro viaje por carretera.
cards.example_en: We decided to stop at the gas station to fill up the tank before continuing our road trip.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture Lena's cup overflowing as it fills up.

cards.definition_en: to be accepted as part of a group or environment

examples.es: Aunque me esforcé por ser amable, sentí que nunca logré encajar entre mis compañeros de clase.
examples.en: Even though I tried to be friendly, I felt that I never managed to fit in with my classmates.

examples.es: Aunque viste ropa de marca, nunca logró encajar en ese grupo de amigos tan exclusivo.
examples.en: Even though he wears designer clothes, he never managed to fit in with that exclusive group of friends.

examples.es: Como extranjero en un país nuevo, es normal que te tome tiempo encajar en la cultura local.
examples.en: As a foreigner in a new country, it is normal that it takes time to fit in with the local culture.

cards.example_sentence: Even though I tried to be friendly, I felt that I never managed to fit in with my classmates.
cards.example_es: Aunque me esforcé por ser amable, sentí que nunca logré encajar entre mis compañeros de clase.
cards.example_en: Even though I tried to be friendly, I felt that I never managed to fit in with my classmates.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a key that fits in a lock, just like encajar means to fit in.

cards.definition_en: to continue doing something despite obstacles or difficulties

examples.es: Tras la interrupción por la lluvia, el árbitro nos indicó que dejáramos de esperar y siguiéramos adelante con el partido.
examples.en: After the rain delay, the referee signaled for us to stop waiting and get on with the match.

examples.es: Después de discutir brevemente sobre quién debería conducir, decidimos dejar de perder el tiempo y seguir adelante con nuestro viaje por carretera.
examples.en: After arguing briefly about who should drive, we decided to stop wasting time and get on with our road trip.

examples.es: Tras la breve interrupción por el fallo técnico, el director nos pidió que dejáramos de quejarnos y siguiéramos adelante a pesar de todo con la grabación de la escena.
examples.en: After the technical glitch interrupted the filming, the director told us to stop complaining and get on with the scene.

cards.example_sentence: After the rain delay, the referee signaled for us to stop waiting and get on with the match.
cards.example_es: Tras la interrupción por la lluvia, el árbitro nos indicó que dejáramos de esperar y siguiéramos adelante con el partido.
cards.example_en: After the rain delay, the referee signaled for us to stop waiting and get on with the match.
cards.mnemonic_en: A cat with a briefcase yells 'Get on with it!' as it pushes forward.

cards.definition_en: To descend from or leave a vehicle, platform, or other surface.

examples.es: Tienes que bajarse del autobús en la próxima parada para llegar al centro.
examples.en: You need to get off the bus at the next stop to reach the city center.

examples.es: El escalador decidió bajarse de la montaña debido a la tormenta inminente.
examples.en: The climber decided to get off the mountain because of the incoming storm.

examples.es: Cuando llegamos a la estación, tuvimos que bajarse de la bicicleta rápidamente.
examples.en: When we arrived at the station, we had to get off the bike quickly.

cards.example_sentence: You need to get off the bus at the next stop to reach the city center.
cards.example_es: Tienes que bajarse del autobús en la próxima parada para llegar al centro.
cards.example_en: You need to get off the bus at the next stop to reach the city center.
cards.mnemonic_en: When you get off, imagine shouting baja as you step down the stairs.

cards.definition_en: To board or mount something, such as a vehicle or animal.

examples.es: Voy a subirme al autobús antes de que salga.
examples.en: I'm going to get on the bus before it leaves.

examples.es: Tienes que subirte a la bicicleta si quieres llegar a tiempo a la clase.
examples.en: You have to get on the bike if you want to arrive at class on time.

examples.es: Es peligroso subirme a un tren en movimiento.
examples.en: It is dangerous to get on a moving train.

cards.example_sentence: I'm going to get on the bus before it leaves.
cards.example_es: Voy a subirme al autobús antes de que salga.
cards.example_en: I'm going to get on the bus before it leaves.
cards.mnemonic_en: I get on the bus while shouting subir as the doors rise

cards.definition_en: To return to a previous place or condition.
collocations: go back to the drawing board

examples.es: Necesito volver a casa antes de la tormenta.
examples.en: I need to go back home before the storm.

examples.es: Olvidé mi pasaporte en el hotel, así que tuve que volver a buscarlo.
examples.en: I forgot my passport at the hotel, so I had to go back to get it.

examples.es: Si no te gusta el trabajo, siempre puedes volver a tu antigua empresa.
examples.en: If you do not like the job, you can always go back to your old company.

cards.example_sentence: I need to go back home before the storm.
cards.example_es: Necesito volver a casa antes de la tormenta.
cards.example_en: I need to go back home before the storm.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a goat (go) backing up toward a volcano (volver).

cards.definition_en: To leave a place and go outside.

examples.es: Vamos a salir después de la cena.
examples.en: We are going to go out after dinner.

examples.es: Ella se puso su mejor vestido para salir esta noche.
examples.en: She put on her best dress to go out tonight.

examples.es: No me gusta salir cuando hace tanto frío afuera.
examples.en: I do not like to go out when it is so cold outside.

cards.example_sentence: We are going to go out after dinner.
cards.example_es: Vamos a salir después de la cena.
cards.example_en: We are going to go out after dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a sailor stepping out of the ship—'salir' sounds like sailor, so you go out.

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to act more quickly.

examples.es: Debes acelerarte si no quieres perder el tren.
examples.en: You have to hurry up if you don't want to miss the train.

examples.es: El chef nos pidió acelerarnos porque la cocina está a punto de cerrar.
examples.en: The chef told us to hurry up because the kitchen is about to close.

examples.es: Si queremos llegar a la cima antes de que oscurezca, tenemos que acelerarnos.
examples.en: If we want to reach the summit before it gets dark, we need to hurry up.

cards.example_sentence: You have to hurry up if you don't want to miss the train.
cards.example_es: Debes acelerarte si no quieres perder el tren.
cards.example_en: You have to hurry up if you don't want to miss the train.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a hare hurrying up a steep hill, shouting Acelera! – that reminds you of hurry up.

cards.definition_en: To delay or postpone something.

examples.es: Decidimos posponer la reunión hasta más tarde.
examples.en: We decided to hold off the meeting until later.

examples.es: El banco me pidió posponer hasta más tarde el pago de la hipoteca.
examples.en: The bank asked me to hold off the mortgage payment until later.

examples.es: Debido a la incertidumbre económica, decidimos posponer hasta más tarde la inversión en el nuevo proyecto.
examples.en: Due to the economic uncertainty, we decided to hold off on the investment in the new project.

cards.example_sentence: We decided to hold off the meeting until later.
cards.example_es: Decidimos posponer la reunión hasta más tarde.
cards.example_en: We decided to hold off the meeting until later.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine holding a post off the road to postpone it.

cards.definition_en: To continue doing something or maintain a certain level or pace.

examples.es: A pesar de que el grupo de corredores iba muy rápido, logré mantener el ritmo durante toda la carrera.
examples.en: Even though the group of runners was moving very fast, I managed to keep up throughout the entire race.

examples.es: Aunque el ritmo de la clase es muy rápido, trato de permanecer despierto con los temas nuevos.
examples.en: Although the pace of the class is very fast, I try to keep up with the new topics.

examples.es: Si no estudias todos los días, será imposible permanecer despierto con el nivel de exigencia del curso.
examples.en: If you do not study every day, it will be impossible to keep up with the demands of the course.

cards.example_sentence: Even though the group of runners was moving very fast, I managed to keep up throughout the entire race.
cards.example_es: A pesar de que el grupo de corredores iba muy rápido, logré mantener el ritmo durante toda la carrera.
cards.example_en: Even though the group of runners was moving very fast, I managed to keep up throughout the entire race.
cards.mnemonic_en: I keep up with coffee, staying despierto all night.

cards.definition_en: To leave hurriedly, especially after stealing something.

examples.es: Los ladrones aprovecharon la confusión para irse sin pagar con todas las joyas de la vitrina.
examples.en: The thieves took advantage of the confusion to make off with all the jewelry from the display case.

examples.es: El joven vio que el dueño estaba distraído y decidió irse sin pagar con el periódico que acababa de tomar.
examples.en: The young man saw that the owner was distracted and decided to make off with the newspaper he had just picked up.

examples.es: Tras robar la caja registradora, los sospechosos intentaron irse sin pagar en un coche que los esperaba afuera.
examples.en: After robbing the cash register, the suspects tried to make off in a car that was waiting for them outside.

cards.example_sentence: The thieves took advantage of the confusion to make off with all the jewelry from the display case.
cards.example_es: Los ladrones aprovecharon la confusión para irse sin pagar con todas las joyas de la vitrina.
cards.example_en: The thieves took advantage of the confusion to make off with all the jewelry from the display case.
cards.mnemonic_en: He makes off, like a thief making the bill vanish off the table.

cards.definition_en: To create something false or fictitious, such as a story, excuse, or invention.

examples.es: No deberías inventar excusas cada vez que llegas tarde al trabajo.
examples.en: You should not make up excuses every time you arrive late to work.

examples.es: Los niños suelen inventar historias fantásticas sobre dragones y castillos.
examples.en: Children often make up fantastic stories about dragons and castles.

examples.es: Tuve que inventar una razón convincente para explicar mi ausencia en la reunión.
examples.en: I had to make up a convincing reason to explain my absence from the meeting.

cards.example_sentence: You should not make up excuses every time you arrive late to work.
cards.example_es: No deberías inventar excusas cada vez que llegas tarde al trabajo.
cards.example_en: You should not make up excuses every time you arrive late to work.
cards.mnemonic_en: He makes up a new gadget, invent a device—just like inventar.

cards.definition_en: To go beyond or ignore something.

examples.es: Aunque el trauma fue difícil, finalmente logró pasar por encima de ese capítulo doloroso de su vida.
examples.en: Even though the trauma was difficult, she finally managed to move past that painful chapter of her life.

examples.es: Es difícil para ella pasar por encima de la traición de su mejor amigo.
examples.en: It is difficult for her to move past the betrayal of her best friend.

examples.es: Debemos pasar por encima de nuestras diferencias para trabajar juntos en el proyecto.
examples.en: We must move past our differences to work together on the project.

cards.example_sentence: Even though the trauma was difficult, she finally managed to move past that painful chapter of her life.
cards.example_es: Aunque el trauma fue difícil, finalmente logró pasar por encima de ese capítulo doloroso de su vida.
cards.example_en: Even though the trauma was difficult, she finally managed to move past that painful chapter of her life.
cards.mnemonic_en: Pass a rope over the fence to move past the obstacle.

cards.definition_en: to give or transmit something to someone else
collocations: pass on to the next generation

examples.es: Si hablas con el jefe, por favor transmítele el mensaje de que no podré asistir a la conferencia de prensa.
examples.en: If you speak with the boss, please pass on the message that I will not be able to attend the press conference.

examples.es: Cuando el profesor me envió las instrucciones del proyecto por correo electrónico, me pidió que se las pasara a mis compañeros de clase.
examples.en: When the professor emailed me the project instructions, he asked me to pass on the details to my classmates.

examples.es: Cuando el gerente me contó los cambios en el equipo, me pidió que le pasara la información a los demás empleados.
examples.en: When the manager told me about the team changes, he asked me to pass on the information to the other employees.

cards.example_sentence: If you speak with the boss, please pass on the message that I will not be able to attend the press conference.
cards.example_es: Si hablas con el jefe, por favor transmítele el mensaje de que no podré asistir a la conferencia de prensa.
cards.example_en: If you speak with the boss, please pass on the message that I will not be able to attend the press conference.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine saying 'pasa on' while you casually drop a comment.

cards.definition_en: To lift something from a surface or to collect it.
collocations: pick up where you left off

examples.es: Voy a recoger el paquete de la oficina.
examples.en: I will pick up the package from the office.

examples.es: Podrías recoger a los niños del colegio a las tres.
examples.en: You could pick up the kids from school at three.

examples.es: Necesito recoger los juguetes que están tirados por todo el suelo.
examples.en: I need to pick up the toys that are scattered all over the floor.

cards.example_sentence: I will pick up the package from the office.
cards.example_es: Voy a recoger el paquete de la oficina.
cards.example_en: I will pick up the package from the office.
cards.mnemonic_en: Pick up the record gear lying on the table

cards.definition_en: To identify or locate something precisely, often by determining its exact nature or details.

examples.es: El detective logró señalar con precisión la ubicación del cuerpo.
examples.en: The detective managed to pin down the location of the body.

examples.es: Es difícil señalar con precisión la causa exacta de este error técnico en el servidor.
examples.en: It is difficult to pin down the exact cause of this technical error on the server.

examples.es: No pude señalar con precisión qué fue lo que me hizo sentir tan incómodo durante la reunión.
examples.en: I could not pin down what exactly made me feel so uncomfortable during the meeting.

cards.example_sentence: The detective managed to pin down the location of the body.
cards.example_es: El detective logró señalar con precisión la ubicación del cuerpo.
cards.example_en: The detective managed to pin down the location of the body.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a pin marking the exact spot you point at

cards.definition_en: to propose or suggest something for consideration

examples.es: El abogado presentó formalmente la evidencia al jurado.
examples.en: The lawyer put forward the evidence to the jury.

examples.es: El comité decidió presentar formalmente una nueva propuesta para reducir los gastos.
examples.en: The committee decided to put forward a new proposal to cut costs.

examples.es: Varios científicos presentaron formalmente una teoría revolucionaria sobre el cambio climático.
examples.en: Several scientists put forward a revolutionary theory about climate change.

cards.example_sentence: The lawyer put forward the evidence to the jury.
cards.example_es: El abogado presentó formalmente la evidencia al jurado.
cards.example_en: The lawyer put forward the evidence to the jury.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a presenter putting a forward arrow on the screen

cards.definition_en: To dress oneself in something, or to cause something to be worn.

examples.es: Es una mañana fría, así que deberías ponerte un abrigo antes de salir.
examples.en: It is a cold morning, so you should put on a coat before going outside.

examples.es: Ella decidió ponerse sus zapatos favoritos para la entrevista de trabajo.
examples.en: She decided to put on her favorite shoes for the job interview.

examples.es: No olvides ponerte el cinturón de seguridad antes de que el coche empiece a moverse.
examples.en: Do not forget to put on your seatbelt before the car starts moving.

cards.example_sentence: It is a cold morning, so you should put on a coat before going outside.
cards.example_es: Es una mañana fría, así que deberías ponerte un abrigo antes de salir.
cards.example_en: It is a cold morning, so you should put on a coat before going outside.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture putting on a bright jacket that lights up, just like you encender a lamp.

cards.definition_en: To extinguish a fire, light, or similar source.

examples.es: Apaga la vela antes de irte.
examples.en: Put out the candle before you leave.

examples.es: Los bomberos tardaron horas en apagar el incendio forestal.
examples.en: The firefighters took hours to put out the forest fire.

examples.es: Por favor, apaga el cigarrillo antes de entrar en la gasolinera.
examples.en: Please put out your cigarette before entering the gas station.

cards.example_sentence: Put out the candle before you leave.
cards.example_es: Apaga la vela antes de irte.
cards.example_en: Put out the candle before you leave.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a fire and hand a pager that says STOP to put it out.

cards.definition_en: To discard or eject something.

examples.es: El guardia tuvo que expulsar al ladrón del edificio.
examples.en: The guard had to throw out the thief from the building.

examples.es: Mi madre decidió expulsar toda la ropa vieja que ya no usamos del armario.
examples.en: My mother decided to throw out all the old clothes we no longer use from the closet.

examples.es: El entrenador tuvo que expulsar al jugador del campo tras su comportamiento agresivo.
examples.en: The coach had to throw out the player from the field after his aggressive behavior.

cards.example_sentence: The guard had to throw out the thief from the building.
cards.example_es: El guardia tuvo que expulsar al ladrón del edificio.
cards.example_en: The guard had to throw out the thief from the building.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize throwing a trash can out the door, you expel it—like the Spanish expulsar.

cards.definition_en: to appear or occur unexpectedly

examples.es: Problemas inesperados suelen resurgir durante la fase final de un proyecto importante.
examples.en: Unexpected problems tend to crop up during the final phase of a major project.

examples.es: Aunque intentamos mantener la reunión bajo control, siempre resurgir temas polémicos que no estaban en la agenda.
examples.en: Although we tried to keep the meeting under control, controversial topics always crop up that were not on the agenda.

examples.es: Es frustrante cuando los errores técnicos resurgir justo después de que creíamos haberlos solucionado.
examples.en: It is frustrating when technical bugs crop up just after we thought we had fixed them.

cards.example_sentence: Unexpected problems tend to crop up during the final phase of a major project.
cards.example_es: Problemas inesperados suelen resurgir durante la fase final de un proyecto importante.
cards.example_en: Unexpected problems tend to crop up during the final phase of a major project.
cards.mnemonic_en: The resurging wheat fields suddenly crop up like green fireworks.

cards.definition_en: to become aware of something

examples.es: Tardé un buen rato en darme cuenta de que me estaban tomando el pelo con esa oferta tan sospechosa.
examples.en: It took me a while to cotton on that they were trying to trick me with such a suspicious offer.

examples.es: Al ver que todos sus compañeros se reían de sus chistes privados, finalmente me di cuenta de que me estaban excluyendo del grupo.
examples.en: Seeing my colleagues whispering and sharing private jokes, I finally began to cotton on to the fact that I was being excluded from the group.

examples.es: Tardó varios minutos en darse cuenta de que le estaban tomando el pelo.
examples.en: It took him several minutes to cotton on that they were pulling his leg.

cards.example_sentence: It took me a while to cotton on that they were trying to trick me with such a suspicious offer.
cards.example_es: Tardé un buen rato en darme cuenta de que me estaban tomando el pelo con esa oferta tan sospechosa.
cards.example_en: It took me a while to cotton on that they were trying to trick me with such a suspicious offer.
cards.mnemonic_en: Seeing the soft cotton pillow, I finally cotton on to the hidden clue.

cards.definition_en: to confront or deal with something directly, especially a problem or difficulty.

examples.es: Tengo que enfrentarme a mis miedos.
examples.en: I have to face up to my fears.

examples.es: Es hora de que enfrentes a la realidad sobre tu situación financiera.
examples.en: It is time you face up to the reality of your financial situation.

examples.es: Ella finalmente decidió enfrentarse a los errores que cometió en el pasado.
examples.en: She finally decided to face up to the mistakes she made in the past.

cards.example_sentence: I have to face up to my fears.
cards.example_es: Tengo que enfrentarme a mis miedos.
cards.example_en: I have to face up to my fears.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a face looking up, ready to enfrentarse a the challenge.

cards.definition_en: to write something quickly, especially brief notes.

examples.es: Durante la clase, me gusta tomar apuntes para no olvidar nada.
examples.en: During the class, I like to jot down notes so I don't forget anything.

examples.es: El periodista se apresuró a tomar apuntes de las declaraciones del testigo.
examples.en: The reporter hurried to jot down the witness's statements.

examples.es: Si tienes una idea brillante, es mejor tomar apuntes antes de que se te olvide.
examples.en: If you have a brilliant idea, it is better to jot down notes before you forget it.

cards.example_sentence: During the class, I like to jot down notes so I don't forget anything.
cards.example_es: Durante la clase, me gusta tomar apuntes para no olvidar nada.
cards.example_en: During the class, I like to jot down notes so I don't forget anything.
cards.mnemonic_en: I jot down notes while I take apuntes, picturing a tiny jot of paper.

cards.definition_en: To simplify something to its essential point or main idea.
collocations: In the end, it boils down to

examples.es: Al final, todo el conflicto se reduce a una cuestión de dinero.
examples.en: In the end, the entire conflict boils down to a matter of money.

examples.es: El éxito de esta receta tan compleja se reduce a la calidad de los ingredientes frescos.
examples.en: The success of this complex recipe boils down to the quality of the fresh ingredients.

examples.es: La decisión del juez se reduce a determinar si hubo intención de engañar al cliente.
examples.en: The judge's decision boils down to determining whether there was an intent to deceive the client.

cards.example_sentence: In the end, the entire conflict boils down to a matter of money.
cards.example_es: Al final, todo el conflicto se reduce a una cuestión de dinero.
cards.example_en: In the end, the entire conflict boils down to a matter of money.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a boiling bowl that shrinks down to a tiny cup.

cards.definition_en: To imagine or invent something, especially an idea or plan.

examples.es: El científico salió con una idea brillante para resolver el problema.
examples.en: The scientist managed to dream up a brilliant idea to solve the complex problem.

examples.es: Los guionistas salieron con una idea descabellada para el final de la serie.
examples.en: The screenwriters had to dream up a wild plot twist for the season finale.

examples.es: Ella salió con una idea creativa para decorar la oficina con poco presupuesto.
examples.en: She was able to dream up a creative way to decorate the office on a tight budget.

cards.example_sentence: The scientist managed to dream up a brilliant idea to solve the complex problem.
cards.example_es: El científico salió con una idea brillante para resolver el problema.
cards.example_en: The scientist managed to dream up a brilliant idea to solve the complex problem.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a dream balloon floating up, as if a Spanish sueño flies arriba with a new idea.

cards.definition_en: to leave or abandon something or someone

examples.es: Después de trabajar veinte años en la misma empresa, decidió renunciar a una oferta de ascenso para jubilarse antes.
examples.en: After working twenty years at the same company, he decided to walk away from a promotion offer to retire early.

examples.es: Es muy difícil renunciar a una relación tóxica cuando has invertido tanto tiempo y esfuerzo en ella.
examples.en: It is very difficult to walk away from a toxic relationship when you have invested so much time and effort into it.

examples.es: El jugador estrella decidió renunciar a un contrato millonario para jugar en el equipo de su ciudad natal.
examples.en: The star player decided to walk away from a multi-million dollar contract to play for his hometown team.

cards.example_sentence: After working twenty years at the same company, he decided to walk away from a promotion offer to retire early.
cards.example_es: Después de trabajar veinte años en la misma empresa, decidió renunciar a una oferta de ascenso para jubilarse antes.
cards.example_en: After working twenty years at the same company, he decided to walk away from a promotion offer to retire early.
cards.mnemonic_en: When you renounce, you walk away from the scene.

cards.definition_en: to become comfortable and stable in a place or situation, often by establishing a residence or routine
collocations: settle down in a new city

examples.es: Después de mudarse a la nueva ciudad, decidió asentarse en un pequeño apartamento cerca del parque.
examples.en: After moving to the new city, he decided to settle down in a small apartment near the park.

examples.es: Tras años de viajar por todo el mundo, finalmente quiso asentarse y formar una familia en su pueblo natal.
examples.en: After years of traveling around the world, he finally wanted to settle down and start a family in his hometown.

examples.es: Después de años de vivir de fiesta en fiesta, finalmente decidió sentar cabeza y buscar un trabajo estable.
examples.en: After years of partying every weekend, he finally decided to settle down and find a steady job.

cards.example_sentence: After moving to the new city, he decided to settle down in a small apartment near the park.
cards.example_es: Después de mudarse a la nueva ciudad, decidió asentarse en un pequeño apartamento cerca del parque.
cards.example_en: After moving to the new city, he decided to settle down in a small apartment near the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Settle down like finding a seat (asiento) and sitting.

cards.definition_en: to originate or develop from something

examples.es: La inestabilidad económica de la región deriva de las recientes políticas comerciales.
examples.en: The region's economic instability stems from recent trade policies.

examples.es: El éxito de este proyecto deriva de una planificación cuidadosa y una ejecución impecable.
examples.en: The success of this project stems from careful planning and flawless execution.

examples.es: La desconfianza que siente hacia los extraños deriva de una mala experiencia que tuvo en el pasado.
examples.en: His distrust of strangers stems from a bad experience he had in the past.

cards.example_sentence: The region's economic instability stems from recent trade policies.
cards.example_es: La inestabilidad económica de la región deriva de las recientes políticas comerciales.
cards.example_en: The region's economic instability stems from recent trade policies.
cards.mnemonic_en: The tree's stem from the earth derives, just like a river (deriva) flows out.

cards.definition_en: to assume a higher level of responsibility or authority

examples.es: Cuando el capitán cayó enfermo, tuvo que asumir el mando del barco.
examples.en: When the captain fell ill, he had to step up and take command of the ship.

examples.es: El equipo perdía por tres goles, así que el capitán decidió asumir el mando en la segunda parte.
examples.en: The team was down by three goals, so the captain decided to step up and lead the comeback in the second half.

examples.es: Ante la crisis financiera, el vicepresidente tuvo que asumir el mando de la empresa.
examples.en: Faced with the financial crisis, the vice president had to step up to manage the company's operations.

cards.example_sentence: When the captain fell ill, he had to step up and take command of the ship.
cards.example_es: Cuando el capitán cayó enfermo, tuvo que asumir el mando del barco.
cards.example_en: When the captain fell ill, he had to step up and take command of the ship.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine stepping up a ladder to asume the command.

cards.definition_en: To gradually remove or eliminate unwanted or undesirable items or people.

examples.es: La empresa decidió suprimir gradualmente los puestos de trabajo innecesarios.
examples.en: The company decided to weed out unnecessary positions.

examples.es: El entrenador tuvo que suprimir gradualmente a los jugadores que no cumplían con el nivel físico requerido.
examples.en: The coach had to weed out the players who did not meet the required fitness level.

examples.es: El editor dedicó horas a suprimir gradualmente los errores gramaticales del manuscrito antes de enviarlo a imprenta.
examples.en: The editor spent hours trying to weed out grammatical errors from the manuscript before sending it to the printer.

cards.example_sentence: The company decided to weed out unnecessary positions.
cards.example_es: La empresa decidió suprimir gradualmente los puestos de trabajo innecesarios.
cards.example_en: The company decided to weed out unnecessary positions.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine pulling weeds out slowly, like you suppress (suprimir) them one by one.

cards.definition_en: to separate something into smaller parts or components

examples.es: El analista financiero tuvo que desmenuzar en varias categorías los gastos anuales de la empresa para identificar dónde se estaba perdiendo dinero.
examples.en: The financial analyst had to break down into several categories the company's annual expenses to identify where money was being lost.

examples.es: El profesor decidió desmenuzar en conceptos sencillos la compleja teoría física para que sus alumnos pudieran entenderla mejor.
examples.en: The professor decided to break down into simple concepts the complex physical theory so that his students could understand it better.

examples.es: El chef tuvo que desmenuzar en trozos minúsculos la carne dura para que el estofado quedara tierno.
examples.en: The chef had to break down into tiny pieces the tough meat so that the stew would be tender.

cards.example_sentence: The financial analyst had to break down into several categories the company's annual expenses to identify where money was being lost.
cards.example_es: El analista financiero tuvo que desmenuzar en varias categorías los gastos anuales de la empresa para identificar dónde se estaba perdiendo dinero.
cards.example_en: The financial analyst had to break down into several categories the company's annual expenses to identify where money was being lost.
cards.mnemonic_en: Break the cake into crumbs, just like desmenuzar.

cards.definition_en: to survive or recover from a difficult situation

examples.es: Con mucho trabajo, logramos superar con esfuerzo la crisis económica.
examples.en: With a lot of effort, we managed to pull through the economic crisis.

examples.es: El pronóstico médico era desalentador, pero tras semanas de cuidados intensivos, el enfermo finalmente logró superar con esfuerzo la enfermedad.
examples.en: The medical prognosis was grim, but after weeks of intensive care, the patient finally managed to pull through.

examples.es: La empresa estaba al borde de la quiebra, pero gracias a los nuevos inversores pudieron superar con esfuerzo los meses más difíciles.
examples.en: The company was on the verge of bankruptcy, but thanks to the new investors they were able to pull through the most difficult months.

cards.example_sentence: With a lot of effort, we managed to pull through the economic crisis.
cards.example_es: Con mucho trabajo, logramos superar con esfuerzo la crisis económica.
cards.example_en: With a lot of effort, we managed to pull through the economic crisis.
cards.mnemonic_en: Pull the rope through, like polishing (pulir) your way past a tough wall.

cards.definition_en: to withdraw from a commitment or fail to follow through with a plan

examples.es: No me presenté a la reunión porque me sentía enfermo.
examples.en: I had to back out of the meeting because I was feeling sick.

examples.es: Prometió ayudarme con la mudanza, pero decidió no presentarse a último momento.
examples.en: He promised to help me move, but he decided to back out of the agreement at the last minute.

examples.es: El inversor decidió no presentarse al proyecto tras revisar los estados financieros.
examples.en: The investor decided to back out of the project after reviewing the financial statements.

cards.example_sentence: I had to back out of the meeting because I was feeling sick.
cards.example_es: No me presenté a la reunión porque me sentía enfermo.
cards.example_en: I had to back out of the meeting because I was feeling sick.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture yourself backing out of a door, shouting No presentarse as you vanish before anyone sees you.

cards.definition_en: To draw a line through text or a mark to indicate it should be deleted or ignored.

examples.es: Por favor, tacha los errores en el documento.
examples.en: Please cross out the mistakes in the document.

examples.es: El profesor pidió que tacháramos los nombres de los estudiantes que no asistieron a clase.
examples.en: The teacher asked us to cross out the names of the students who did not attend class.

examples.es: Tacha la respuesta incorrecta en el examen antes de entregar tu hoja.
examples.en: Cross out the incorrect answer on the test before you hand in your paper.

cards.example_sentence: Please cross out the mistakes in the document.
cards.example_es: Por favor, tacha los errores en el documento.
cards.example_en: Please cross out the mistakes in the document.
cards.mnemonic_en: Cross out a mistake by drawing a big T over the char.

cards.definition_en: to accompany someone or something

examples.es: El vino tinto suele ir acompañado de carnes rojas en las cenas elegantes.
examples.en: Red wine tends to go together with red meat at elegant dinners.

examples.es: El aumento de la inflación suele ir acompañado de una caída en el poder adquisitivo de las familias.
examples.en: The rise in inflation tends to go together with a drop in the purchasing power of families.

examples.es: Ese vestido elegante debe ir acompañado de los accesorios adecuados para destacar.
examples.en: That elegant dress must go together with the right accessories to stand out.

cards.example_sentence: Red wine tends to go together with red meat at elegant dinners.
cards.example_es: El vino tinto suele ir acompañado de carnes rojas en las cenas elegantes.
cards.example_en: Red wine tends to go together with red meat at elegant dinners.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a goat (go) walking together with a companion named Acompañado.

cards.definition_en: to rely again on something or someone for support or help

examples.es: Cuando no sé qué hacer, siempre recaigo en mis viejos hábitos.
examples.en: When I don't know what to do, I always fall back on my old habits.

examples.es: Si pierdo mi empleo, tendré que recaer en mis ahorros para pagar el alquiler.
examples.en: If I lose my job, I will have to fall back on my savings to pay the rent.

examples.es: El equipo no tenía un plan B y tuvo que recaer en su estrategia defensiva inicial.
examples.en: The team had no plan B and had to fall back on their initial defensive strategy.

cards.example_sentence: When I don't know what to do, I always fall back on my old habits.
cards.example_es: Cuando no sé qué hacer, siempre recaigo en mis viejos hábitos.
cards.example_en: When I don't know what to do, I always fall back on my old habits.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a falling rope you re‑cair onto when you need backup.

cards.definition_en: To continue doing something with determination despite obstacles.

examples.es: A pesar de la falta de presupuesto, la empresa decidió insistir en desarrollar el nuevo software.
examples.en: Despite the lack of budget, the company decided to press ahead with developing the new software.

examples.es: Aunque el clima empeoró, los escaladores decidieron insistir en subir hasta la cima de la montaña.
examples.en: Even though the weather worsened, the climbers decided to press ahead with reaching the mountain summit.

examples.es: El director no escuchó las advertencias y prefirió insistir en implementar los cambios radicales en la oficina.
examples.en: The manager ignored the warnings and chose to press ahead with implementing the radical changes in the office.

cards.example_sentence: Despite the lack of budget, the company decided to press ahead with developing the new software.
cards.example_es: A pesar de la falta de presupuesto, la empresa decidió insistir en desarrollar el nuevo software.
cards.example_en: Despite the lack of budget, the company decided to press ahead with developing the new software.
cards.mnemonic_en: Press a head like a stubborn insist from Spanish insistir.

title: Discussion and Debate
description: Expressions for giving opinions, agreeing, disagreeing, clarifying, and structuring advanced spoken English.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating total agreement with something.

examples.es: Estoy totalmente de acuerdo con tu propuesta para mejorar el horario de trabajo.
examples.en: I completely agree with your proposal to improve the work schedule.

examples.es: Después de escuchar los argumentos del equipo, estoy totalmente de acuerdo con su conclusión.
examples.en: After hearing the team's arguments, I completely agree with their conclusion.

examples.es: Siendo honesto, estoy totalmente de acuerdo con la decisión que tomó el director hoy.
examples.en: To be honest, I completely agree with the decision the director made today.

cards.example_sentence: I completely agree with your proposal to improve the work schedule.
cards.example_es: Estoy totalmente de acuerdo con tu propuesta para mejorar el horario de trabajo.
cards.example_en: I completely agree with your proposal to improve the work schedule.
cards.mnemonic_en: I completely nod, because agree sounds like Spanish acuerdo.

cards.spanish: Estoy de acuerdo hasta cierto punto
cards.definition_en: To express partial agreement with something.
collocations: I agree to some extent with you
I agree to some extent, however
I agree to some extent on that point

examples.es: Estoy de acuerdo hasta cierto punto con tu propuesta de cambiar el horario de la reunión.
examples.en: I agree to some extent with your proposal to change the meeting time.

examples.es: Aunque el plan parece eficiente, estoy de acuerdo hasta cierto punto sobre los riesgos que mencionas.
examples.en: Although the plan seems efficient, I agree to some extent about the risks you mention.

examples.es: Sobre la nueva política de la empresa, estoy de acuerdo hasta cierto punto, pero faltan detalles.
examples.en: Regarding the new company policy, I agree to some extent, but details are missing.

cards.example_sentence: I agree to some extent with your proposal to change the meeting time.
cards.example_es: Estoy de acuerdo hasta cierto punto con tu propuesta de cambiar el horario de la reunión.
cards.example_en: I agree to some extent with your proposal to change the meeting time.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize an agrarian handshake extending just enough to pitch a tiny tent at a point.

cards.english: I do not see it that way
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker does not share that perspective.
main_translations_es: No lo percibo de esa manera
collocations: I do not see it that way, but...
I do not see it that way at all
I do not see it that way, however
synonyms_en: That's not how I view it

examples.es: Aunque creas que el proyecto es un éxito, no lo veo así después de revisar los números.
examples.en: Even though you think the project is a success, I do not see it that way after reviewing the numbers.

examples.es: Dices que deberíamos vender la empresa ahora, pero no lo veo así porque el mercado va a mejorar pronto.
examples.en: You say we should sell the company now, but I do not see it that way because the market is going to improve soon.

examples.es: Él insiste en que el cambio es necesario, pero no lo veo así dado el impacto negativo en el personal.
examples.en: He insists that the change is necessary, but I do not see it that way given the negative impact on the staff.

cards.example_sentence: Even though you think the project is a success, I do not see it that way after reviewing the numbers.
cards.example_es: Aunque creas que el proyecto es un éxito, no lo veo así después de revisar los números.
cards.example_en: Even though you think the project is a success, I do not see it that way after reviewing the numbers.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a NO sign covering a low V while a sea says as I

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that someone is not fully persuaded.
collocations: I am not entirely convinced that…
We are not entirely convinced about…

examples.es: No estoy del todo convencido de que esa sea la mejor solución.
examples.en: I am not entirely convinced that this is the best solution.

examples.es: Aunque el plan parece lógico, no estoy del todo convencido de que podamos terminar a tiempo.
examples.en: Although the plan seems logical, I am not entirely convinced that we can finish on time.

examples.es: Escuché tu propuesta sobre el nuevo presupuesto, pero no estoy del todo convencido de que sea rentable.
examples.en: I heard your proposal about the new budget, but I am not entirely convinced that it is profitable.

cards.example_sentence: I am not entirely convinced that this is the best solution.
cards.example_es: No estoy del todo convencido de que esa sea la mejor solución.
cards.example_en: I am not entirely convinced that this is the best solution.
cards.mnemonic_en: I stare at a half‑filled glass, not entirely convinced it's full.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of saying that you disagree.
collocations: I beg to differ with you
I beg to differ on that point

examples.es: Discrepo con su opinión sobre el tema.
examples.en: I beg to differ with his opinion on the matter.

examples.es: Aunque todos apoyan el plan, yo discrepo totalmente.
examples.en: Although everyone supports the plan, I beg to differ entirely.

examples.es: Si me permite, discrepo respecto a las cifras que acaba de presentar.
examples.en: If you will allow me, I beg to differ regarding the figures you just presented.

cards.example_sentence: I beg to differ with his opinion on the matter.
cards.example_es: Discrepo con su opinión sobre el tema.
cards.example_en: I beg to differ with his opinion on the matter.
cards.mnemonic_en: I beg to differ, thinking "this crepe, oh!" isn’t the same.

cards.spanish: Eso no refleja toda la realidad
cards.english: That does not tell the whole story
cards.definition_en: It means that something fails to convey the complete truth or full situation.
main_translations_es: Eso no muestra toda la realidad
Eso no cuenta toda la historia
Eso no refleja todo el panorama
collocations: The numbers do not tell the whole story
One example does not tell the whole story
Statistics often do not tell the whole story
A single incident does not tell the whole story

examples.es: Aunque las cifras de ventas son positivas, eso no refleja toda la realidad sobre la crisis de la empresa.
examples.en: Although the sales figures are positive, that does not tell the whole story about the company's crisis.

examples.es: Solo estás mirando los beneficios económicos, pero eso no refleja toda la realidad de los daños ambientales.
examples.en: You are only looking at the economic benefits, but that does not tell the whole story of the environmental damage.

examples.es: Su informe se centra únicamente en los éxitos del proyecto; eso no refleja toda la realidad de los problemas que enfrentamos.
examples.en: His report focuses only on the project's successes; that does not tell the whole story of the problems we faced.

cards.example_sentence: Although the sales figures are positive, that does not tell the whole story about the company's crisis.
cards.example_es: Aunque las cifras de ventas son positivas, eso no refleja toda la realidad sobre la crisis de la empresa.
cards.example_en: Although the sales figures are positive, that does not tell the whole story about the company's crisis.
cards.mnemonic_en: That does not tell the whole story, like a real idea (realidad) with missing pages.
cloze_distractors_en: That is a very popular opinion
It is a completely different topic

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something is being considered according to one's own point of view.
collocations: From my perspective, the situation is clear
From my perspective, we should proceed
From my perspective, the results are promising

examples.es: Desde mi punto de vista, la educación debería ser gratuita para todos.
examples.en: From my perspective, education should be free for everyone.

examples.es: Desde mi punto de vista, el proyecto actual no es viable debido a la falta de presupuesto.
examples.en: From my perspective, the current project is not viable due to the lack of budget.

examples.es: Desde mi punto de vista, trabajar desde casa mejora significativamente la productividad de los empleados.
examples.en: From my perspective, working from home significantly improves employee productivity.

cards.example_sentence: From my perspective, education should be free for everyone.
cards.example_es: Desde mi punto de vista, la educación debería ser gratuita para todos.
cards.example_en: From my perspective, education should be free for everyone.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a camera called From my pointing at a vista, reminding you of desde mi punto de vista.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce one's personal opinion or perspective.
collocations: as I see it, the problem is
as I see it, we should
as I see it, that's the case

examples.es: Tal como yo lo veo, la situación es insostenible.
examples.en: As I see it, the situation is unsustainable.

examples.es: Tal como yo lo veo, deberíamos invertir más dinero en educación.
examples.en: As I see it, we should invest more money in education.

examples.es: Tal como yo lo veo, este proyecto no estará terminado a tiempo.
examples.en: As I see it, this project will not be finished on time.

cards.example_sentence: As I see it, the situation is unsustainable.
cards.example_es: Tal como yo lo veo, la situación es insostenible.
cards.example_en: As I see it, the situation is unsustainable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a camera: 'As I see it'—just like saying 'tal como yo lo veo'.

cards.definition_en: It indicates that the speaker is stating something based on their current understanding.
collocations: as far as I understand it
as far as I understand the situation
as far as I understand from the report

examples.es: Por lo que entiendo, el proyecto se completará a tiempo.
examples.en: As far as I understand, the project will be completed on time.

examples.es: Por lo que entiendo, la nueva política de la empresa entrará en vigor el próximo lunes.
examples.en: As far as I understand, the new company policy will go into effect next Monday.

examples.es: Por lo que entiendo, todavía no han tomado una decisión sobre quién liderará el equipo de ventas.
examples.en: As far as I understand, they have not yet made a decision about who will lead the sales team.

cards.example_sentence: As far as I understand, the project will be completed on time.
cards.example_es: Por lo que entiendo, el proyecto se completará a tiempo.
cards.example_en: As far as I understand, the project will be completed on time.
cards.mnemonic_en: From far away, I understand the 'por' door opens.

cards.definition_en: It means that the speaker is stating something based on their current knowledge, but may not be certain.
collocations: as far as I know, it's true
as far as I know, there are no issues
as far as I know, that's the case
synonyms_en: to the best of my knowledge

examples.es: Hasta donde sé, la reunión será mañana a las diez.
examples.en: As far as I know, the meeting will be tomorrow at ten.

examples.es: Hasta donde sé, el proyecto todavía no ha sido aprobado por el director.
examples.en: As far as I know, the project has not been approved by the director yet.

examples.es: Hasta donde sé, ellos se mudarán a otra ciudad el próximo mes.
examples.en: As far as I know, they are moving to another city next month.

cards.example_sentence: As far as I know, the meeting will be tomorrow at ten.
cards.example_es: Hasta donde sé, la reunión será mañana a las diez.
cards.example_en: As far as I know, the meeting will be tomorrow at ten.
cards.mnemonic_en: I see a far horizon and whisper hasta while I know the answer.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a statement that qualifies or contrasts with what was previously said.
collocations: That said, the results are inconclusive

examples.es: Dicho esto, creo que deberíamos continuar con el plan original.
examples.en: That said, I think we should proceed with the original plan.

examples.es: El informe presenta datos muy positivos; dicho esto, debemos ser cautelosos con las proyecciones futuras.
examples.en: The report presents very positive data; that said, we must be cautious with future projections.

examples.es: Entiendo que el presupuesto es limitado, pero dicho esto, no podemos comprometer la calidad del proyecto.
examples.en: I understand that the budget is limited, but that said, we cannot compromise the quality of the project.

cards.example_sentence: That said, I think we should proceed with the original plan.
cards.example_es: Dicho esto, creo que deberíamos continuar con el plan original.
cards.example_en: That said, I think we should proceed with the original plan.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hat shouting 'That said' as you read the Spanish phrase dicho esto.

cards.definition_en: Indicates that two or more events occur simultaneously.

examples.es: Al mismo tiempo, debemos considerar los beneficios y los riesgos de este nuevo proyecto.
examples.en: At the same time, we must consider the benefits and the risks of this new project.

examples.es: Ella estaba estudiando para su examen y, al mismo tiempo, escuchaba música clásica.
examples.en: She was studying for her exam and, at the same time, she was listening to classical music.

examples.es: El equipo quiere aumentar la producción y, al mismo tiempo, reducir los costos operativos.
examples.en: The team wants to increase production and, at the same time, reduce operating costs.

cards.example_sentence: At the same time, we must consider the benefits and the risks of this new project.
cards.example_es: Al mismo tiempo, debemos considerar los beneficios y los riesgos de este nuevo proyecto.
cards.example_en: At the same time, we must consider the benefits and the risks of this new project.
cards.mnemonic_en: Sam and Tim are at the same time.

cards.definition_en: In a way that does not diminish or lessen the importance of something.

examples.es: Sin restarle importancia a los problemas menores, debemos enfocarnos en la solución principal.
examples.en: Without downplaying the minor issues, we must focus on the main solution.

examples.es: Sin restarle importancia a los logros del año pasado, debemos centrarnos en los nuevos objetivos de ventas.
examples.en: Without downplaying the achievements of last year, we must focus on the new sales targets.

examples.es: Sin restarle importancia a los riesgos financieros, el proyecto tiene un potencial de crecimiento enorme.
examples.en: Without downplaying the financial risks, the project has enormous growth potential.

cards.example_sentence: Without downplaying the minor issues, we must focus on the main solution.
cards.example_es: Sin restarle importancia a los problemas menores, debemos enfocarnos en la solución principal.
cards.example_en: Without downplaying the minor issues, we must focus on the main solution.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sin that refuses to downplay its importance.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to restate something in a different way.

examples.es: El proyecto no está generando beneficios; en otras palabras, estamos perdiendo dinero cada mes.
examples.en: The project is not generating any profit; in other words, we are losing money every month.

examples.es: No quiere trabajar horas extra; en otras palabras, prefiere irse a casa temprano.
examples.en: He does not want to work overtime; in other words, he prefers to go home early.

examples.es: El informe es demasiado largo y confuso; en otras palabras, necesitamos resumirlo para la presentación.
examples.en: The report is too long and confusing; in other words, we need to summarize it for the presentation.

cards.example_sentence: The project is not generating any profit; in other words, we are losing money every month.
cards.example_es: El proyecto no está generando beneficios; en otras palabras, estamos perdiendo dinero cada mes.
cards.example_en: The project is not generating any profit; in other words, we are losing money every month.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sign reading EN OTRAS PALABRAS and you whisper in other words to translate it.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a restatement of something in other words.

examples.es: Dicho de otro modo, la situación es insostenible.
examples.en: Put differently, the situation is unsustainable.

examples.es: Dicho de otro modo, no podemos permitirnos este gasto extra este mes.
examples.en: Put differently, we cannot afford this extra expense this month.

examples.es: Dicho de otro modo, el proyecto fracasará si no cambiamos nuestra estrategia ahora.
examples.en: Put differently, the project will fail if we do not change our strategy now.

cards.example_sentence: Put differently, the situation is unsustainable.
cards.example_es: Dicho de otro modo, la situación es insostenible.
cards.example_en: Put differently, the situation is unsustainable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Put a pot (put) on a different shelf labeled 'diferente'.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a more exact statement or clarification.
collocations: To be more precise, the answer is ...
To be more precise, we should note that ...

examples.es: Para ser más preciso, el presupuesto inicial debe aumentar un diez por ciento.
examples.en: To be more precise, the initial budget needs to increase by ten percent.

examples.es: Para ser más preciso, el informe indica que las ventas cayeron durante el verano.
examples.en: To be more precise, the report indicates that sales dropped during the summer.

examples.es: Para ser más preciso, el evento comenzará a las ocho de la mañana en punto.
examples.en: To be more precise, the event will start at eight o'clock sharp in the morning.

cards.example_sentence: To be more precise, the initial budget needs to increase by ten percent.
cards.example_es: Para ser más preciso, el presupuesto inicial debe aumentar un diez por ciento.
cards.example_en: To be more precise, the initial budget needs to increase by ten percent.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a precise (preciso) ruler announcing, to be more precise, I measure twice.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of saying that you will restate something using different words.
collocations: Let me rephrase that for clarity
Let me rephrase that in simpler terms
Let me rephrase that to be sure
Let me rephrase that another way

examples.es: Permíteme reformularlo, ya que creo que mi explicación anterior fue un poco confusa.
examples.en: Let me rephrase that, as I think my previous explanation was a bit confusing.

examples.es: Si no entiendes mi punto de vista, permíteme reformularlo con otras palabras.
examples.en: If you do not understand my point of view, let me rephrase that using different words.

examples.es: No quiero que haya malentendidos, así que permíteme reformularlo para ser más preciso.
examples.en: I do not want there to be any misunderstandings, so let me rephrase that to be more precise.

cards.example_sentence: Let me rephrase that, as I think my previous explanation was a bit confusing.
cards.example_es: Permíteme reformularlo, ya que creo que mi explicación anterior fue un poco confusa.
cards.example_en: Let me rephrase that, as I think my previous explanation was a bit confusing.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine me saying let me rephrase that as I reform a broken sign, linking rephrase to reformar

cards.english: What I am trying to say is
cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce or clarify what the speaker intends to convey.
collocations: What I am trying to say is that...
What I am trying to say is, in short,...
What I am trying to say is, basically,...

examples.es: Lo que intento decir es que el presupuesto actual no cubre todos los gastos del proyecto.
examples.en: What I am trying to say is that the current budget does not cover all the project expenses.

examples.es: Lo que intento decir es que deberíamos posponer la reunión hasta que todos hayan leído el informe.
examples.en: What I am trying to say is that we should postpone the meeting until everyone has read the report.

examples.es: Lo que intento decir es que tu propuesta es interesante, pero no es viable en este momento.
examples.en: What I am trying to say is that your proposal is interesting, but it is not viable at this moment.

cards.example_sentence: What I am trying to say is that the current budget does not cover all the project expenses.
cards.example_es: Lo que intento decir es que el presupuesto actual no cubre todos los gastos del proyecto.
cards.example_en: What I am trying to say is that the current budget does not cover all the project expenses.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an intent speaker shouting what I am trying to say is

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a detailed analysis or explanation of something.
collocations: If we break it down further
If we break it down step by step
If we break it down, we can see
If we break it down, the problem becomes clear
synonyms_en: If we look at it closely

examples.es: Si lo analizamos detenidamente, encontraremos la causa del problema.
examples.en: If we break it down carefully, we will find the cause of the problem.

examples.es: Si lo analizamos paso a paso, entenderemos mejor cómo funciona este nuevo software.
examples.en: If we break it down step by step, we will better understand how this new software works.

examples.es: Si lo analizamos en términos de presupuesto, veremos que el proyecto no es viable.
examples.en: If we break it down in terms of budget, we will see that the project is not viable.

cards.example_sentence: If we break it down carefully, we will find the cause of the problem.
cards.example_es: Si lo analizamos detenidamente, encontraremos la causa del problema.
cards.example_en: If we break it down carefully, we will find the cause of the problem.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a scientist saying If we break it down while whispering si lo analizamos as he dissects a puzzle.

cards.definition_en: A statement acknowledging that someone has made a valid or reasonable argument.
collocations: I have to admit that's a fair point.

examples.es: Ese es un buen punto, deberíamos considerarlo antes de decidir.
examples.en: That is a fair point, we should consider it before deciding.

examples.es: Aunque no estoy de acuerdo con tu conclusión, admito que ese es un buen punto.
examples.en: Although I disagree with your conclusion, I admit that is a fair point.

examples.es: Si propones reducir el presupuesto, ese es un buen punto para empezar la negociación.
examples.en: If you propose cutting the budget, that is a fair point to start the negotiation.

cards.example_sentence: That is a fair point, we should consider it before deciding.
cards.example_es: Ese es un buen punto, deberíamos considerarlo antes de decidir.
cards.example_en: That is a fair point, we should consider it before deciding.
cards.mnemonic_en: At the fair the judge stamps a badge that reads Buen Point

cards.definition_en: It means something deserves attention or consideration.

examples.es: El presupuesto para el nuevo proyecto es muy alto, pero eso merece atención antes de rechazarlo.
examples.en: The budget for the new project is quite high, but that is worth considering before we turn it down.

examples.es: Tu propuesta de cambiar el horario de trabajo es interesante y eso merece atención en la próxima reunión.
examples.en: Your proposal to change the work schedule is interesting and that is worth considering at the next meeting.

examples.es: Aunque la idea parece arriesgada, eso merece atención por los beneficios a largo plazo que ofrece.
examples.en: Although the idea seems risky, that is worth considering because of the long-term benefits it offers.

cards.example_sentence: The budget for the new project is quite high, but that is worth considering before we turn it down.
cards.example_es: El presupuesto para el nuevo proyecto es muy alto, pero eso merece atención antes de rechazarlo.
