cards.example_en: The budget for the new project is quite high, but that is worth considering before we turn it down.
cards.mnemonic_en: A gold coin worth checking, because it merece attention.

cards.english: I had not thought of that
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that one had not considered something before.
collocations: I had not thought of that before
I had not thought of that possibility
I had not thought of that angle

examples.es: Si trasladamos la reunión a la tarde para que todos puedan asistir, no había pensado en eso, pero es una excelente sugerencia.
examples.en: If we move the meeting to the afternoon so everyone can attend, I had not thought of that, but it is an excellent suggestion.

examples.es: Es una perspectiva interesante; sinceramente, no había pensado en eso hasta ahora.
examples.en: That is an interesting perspective; honestly, I had not thought of that until now.

examples.es: Considerar el uso de energía solar para reducir los costos operativos es una idea brillante; la verdad es que no había pensado en eso.
examples.en: Considering solar power to cut our operating costs is a brilliant idea; honestly, I had not thought of that.

cards.example_sentence: If we move the meeting to the afternoon so everyone can attend, I had not thought of that, but it is an excellent suggestion.
cards.example_es: Si trasladamos la reunión a la tarde para que todos puedan asistir, no había pensado en eso, pero es una excelente sugerencia.
cards.example_en: If we move the meeting to the afternoon so everyone can attend, I had not thought of that, but it is an excellent suggestion.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bee saying no while I forget the idea

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce a statement that contrasts with or qualifies what was previously said.
collocations: Having said that, I think we should proceed.
Having said that, we need to consider the costs.
Having said that, it's not a guarantee.
Having said that, let's move on.

examples.es: El nuevo software es increíblemente rápido, sin embargo, la interfaz de usuario es muy difícil de navegar.
examples.en: The new software is incredibly fast; having said that, the user interface is very difficult to navigate.

examples.es: El equipo técnico es muy eficiente; sin embargo, el presupuesto es insuficiente para terminar la obra.
examples.en: The technical team is very efficient; having said that, the budget is insufficient to finish the work.

examples.es: Nuestras ventas han subido este trimestre; sin embargo, debemos reducir los gastos operativos para mantener la rentabilidad.
examples.en: Our sales have increased this quarter; having said that, we must reduce operating expenses to maintain profitability.

cards.example_sentence: The new software is incredibly fast; having said that, the user interface is very difficult to navigate.
cards.example_es: El nuevo software es increíblemente rápido, sin embargo, la interfaz de usuario es muy difícil de navegar.
cards.example_en: The new software is incredibly fast; having said that, the user interface is very difficult to navigate.
cards.mnemonic_en: Having said that, picture a sin without an embargo, a forbidden gate swinging open.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a statement that contrasts with or qualifies what has just been said.

examples.es: Aun así, decidió ir al concierto a pesar de la lluvia.
examples.en: Even so, he decided to go to the concert despite the rain.

examples.es: El proyecto tiene muchos errores, pero aun así, el jefe quiere presentarlo mañana.
examples.en: The project has many errors, but even so, the boss wants to present it tomorrow.

examples.es: Es una película muy larga y lenta; aun así, me mantuvo atento hasta el final.
examples.en: It is a very long and slow movie; even so, it kept me attentive until the end.

cards.example_sentence: Even so, he decided to go to the concert despite the rain.
cards.example_es: Aun así, decidió ir al concierto a pesar de la lluvia.
cards.example_en: Even so, he decided to go to the concert despite the rain.
cards.mnemonic_en: Even so, picture a lawn (awn) still standing after rain, just like aun.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something is true in spite of a previously mentioned fact.
collocations: Despite that, the results were positive

examples.es: Pese a ello, decidió continuar con el proyecto.
examples.en: Despite that, he decided to continue with the project.

examples.es: El equipo perdió el primer partido; pese a ello, siguen siendo los favoritos para ganar el campeonato.
examples.en: The team lost the first game; despite that, they remain the favorites to win the championship.

examples.es: La propuesta fue rechazada por el comité; pese a ello, ella planea presentarla de nuevo el próximo mes.
examples.en: The proposal was rejected by the committee; despite that, she plans to present it again next month.

cards.example_sentence: Despite that, he decided to continue with the project.
cards.example_es: Pese a ello, decidió continuar con el proyecto.
cards.example_en: Despite that, he decided to continue with the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture peas rolling past the word that, still moving despite that

cards.definition_en: Used to indicate that something is not always the case or not required.

examples.es: No necesariamente tienes que trabajar los fines de semana.
examples.en: You don't necessarily have to work on weekends.

examples.es: Que sea un experto en tecnología no necesariamente lo convierte en un buen profesor.
examples.en: Being an expert in technology does not necessarily make him a good teacher.

examples.es: Aunque el proyecto sea popular, no necesariamente significa que sea rentable.
examples.en: Even if the project is popular, it does not necessarily mean it is profitable.

cards.example_sentence: You don't necessarily have to work on weekends.
cards.example_es: No necesariamente tienes que trabajar los fines de semana.
cards.example_en: You don't necessarily have to work on weekends.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a loose knot that isn’t necessarily tied, as if the Spanish 'no' refuses to be fixed.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that a conclusion cannot be assumed from the given premise.
main_translations_es: No se concluye a partir de eso

examples.es: El hecho de que haya mucha demanda de coches eléctricos no implica que las ventas de gasolina vayan a desaparecer; no se sigue de eso.
examples.en: Just because there is high demand for electric cars, it does not mean gasoline sales will vanish; that does not necessarily follow.

examples.es: El hecho de que el equipo haya ganado el último partido no significa que vayan a ganar el campeonato; no se sigue de eso.
examples.en: Just because the team won the last game, it does not mean they will win the championship; that does not necessarily follow.

examples.es: El hecho de que el candidato tenga mucha experiencia no garantiza que sea el mejor para el puesto; eso no se sigue de eso.
examples.en: Just because the candidate has a lot of experience, it does not guarantee they are the best fit for the job; that does not necessarily follow.

cards.example_sentence: Just because there is high demand for electric cars, it does not mean gasoline sales will vanish; that does not necessarily follow.
cards.example_es: El hecho de que haya mucha demanda de coches eléctricos no implica que las ventas de gasolina vayan a desaparecer; no se sigue de eso.
cards.example_en: Just because there is high demand for electric cars, it does not mean gasoline sales will vanish; that does not necessarily follow.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sign reading “No se” that refuses to follow the line.

cards.definition_en: It indicates that the outcome or answer varies depending on certain conditions.

examples.es: ¿Crees que iremos a la playa este fin de semana? Eso depende del pronóstico del tiempo.
examples.en: Do you think we will go to the beach this weekend? That depends on the weather forecast.

examples.es: ¿Vas a aceptar la oferta de trabajo en otra ciudad? Eso depende de si me ofrecen un mejor salario.
examples.en: Are you going to accept the job offer in another city? That depends on whether they offer me a better salary.

examples.es: ¿Podremos terminar el proyecto antes de la fecha límite? Eso depende de cuánta ayuda recibamos del equipo.
examples.en: Will we be able to finish the project before the deadline? That depends on how much help we get from the team.

cards.example_sentence: Do you think we will go to the beach this weekend? That depends on the weather forecast.
cards.example_es: ¿Crees que iremos a la playa este fin de semana? Eso depende del pronóstico del tiempo.
cards.example_en: Do you think we will go to the beach this weekend? That depends on the weather forecast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a seesaw labeled eso that tilts each time you say that depends

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the meaning or outcome varies according to the situation.
collocations: It all depends on the context

examples.es: Depende del contexto, pero generalmente se usa en situaciones formales.
examples.en: It depends on the context, but it is usually used in formal situations.

examples.es: Si me preguntas si es correcto decir esa palabra, te diré que depende del contexto.
examples.en: If you ask me if it is correct to say that word, I will tell you that it depends on the context.

examples.es: No puedo darte una respuesta simple sobre si esa acción es aceptable, ya que todo depende del contexto.
examples.en: I cannot give you a simple answer about whether that action is acceptable, since it depends on the context.

cards.example_sentence: It depends on the context, but it is usually used in formal situations.
cards.example_es: Depende del contexto, pero generalmente se usa en situaciones formales.
cards.example_en: It depends on the context, but it is usually used in formal situations.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a dependent leaning on a book of text, because it depends on the context

cards.definition_en: It means that something is not as simple or clear as it might seem.
collocations: It is not that straightforward to explain
It is not that straightforward to solve
It is not that straightforward in practice
It is not that straightforward for beginners

examples.es: No es tan simple como parece implementar esta nueva política en la empresa.
examples.en: It is not that straightforward to implement this new company policy.

examples.es: No es tan simple llegar a un acuerdo cuando ambas partes tienen intereses opuestos.
examples.en: It is not that straightforward to reach an agreement when both sides have conflicting interests.

examples.es: No es tan simple explicar las causas de este fenómeno económico complejo.
examples.en: It is not that straightforward to explain the causes of this complex economic phenomenon.

cards.example_sentence: It is not that straightforward to implement this new company policy.
cards.example_es: No es tan simple como parece implementar esta nueva política en la empresa.
cards.example_en: It is not that straightforward to implement this new company policy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a 'no' sign crossing a simple line—so it's not that straightforward.

cards.definition_en: A statement that reduces a complex issue to overly simple terms.
collocations: That's an oversimplification of the issue
It's an oversimplification of the problem
Such a view is an oversimplification

examples.es: Decir que el éxito profesional depende solo de la suerte es una simplificación excesiva.
examples.en: Claiming that professional success depends solely on luck is an oversimplification.

examples.es: Reducir el complejo debate sobre el cambio climático a una cuestión de impuestos es una simplificación excesiva.
examples.en: Reducing the complex climate change debate to a matter of taxes is an oversimplification.

examples.es: Afirmar que los videojuegos solo fomentan la violencia en los jóvenes es una simplificación excesiva.
examples.en: Asserting that video games only promote violence in youth is an oversimplification.

cards.example_sentence: Claiming that professional success depends solely on luck is an oversimplification.
cards.example_es: Decir que el éxito profesional depende solo de la suerte es una simplificación excesiva.
cards.example_en: Claiming that professional success depends solely on luck is an oversimplification.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an over‑simplified map called oversimplification, echoing the Spanish simplificación.

cards.definition_en: It means that something may be understood in another way.
main_translations_es: Se podría interpretar de otra manera
collocations: This statement could be interpreted differently
The results could be interpreted differently
Such a comment could be interpreted differently
These findings could be interpreted differently
synonyms_en: It could be read another way

examples.es: Podría interpretarse de otra manera si analizamos las intenciones ocultas del autor.
examples.en: It could be interpreted differently if we analyze the author's hidden intentions.

examples.es: Aunque el contrato parece claro, podría interpretarse de otra manera según la legislación vigente.
examples.en: Although the contract seems clear, it could be interpreted differently according to current legislation.

examples.es: Su silencio durante la reunión fue ambiguo; podría interpretarse de otra manera por quienes no lo conocen bien.
examples.en: His silence during the meeting was ambiguous; it could be interpreted differently by those who do not know him well.

cards.example_sentence: It could be interpreted differently if we analyze the author's hidden intentions.
cards.example_es: Podría interpretarse de otra manera si analizamos las intenciones ocultas del autor.
cards.example_en: It could be interpreted differently if we analyze the author's hidden intentions.
cards.mnemonic_en: A pod could be read as a different story.

cards.definition_en: It means that a topic can be discussed and different opinions are possible.
collocations: There is room for debate on this issue
This point is open to debate
The matter remains open to debate

examples.es: En la reunión, hay margen para debatir los cambios propuestos.
examples.en: In the meeting, there is room for debate about the proposed changes.

examples.es: Aunque el informe es claro, todavía hay margen para debatir sobre la estrategia a seguir.
examples.en: Although the report is clear, there is room for debate regarding the strategy to follow.

examples.es: Sobre la nueva política de la empresa, creo que aún hay margen para debatir antes de tomar una decisión final.
examples.en: Regarding the new company policy, I believe there is room for debate before we make a final decision.

cards.example_sentence: In the meeting, there is room for debate about the proposed changes.
cards.example_es: En la reunión, hay margen para debatir los cambios propuestos.
cards.example_en: In the meeting, there is room for debate about the proposed changes.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a spacious room with a margin line where everyone debates loudly.

cards.definition_en: It means that the matter is still open to debate.
main_translations_es: Eso aún es objeto de debate

examples.es: Aunque ya pasó mucho tiempo, eso sigue siendo discutible.
examples.en: Even after all this time, that remains debatable.

examples.es: El experto afirma que el cambio climático es un hecho, pero para algunos políticos eso sigue siendo discutible.
examples.en: The expert claims that climate change is a fact, but for some politicians that remains debatable.

examples.es: La empresa dice que el nuevo contrato es justo, pero para los trabajadores eso sigue siendo discutible.
examples.en: The company says the new contract is fair, but for the workers that remains debatable.

cards.example_sentence: Even after all this time, that remains debatable.
cards.example_es: Aunque ya pasó mucho tiempo, eso sigue siendo discutible.
cards.example_en: Even after all this time, that remains debatable.
cards.mnemonic_en: That remains debatable, as 'discutible' sounds like 'this cute table' that never settles.

cards.definition_en: A statement that something is complicated or difficult.
main_translations_es: Se trata de un asunto complicado

examples.es: No podemos dar una respuesta simple porque es una cuestión compleja.
examples.en: We cannot provide a simple answer because it is a complex issue.

examples.es: La reforma del sistema educativo es una cuestión compleja que genera mucho debate.
examples.en: The reform of the educational system is a complex issue that generates a lot of debate.

examples.es: Debemos analizar los datos con cuidado, ya que la desigualdad económica es una cuestión compleja.
examples.en: We must analyze the data carefully, as economic inequality is a complex issue.

cards.example_sentence: We cannot provide a simple answer because it is a complex issue.
cards.example_es: No podemos dar una respuesta simple porque es una cuestión compleja.
cards.example_en: We cannot provide a simple answer because it is a complex issue.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a complex maze with a sign that reads cuestión to remember it’s a complex issue

cards.definition_en: It means that no single solution works for every situation.
collocations: There is no one-size-fits-all answer, so we must adapt
Because there is no one-size-fits-all answer, each case is different
There is no one-size-fits-all answer; tailor your strategy accordingly

examples.es: Sobre cómo mejorar la educación, no hay una respuesta única para todos los países.
examples.en: Regarding how to improve education, there is no one-size-fits-all answer for every country.

examples.es: Al elegir una dieta saludable, no hay una respuesta única que funcione para todo el mundo.
examples.en: When choosing a healthy diet, there is no one-size-fits-all answer that works for everyone.

examples.es: En cuanto a la gestión del estrés laboral, no hay una respuesta única que podamos aplicar a todos los empleados.
examples.en: As for managing workplace stress, there is no one-size-fits-all answer that we can apply to all employees.

cards.example_sentence: Regarding how to improve education, there is no one-size-fits-all answer for every country.
cards.example_es: Sobre cómo mejorar la educación, no hay una respuesta única para todos los países.
cards.example_en: Regarding how to improve education, there is no one-size-fits-all answer for every country.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a 'no hay' sign over a one-size-fits-all answer, showing it's not unique.
cloze_distractors_en: There is a mandatory global standard
There exists a perfect universal method
There remains a single fixed requirement

cards.definition_en: Used to state that you support or agree with something.
collocations: I am in favor of the proposal
I am in favor of this plan
I am in favor of change

examples.es: Estoy a favor de implementar un horario flexible en la oficina.
examples.en: I am in favor of implementing a flexible schedule at the office.

examples.es: Aunque otros se opongan, estoy a favor de aumentar el presupuesto para educación.
examples.en: Although others may oppose it, I am in favor of increasing the budget for education.

examples.es: Tras analizar los beneficios, estoy a favor de adoptar esta nueva tecnología.
examples.en: After analyzing the benefits, I am in favor of adopting this new technology.

cards.example_sentence: I am in favor of implementing a flexible schedule at the office.
cards.example_es: Estoy a favor de implementar un horario flexible en la oficina.
cards.example_en: I am in favor of implementing a flexible schedule at the office.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture yourself handing a favor to someone and saying, I am in favor of it.

cards.definition_en: It means that I am against something.
collocations: I am opposed to the new policy
I am opposed to that idea

examples.es: Estoy en contra de la decisión del gobierno.
examples.en: I am opposed to the government's decision.

examples.es: Estoy en contra de construir una fábrica en este parque natural.
examples.en: I am opposed to building a factory in this nature reserve.

examples.es: Estoy en contra de cualquier medida que aumente los impuestos a los estudiantes.
examples.en: I am opposed to any measure that increases taxes on students.

cards.example_sentence: I am opposed to the government's decision.
cards.example_es: Estoy en contra de la decisión del gobierno.
cards.example_en: I am opposed to the government's decision.
cards.mnemonic_en: I am opposed to the big red 'contra' sign on the door.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of saying that you understand and value someone's argument or opinion.
collocations: I appreciate your point of view
I appreciate your point and will consider it

examples.es: Valoro tu punto, pero creo que hay otra perspectiva.
examples.en: I appreciate your point, but I think there's another perspective.

examples.es: Valoro tu punto sobre el presupuesto, aunque sigo pensando que necesitamos invertir más en tecnología.
examples.en: I appreciate your point about the budget, although I still think we need to invest more in technology.

examples.es: Valoro tu punto respecto a la flexibilidad laboral, pero me preocupa el impacto en la productividad del equipo.
examples.en: I appreciate your point regarding flexible working, but I am concerned about the impact on team productivity.

cards.example_sentence: I appreciate your point, but I think there's another perspective.
cards.example_es: Valoro tu punto, pero creo que hay otra perspectiva.
cards.example_en: I appreciate your point, but I think there's another perspective.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a valet (valoro) handing you a pointy pen, saying 'I appreciate your point'.

cards.english: I see where you are coming from
cards.definition_en: It means that you understand someone's perspective or reasoning.
collocations: I see where you're coming from

examples.es: Entiendo de dónde vienes, pero creo que el presupuesto del proyecto es insuficiente.
examples.en: I see where you are coming from, but I think the project budget is insufficient.

examples.es: Aunque no comparto tu opinión, entiendo de dónde vienes respecto a este conflicto laboral.
examples.en: Although I do not share your opinion, I see where you are coming from regarding this labor dispute.

examples.es: Entiendo de dónde vienes al sugerir que cambiemos la fecha de la reunión para evitar el fin de semana.
examples.en: I see where you are coming from by suggesting we move the meeting date to avoid the weekend.

cards.example_sentence: I see where you are coming from, but I think the project budget is insufficient.
cards.example_es: Entiendo de dónde vienes, pero creo que el presupuesto del proyecto es insuficiente.
cards.example_en: I see where you are coming from, but I think the project budget is insufficient.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a vein (vien) pointing from your hometown, so I see where you come from
cloze_distractors_en: I have no idea what happens

cards.definition_en: Used to say that something is logical or understandable.

examples.es: Eso tiene sentido, así que seguiré el plan.
examples.en: That makes sense, so I'll follow the plan.

examples.es: Si explicas los datos de esa manera, eso tiene sentido para el equipo.
examples.en: If you explain the data that way, that makes sense to the team.

examples.es: Aunque no estoy de acuerdo con todo, admito que eso tiene sentido.
examples.en: Although I don't agree with everything, I admit that that makes sense.

cards.example_sentence: That makes sense, so I'll follow the plan.
cards.example_es: Eso tiene sentido, así que seguiré el plan.
cards.example_en: That makes sense, so I'll follow the plan.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a sensible hat that says sentido and nods, showing that makes sense.

cards.spanish: No estoy seguro de que eso sea exacto
cards.english: I am not sure that is accurate
cards.definition_en: A statement expressing uncertainty about the accuracy of something.
main_translations_es: No estoy seguro de que sea exacto
No tengo la certeza de que eso sea exacto
collocations: I'm not sure if that's accurate
I'm not sure about the accuracy

examples.es: No estoy seguro de que eso sea exacto, pero creo que está cerca.
examples.en: I am not sure that is accurate, but I think it is close.

examples.es: Según los datos del informe, no estoy seguro de que eso sea exacto.
examples.en: According to the report data, I am not sure that is accurate.

examples.es: Aunque el testigo lo afirma, no estoy seguro de que eso sea exacto.
examples.en: Even though the witness claims it, I am not sure that is accurate.

cards.example_sentence: I am not sure that is accurate, but I think it is close.
cards.example_es: No estoy seguro de que eso sea exacto, pero creo que está cerca.
cards.example_en: I am not sure that is accurate, but I think it is close.
cards.mnemonic_en: I'm not sure, like a doubtful cow rating exactness, so I question if it's accurate

cards.definition_en: It means that something is not perceived as believable or persuasive.

examples.es: El testigo dio una excusa extraña y eso no parece convincente para el jurado.
examples.en: The witness gave a strange excuse and that does not sound convincing to the jury.

examples.es: Aunque el vendedor insiste en que el coche es nuevo, eso no parece convincente después de ver el motor oxidado.
examples.en: Although the seller insists the car is new, that does not sound convincing after seeing the rusty engine.

examples.es: Tu explicación sobre por qué llegaste tarde a la reunión es muy vaga; eso no parece convincente.
examples.en: Your explanation for why you were late to the meeting is very vague; that does not sound convincing.

cards.example_sentence: The witness gave a strange excuse and that does not sound convincing to the jury.
cards.example_es: El testigo dio una excusa extraña y eso no parece convincente para el jurado.
cards.example_en: The witness gave a strange excuse and that does not sound convincing to the jury.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a speaker shouting That does not sound convincing as the word sonido shatters.

cards.english: I struggle to accept that idea
cards.definition_en: To have difficulty accepting that idea.
main_translations_es: Me resulta difícil aceptar esa idea
Tengo problemas para aceptar esa idea
collocations: I struggle to accept that idea
It's hard to accept that idea
I find it difficult to accept that idea
I can't seem to accept that idea
synonyms_en: find it hard to accept that idea
can't come to terms with that idea

examples.es: Aunque el plan de reducir los beneficios suena lógico, me cuesta aceptar esa idea porque afectará a los empleados.
examples.en: Although the plan to cut benefits sounds logical, I struggle to accept that idea because it will negatively impact the staff.

examples.es: Aunque el equipo propone cambios drásticos, me cuesta aceptar esa idea por falta de pruebas.
examples.en: Although the team proposes drastic changes, I struggle to accept that idea due to a lack of evidence.

examples.es: Cuando escucho argumentos tan radicales, me cuesta aceptar esa idea sin analizar los datos primero.
examples.en: When I hear such radical arguments, I struggle to accept that idea without analyzing the data first.

cards.example_sentence: Although the plan to cut benefits sounds logical, I struggle to accept that idea because it will negatively impact the staff.
cards.example_es: Aunque el plan de reducir los beneficios suena lógico, me cuesta aceptar esa idea porque afectará a los empleados.
cards.example_en: Although the plan to cut benefits sounds logical, I struggle to accept that idea because it will negatively impact the staff.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a heavy cost weighing on me as I try to accept the idea.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of saying that someone could be correct.
collocations: You may be right about that
You may be right, after all
You may be right, I guess

examples.es: Podrías tener razón, pero aún así quiero intentarlo.
examples.en: You may be right, but I still want to try.

examples.es: Aunque el presupuesto parece insuficiente, podrías tener razón sobre la viabilidad del proyecto.
examples.en: Although the budget seems insufficient, you may be right about the project's viability.

examples.es: Si analizamos los datos de ventas, podrías tener razón al sugerir un cambio de estrategia.
examples.en: If we analyze the sales data, you may be right to suggest a change in strategy.

cards.example_sentence: You may be right, but I still want to try.
cards.example_es: Podrías tener razón, pero aún así quiero intentarlo.
cards.example_en: You may be right, but I still want to try.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a judge nodding, saying 'You may be right', holding a big sign that reads RAZÓN.

cards.spanish: Hay algo de verdad en eso
cards.english: There is some truth in that
cards.definition_en: It means that the statement contains a certain amount of truth.
main_translations_es: Hay algo de verdad en eso
Hay algo de verdad en lo que dices
Hay algo de verdad en esa afirmación
collocations: There's some truth in what you said.
There's some truth behind that claim.

examples.es: Hay algo de verdad en eso, aunque no todo es exacto.
examples.en: There is some truth in that, although not everything is accurate.

examples.es: Admito que el presupuesto es alto, pero hay algo de verdad en eso cuando dicen que la calidad cuesta dinero.
examples.en: I admit the budget is high, but there is some truth in that when they say quality costs money.

examples.es: Aunque su argumento parece exagerado, hay algo de verdad en eso respecto a la falta de personal.
examples.en: Even though his argument seems exaggerated, there is some truth in that regarding the staff shortage.

cards.example_sentence: There is some truth in that, although not everything is accurate.
cards.example_es: Hay algo de verdad en eso, aunque no todo es exacto.
cards.example_en: There is some truth in that, although not everything is accurate.
cards.mnemonic_en: When you shout Hey and see a big truth sign, you remember There is some truth in that.

cards.english: I would not rule that out
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that something is still considered possible.
collocations: I would not rule out the possibility
I would not rule out that option
I would not rule out a chance
I would not rule out the idea

examples.es: No descartaría esa posibilidad, aunque todavía tengo dudas.
examples.en: I would not rule that out, even though I'm still uncertain.

examples.es: Aunque el presupuesto es bajo, no descartaría esa posibilidad si encontramos un patrocinador.
examples.en: Even though the budget is low, I would not rule that out if we find a sponsor.

examples.es: Si el mercado cambia drásticamente mañana, no descartaría esa posibilidad para nuestro plan de expansión.
examples.en: If the market changes drastically tomorrow, I would not rule that out for our expansion plan.

cards.example_sentence: I would not rule that out, even though I'm still uncertain.
cards.example_es: No descartaría esa posibilidad, aunque todavía tengo dudas.
cards.example_en: I would not rule that out, even though I'm still uncertain.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a big sign saying No regla while I say I would not rule that out

cards.definition_en: Something that should be looked at because it may be valuable or important.
collocations: It is worth examining the data
It is worth examining the evidence

examples.es: Vale la pena analizarlo con detenimiento para entender por qué el motor dejó de funcionar de repente.
examples.en: It is worth examining the engine closely to understand why it suddenly stopped working.

examples.es: Aunque el contrato parece muy complejo, vale la pena analizarlo antes de firmar cualquier compromiso.
examples.en: Although the contract seems very complex, it is worth examining before signing any commitment.

examples.es: Aunque el informe financiero parece confuso, vale la pena analizarlo para encontrar dónde se perdió el dinero.
examples.en: Although the financial report looks confusing, it is worth examining to find where the money was lost.

cards.example_sentence: It is worth examining the engine closely to understand why it suddenly stopped working.
cards.example_es: Vale la pena analizarlo con detenimiento para entender por qué el motor dejó de funcionar de repente.
cards.example_en: It is worth examining the engine closely to understand why it suddenly stopped working.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a valuable pen being examined under a microscope.

cards.english: We should not jump to conclusions
cards.definition_en: It means we ought not to form opinions before having all the facts.

examples.es: No deberíamos apresurarnos a sacar conclusiones sin analizar todos los datos.
examples.en: We should not jump to conclusions before analyzing all the data.

examples.es: Aunque el testigo parezca culpable, no deberíamos apresurarnos a sacar conclusiones hasta escuchar su versión.
examples.en: Even if the witness seems guilty, we should not jump to conclusions until we hear his side of the story.

examples.es: Vi a mi jefe hablando con otro candidato, pero no deberíamos apresurarnos a sacar conclusiones sobre mi despido.
examples.en: I saw my boss talking to another candidate, but we should not jump to conclusions about me being fired.

cards.example_sentence: We should not jump to conclusions before analyzing all the data.
cards.example_es: No deberíamos apresurarnos a sacar conclusiones sin analizar todos los datos.
cards.example_en: We should not jump to conclusions before analyzing all the data.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a frantic jumper pausing at the finish line, reminding you not to rush and jump to conclusions.

cards.definition_en: To add a clarification or nuance to a statement.

examples.es: Conviene matizar que el informe no incluye todos los datos relevantes.
examples.en: We need to qualify that the report does not include all the relevant data.

examples.es: Antes de aceptar la propuesta, conviene matizar que los costos podrían aumentar el próximo año.
examples.en: Before accepting the proposal, we need to qualify that costs could increase next year.

examples.es: Aunque el plan de expansión parece sólido, conviene matizar que no hemos tenido en cuenta el impacto de la inflación en los precios.
examples.en: Although the expansion plan seems solid, we need to qualify that we have not taken into account the impact of inflation on prices.

cards.example_sentence: We need to qualify that the report does not include all the relevant data.
cards.example_es: Conviene matizar que el informe no incluye todos los datos relevantes.
cards.example_en: We need to qualify that the report does not include all the relevant data.
cards.mnemonic_en: To qualify that, imagine a judge placing a mat on the document.

cards.definition_en: It states that something needs proof or supporting evidence.

examples.es: No puedes simplemente decir que el proyecto fracasó; eso requiere evidencia para respaldar tu afirmación.
examples.en: You cannot simply say the project failed; that requires evidence to support your claim.

examples.es: No puedes afirmar que el clima está cambiando sin datos, eso requiere evidencia científica para ser tomado en serio.
examples.en: You cannot claim the climate is changing without data; that requires evidence to be taken seriously.

examples.es: No puedes cambiar la política de la empresa, porque eso requiere evidencia de que no está funcionando.
examples.en: You cannot change the company policy, because that requires evidence that it is not working.

cards.example_sentence: You cannot simply say the project failed; that requires evidence to support your claim.
cards.example_es: No puedes simplemente decir que el proyecto fracasó; eso requiere evidencia para respaldar tu afirmación.
cards.example_en: You cannot simply say the project failed; that requires evidence to support your claim.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a detective saying That requires evidence while holding a file labeled evidencia.

cards.english: What are you basing that on?
cards.definition_en: A question asking for the basis or justification of something.

examples.es: ¿En qué te basas para afirmar que las ventas caerán el próximo trimestre?
examples.en: What are you basing that on when you claim sales will drop next quarter?

examples.es: Dices que este candidato es el mejor, pero ¿en qué te basas?
examples.en: You say this candidate is the best, but what are you basing that on?

examples.es: Si crees que la estrategia de marketing no funcionará, ¿en qué te basas?
examples.en: If you think the marketing strategy won't work, what are you basing that on?

cards.example_sentence: What are you basing that on when you claim sales will drop next quarter?
cards.example_es: ¿En qué te basas para afirmar que las ventas caerán el próximo trimestre?
cards.example_en: What are you basing that on when you claim sales will drop next quarter?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a baseball base shouting, What are you basing that on?

cards.definition_en: A polite request for more detail about something previously mentioned.
collocations: Could you elaborate on that, please?
Could you elaborate on that a bit more?
Could you elaborate on that further?

examples.es: Tu propuesta sobre el presupuesto es interesante, ¿podrías desarrollar esa idea?
examples.en: Your proposal regarding the budget is interesting; could you elaborate on that?

examples.es: Mencionaste un nuevo enfoque para el proyecto, ¿podrías desarrollar esa idea?
examples.en: You mentioned a new approach for the project; could you elaborate on that?

examples.es: No entiendo bien tu punto sobre la estrategia de ventas, ¿podrías desarrollar esa idea?
examples.en: I don't quite understand your point about the sales strategy; could you elaborate on that?

cards.example_sentence: Your proposal regarding the budget is interesting; could you elaborate on that?
cards.example_es: Tu propuesta sobre el presupuesto es interesante, ¿podrías desarrollar esa idea?
cards.example_en: Your proposal regarding the budget is interesting; could you elaborate on that?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a teacher asking could you elaborate while you elabora the idea on the board.

cards.definition_en: A polite request asking someone to provide an example.
collocations: Could you give an example of that?
Could you give an example, please?
Could you give an example to illustrate?

examples.es: No me queda claro qué quieres decir con 'sostenibilidad', ¿podrías dar un ejemplo?
examples.en: I am not sure what you mean by 'sustainability', so could you give an example?

examples.es: No entiendo tu argumento, ¿podrías dar un ejemplo para aclararlo?
examples.en: I do not understand your argument, so could you give an example to clarify it?

examples.es: No entiendo cómo esta nueva política de la empresa va a reducir los gastos, ¿podrías dar un ejemplo?
examples.en: I do not understand how this new company policy is going to cut costs, so could you give an example?

cards.example_sentence: I am not sure what you mean by 'sustainability', so could you give an example?
cards.example_es: No me queda claro qué quieres decir con 'sostenibilidad', ¿podrías dar un ejemplo?
cards.example_en: I am not sure what you mean by 'sustainability', so could you give an example?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chef handing you a sample dish, saying ejemplo as he gives an example.

cards.definition_en: A request for clarification about the precise meaning of something.
collocations: What exactly do you mean by that?
I’m not sure what exactly you mean.
Could you explain what exactly you mean?
Tell me what exactly you mean.
synonyms_en: What are you trying to say?

examples.es: Cuando me explicó su plan, le pregunté: ¿Qué quieres decir exactamente?
examples.en: When he explained his plan, I asked, "What exactly do you mean?"

examples.es: No entiendo tu comentario sobre el presupuesto, ¿qué quieres decir exactamente?
examples.en: I don't understand your comment about the budget; what exactly do you mean?

examples.es: Mencionaste que el proyecto es inviable, ¿qué quieres decir exactamente?
examples.en: You mentioned that the project is unfeasible; what exactly do you mean?

cards.example_sentence: When he explained his plan, I asked, "What exactly do you mean?"
cards.example_es: Cuando me explicó su plan, le pregunté: ¿Qué quieres decir exactamente?
cards.example_en: When he explained his plan, I asked, "What exactly do you mean?"
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an ex‑actly‑clear sign asking what you mean.

cards.definition_en: A polite phrase used to introduce an explanation of a specific matter.

examples.es: Aclaremos un punto sobre el presupuesto antes de aprobar el proyecto.
examples.en: Let us clarify one point about the budget before we approve the project.

examples.es: Aclaremos un punto respecto a las reglas del debate antes de empezar.
examples.en: Let us clarify one point regarding the debate rules before we begin.

examples.es: Aclaremos un punto sobre la fecha de entrega antes de finalizar el contrato.
examples.en: Let us clarify one point about the delivery date before we finalize the contract.

cards.example_sentence: Let us clarify one point about the budget before we approve the project.
cards.example_es: Aclaremos un punto sobre el presupuesto antes de aprobar el proyecto.
cards.example_en: Let us clarify one point about the budget before we approve the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: Let us make a clear point.

cards.english: I want to make something clear
cards.definition_en: A statement used to indicate that the speaker intends to clarify a point.

examples.es: Quiero dejar algo claro: no aceptaré ninguna oferta que no incluya un aumento de sueldo.
examples.en: I want to make something clear: I will not accept any offer that does not include a salary raise.

examples.es: Antes de empezar la reunión, quiero dejar algo claro sobre los nuevos plazos del proyecto.
examples.en: Before we start the meeting, I want to make something clear regarding the new project deadlines.

examples.es: Quiero dejar algo claro respecto a mi postura: estoy totalmente en contra de esta nueva política.
examples.en: I want to make something clear regarding my position: I am strictly against this new policy.

cards.example_sentence: I want to make something clear: I will not accept any offer that does not include a salary raise.
cards.example_es: Quiero dejar algo claro: no aceptaré ninguna oferta que no incluya un aumento de sueldo.
cards.example_en: I want to make something clear: I will not accept any offer that does not include a salary raise.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a crystal-clear glass shouting claro as I want to make something clear.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to state that the speaker is not implying or proposing something.
collocations: I am not suggesting that you should quit
I am not suggesting that it's a bad idea
I am not suggesting that we go now

examples.es: No pretendo decir que el proyecto sea un fracaso total, pero necesitamos revisar el presupuesto.
examples.en: I am not suggesting that the project is a total failure, but we need to review the budget.

examples.es: No pretendo decir que debamos cancelar la reunión, solo sugiero que la pospongamos hasta mañana.
examples.en: I am not suggesting that we should cancel the meeting, I am just proposing that we postpone it until tomorrow.

examples.es: No pretendo decir que ella sea la única responsable, pero su equipo tomó las decisiones clave.
examples.en: I am not suggesting that she is the only one responsible, but her team made the key decisions.

cards.example_sentence: I am not suggesting that the project is a total failure, but we need to review the budget.
cards.example_es: No pretendo decir que el proyecto sea un fracaso total, pero necesitamos revisar el presupuesto.
cards.example_en: I am not suggesting that the project is a total failure, but we need to review the budget.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a no‑pretend sign stopping a suggestion.

cards.english: It should not be interpreted as
cards.definition_en: Indicates that something must not be understood in a particular way.
collocations: It should not be interpreted as a criticism
It should not be interpreted as a guarantee
It should not be interpreted as a promise
It should not be interpreted as an endorsement
synonyms_en: It must not be read as
It is not to be understood as

examples.es: No debería interpretarse como una falta de respeto, sino como una crítica constructiva.
examples.en: It should not be interpreted as disrespect, but as constructive criticism.

examples.es: Mi silencio durante la reunión no debería interpretarse como un acuerdo con su propuesta.
examples.en: My silence during the meeting it should not be interpreted as agreement with your proposal.

examples.es: Este cambio en el presupuesto no debería interpretarse como una señal de inestabilidad financiera.
examples.en: This change in the budget it should not be interpreted as a sign of financial instability.

cards.example_sentence: It should not be interpreted as disrespect, but as constructive criticism.
cards.example_es: No debería interpretarse como una falta de respeto, sino como una crítica constructiva.
cards.example_en: It should not be interpreted as disrespect, but as constructive criticism.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a big red NO sign over a book, reminding you it should not be interpreted as true.
cloze_distractors_en: It will be clearly rejected by
It has been actively promoted as
It can be reliably measured with

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a general statement or overview of a topic.
collocations: Broadly speaking, the market is expanding
Broadly speaking, the results are positive

examples.es: En términos generales, la mayoría de los estudiantes prefieren las clases presenciales a las virtuales.
examples.en: Broadly speaking, the majority of students prefer in-person classes over virtual ones.

examples.es: En términos generales, el nuevo plan de marketing ha sido bien recibido por los clientes.
examples.en: Broadly speaking, the new marketing plan has been well received by the clients.

examples.es: En términos generales, el clima en esta región es bastante templado durante todo el año.
examples.en: Broadly speaking, the climate in this region is quite mild throughout the year.

cards.example_sentence: Broadly speaking, the majority of students prefer in-person classes over virtual ones.
cards.example_es: En términos generales, la mayoría de los estudiantes prefieren las clases presenciales a las virtuales.
cards.example_en: Broadly speaking, the majority of students prefer in-person classes over virtual ones.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a wide speaker broadcasting general terms to a crowd.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a concrete or realistic explanation of something.
collocations: In practical terms, we can see...
In practical terms, the cost is...

examples.es: En términos concretos, el proyecto costará diez mil euros.
examples.en: In practical terms, the project will cost ten thousand euros.

examples.es: En términos concretos, necesitamos contratar a tres personas más para terminar la obra a tiempo.
examples.en: In practical terms, we need to hire three more people to finish the construction on time.

examples.es: En términos concretos, esta nueva política reducirá el consumo de energía en un veinte por ciento.
examples.en: In practical terms, this new policy will reduce energy consumption by twenty percent.

cards.example_sentence: In practical terms, the project will cost ten thousand euros.
cards.example_es: En términos concretos, el proyecto costará diez mil euros.
cards.example_en: In practical terms, the project will cost ten thousand euros.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a concrete block labeled In practical terms on a construction plan.

cards.definition_en: Used to indicate an initial impression based on a quick look.
collocations: At first glance, the data suggests...
At first glance, the results appear...

examples.es: A primera vista, la propuesta parece económica, pero los costos ocultos son muy elevados.
examples.en: At first glance, the proposal seems affordable, but the hidden costs are very high.

examples.es: A primera vista, el candidato no parecía tener experiencia, pero su currículum demostró lo contrario.
examples.en: At first glance, the candidate did not seem to have experience, but his resume proved otherwise.

examples.es: A primera vista, el problema matemático parece imposible de resolver, aunque tiene una solución lógica.
examples.en: At first glance, the math problem seems impossible to solve, although it has a logical solution.

cards.example_sentence: At first glance, the proposal seems affordable, but the hidden costs are very high.
cards.example_es: A primera vista, la propuesta parece económica, pero los costos ocultos son muy elevados.
cards.example_en: At first glance, the proposal seems affordable, but the hidden costs are very high.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a first‑glance view of a beautiful vista, a prima scene you see instantly.

cards.definition_en: Refers to a period of time far in the future.

examples.es: A largo plazo, la inversión en energía renovable generará beneficios económicos.
examples.en: In the long term, investing in renewable energy will generate economic benefits.

examples.es: Debemos considerar los efectos de esta política a largo plazo antes de tomar una decisión final.
examples.en: We must consider the effects of this policy in the long term before making a final decision.

examples.es: Aunque los costos iniciales son altos, el proyecto será rentable a largo plazo.
examples.en: Although the initial costs are high, the project will be profitable in the long term.

cards.example_sentence: In the long term, investing in renewable energy will generate economic benefits.
cards.example_es: A largo plazo, la inversión en energía renovable generará beneficios económicos.
cards.example_en: In the long term, investing in renewable energy will generate economic benefits.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a long, endless runway labeled 'largo' showing the future lasting forever.

cards.definition_en: During a brief period of time; referring to the near future.

examples.es: A corto plazo, la empresa planea aumentar sus ventas en un 10%.
examples.en: In the short term, the company plans to increase its sales by 10%.

examples.es: A corto plazo, necesitamos reducir los gastos operativos para evitar pérdidas mayores.
examples.en: In the short term, we need to reduce operating expenses to avoid further losses.

examples.es: Aunque el proyecto es ambicioso, a corto plazo solo nos enfocaremos en completar la fase de diseño.
examples.en: Although the project is ambitious, in the short term we will only focus on completing the design phase.

cards.example_sentence: In the short term, the company plans to increase its sales by 10%.
cards.example_es: A corto plazo, la empresa planea aumentar sus ventas en un 10%.
cards.example_en: In the short term, the company plans to increase its sales by 10%.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a short‑term deadline tied to a tight 'corto' rope.

cards.spanish: En el peor de los casos
cards.definition_en: It refers to the most unfavorable possible outcome.
main_translations_es: En el peor de los casos
collocations: In the worst-case scenario, we could lose everything.
In the worst-case scenario, the project will be delayed.
In the worst-case scenario, the system may crash.

examples.es: En el peor de los casos, tendremos que cancelar el evento.
examples.en: In the worst-case scenario, we will have to cancel the event.

examples.es: En el peor de los casos, el presupuesto no alcanzará para terminar la construcción.
examples.en: In the worst-case scenario, the budget will not be enough to finish the construction.

examples.es: Si el servidor falla, en el peor de los casos perderemos toda la información guardada.
examples.en: If the server fails, in the worst-case scenario we will lose all the saved information.

cards.example_sentence: In the worst-case scenario, we will have to cancel the event.
cards.example_es: En el peor de los casos, tendremos que cancelar el evento.
cards.example_en: In the worst-case scenario, we will have to cancel the event.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a worst-case scenario as a 'peor' case spilling over the floor.

cards.spanish: En el mejor de los casos
cards.definition_en: Used to describe the most favorable possible outcome.
main_translations_es: En el mejor de los casos
collocations: In the best-case scenario, we succeed
In the best-case scenario, costs are lower
In the best-case scenario, the project finishes early

examples.es: En el mejor de los casos, el proyecto se completará en tres meses.
examples.en: In the best-case scenario, the project will be completed in three months.

examples.es: En el mejor de los casos, la lluvia parará antes de que empiece el partido de fútbol.
examples.en: In the best-case scenario, the rain will stop before the soccer match begins.

examples.es: En el mejor de los casos, lograremos recaudar suficientes fondos para renovar la biblioteca escolar.
examples.en: In the best-case scenario, we will manage to raise enough funds to renovate the school library.

cards.example_sentence: In the best-case scenario, the project will be completed in three months.
cards.example_es: En el mejor de los casos, el proyecto se completará en tres meses.
cards.example_en: In the best-case scenario, the project will be completed in three months.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a mayor (mejor) approving the best‑case scenario.

cards.definition_en: In the end, after everything else has been considered.
collocations: Ultimately, we decided to go ahead
Ultimately, it comes down to cost
Ultimately, the results speak for themselves

examples.es: En última instancia, decidiremos si el proyecto continúa.
examples.en: Ultimately, we will decide whether the project continues.

examples.es: En última instancia, la responsabilidad de la seguridad recae sobre el gerente.
examples.en: Ultimately, the responsibility for safety rests with the manager.

examples.es: En última instancia, el éxito de la negociación depende de nuestra disposición a ceder.
examples.en: Ultimately, the success of the negotiation depends on our willingness to compromise.

cards.example_sentence: Ultimately, we will decide whether the project continues.
cards.example_es: En última instancia, decidiremos si el proyecto continúa.
cards.example_en: Ultimately, we will decide whether the project continues.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture the ultimate trophy on the final última podium, showing everything ends there.

cards.definition_en: Used to emphasize a conclusion that follows from what has been said or to point out that something is true when all factors are considered.
collocations: after all is said and done
synonyms_en: when all is said and done

examples.es: No deberías ser tan duro con él; al fin y al cabo, es solo un principiante.
examples.en: You shouldn't be so hard on him; after all, he is only a beginner.

examples.es: El proyecto terminó superando el presupuesto inicial, pero al fin y al cabo, la calidad del resultado final justifica el gasto extra.
examples.en: The project ended up exceeding the initial budget, but after all, the quality of the final result justifies the extra expense.

examples.es: No te preocupes por el error, al fin y al cabo todos estamos aprendiendo en este debate.
examples.en: Don't worry about the mistake; after all, we are all learning in this debate.

cards.example_sentence: You shouldn't be so hard on him; after all, he is only a beginner.
cards.example_es: No deberías ser tan duro con él; al fin y al cabo, es solo un principiante.
cards.example_en: You shouldn't be so hard on him; after all, he is only a beginner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture the final curtain falling and everyone saying after al as the show ends.

cards.definition_en: Used to introduce a brief summary of what has been said.

examples.es: En resumen, el proyecto fue un éxito.
examples.en: To sum up, the project was a success.

examples.es: En resumen, necesitamos reducir los gastos para ahorrar dinero.
examples.en: To sum up, we need to cut expenses to save money.

examples.es: En resumen, los argumentos presentados no son suficientes para cambiar la ley.
examples.en: To sum up, the arguments presented are not enough to change the law.

cards.example_sentence: To sum up, the project was a success.
cards.example_es: En resumen, el proyecto fue un éxito.
cards.example_en: To sum up, the project was a success.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a calculator adding a suma, then you sum up the whole story.

cards.definition_en: To give a brief statement of the main points.

examples.es: Para resumir, el proyecto fue un éxito.
examples.en: To summarize, the project was a success.

examples.es: Para resumir, los puntos principales de la reunión son la reducción de costos y el aumento de ventas.
examples.en: To summarize, the main points of the meeting are cost reduction and increased sales.

examples.es: Para resumir, hemos decidido cambiar la fecha de la conferencia al próximo mes.
examples.en: To summarize, we have decided to move the conference date to next month.

cards.example_sentence: To summarize, the project was a success.
cards.example_es: Para resumir, el proyecto fue un éxito.
cards.example_en: To summarize, the project was a success.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a parrot giving a quick summary, shouting 'para resumir' as it folds the paper.

cards.definition_en: In a basic or fundamental way; basically.

examples.es: En esencia, el proyecto busca mejorar la calidad del aire en la ciudad.
examples.en: Essentially, the project aims to improve air quality in the city.

examples.es: En esencia, nuestra propuesta se centra en reducir los costos operativos de la empresa.
examples.en: Essentially, our proposal focuses on reducing the company's operating costs.

examples.es: En esencia, el argumento del autor es que la tecnología ha cambiado nuestra forma de aprender.
examples.en: Essentially, the author's argument is that technology has changed the way we learn.

cards.example_sentence: Essentially, the project aims to improve air quality in the city.
cards.example_es: En esencia, el proyecto busca mejorar la calidad del aire en la ciudad.
cards.example_en: Essentially, the project aims to improve air quality in the city.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a bottle labeled en esencia, the essential core, and you’ll recall essentially.

cards.definition_en: Briefly; in a concise summary.
collocations: to put it in a nutshell
the whole thing in a nutshell

examples.es: En pocas palabras, el proyecto fue un éxito.
examples.en: In a nutshell, the project was a success.

examples.es: En pocas palabras, nuestra estrategia de ventas no funcionó durante el trimestre.
examples.en: In a nutshell, our sales strategy did not work during the quarter.

examples.es: En pocas palabras, necesitamos reducir los gastos para evitar la quiebra.
examples.en: In a nutshell, we need to cut expenses to avoid bankruptcy.

cards.example_sentence: In a nutshell, the project was a success.
cards.example_es: En pocas palabras, el proyecto fue un éxito.
cards.example_en: In a nutshell, the project was a success.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a tiny nut shell holding just a few words.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce the most important problem or topic.
collocations: The key issue is the lack of…

examples.es: La cuestión central es cómo mejorar la educación en las zonas rurales.
examples.en: The key issue is how to improve education in rural areas.

examples.es: La cuestión central es si debemos invertir más dinero en energías renovables este año.
examples.en: The key issue is whether we should invest more money in renewable energy this year.

examples.es: La cuestión central es la falta de comunicación entre los departamentos de la empresa.
examples.en: The key issue is the lack of communication between the company departments.

cards.example_sentence: The key issue is how to improve education in rural areas.
cards.example_es: La cuestión central es cómo mejorar la educación en las zonas rurales.
cards.example_en: The key issue is how to improve education in rural areas.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a key opening a big issue sign, like the Spanish 'qué' and 'cuestión'.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to transition to the next point or topic.
collocations: That brings us to the next point
That brings us to a conclusion
That brings us to the final step
That brings us to the heart of the matter

examples.es: Hemos terminado de hablar sobre el presupuesto, así que eso nos lleva a discutir los planes de marketing.
examples.en: We have finished talking about the budget, so that brings us to discussing the marketing plans.

examples.es: Ya analizamos los problemas técnicos, y eso nos lleva a considerar una solución permanente.
examples.en: We have already analyzed the technical problems, and that brings us to considering a permanent solution.

examples.es: Hemos terminado de revisar los resultados del primer trimestre, lo cual eso nos lleva a analizar las proyecciones para el próximo año.
examples.en: We have finished reviewing the first-quarter results, and that brings us to analyzing the projections for next year.

cards.example_sentence: We have finished talking about the budget, so that brings us to discussing the marketing plans.
cards.example_es: Hemos terminado de hablar sobre el presupuesto, así que eso nos lleva a discutir los planes de marketing.
cards.example_en: We have finished talking about the budget, so that brings us to discussing the marketing plans.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bridge labeled That brings us to, while the Spanish chant eso nos lleva a echoes across it.

cards.english: There is another angle to consider
cards.definition_en: It means that there is an additional perspective or aspect that should be taken into account.
collocations: We should remember there is another angle to consider
It's important to note there is another angle to consider
Before concluding, consider that there is another angle to consider

examples.es: Aunque el diseño parece terminado, hay otro ángulo que debemos considerar para mejorar la accesibilidad del sitio web.
examples.en: Although the design seems finished, there is another angle to consider to improve the website's accessibility.

examples.es: Aunque el plan parece sólido, hay otro ángulo que no hemos explorado en esta reunión.
examples.en: Although the plan seems solid, there is another angle to consider that we haven't explored in this meeting.

examples.es: No te apresures a juzgar el caso; hay otro ángulo que debemos considerar sobre el comportamiento del testigo.
examples.en: Do not rush to judge the case; there is another angle to consider regarding the witness's behavior.

cards.example_sentence: Although the design seems finished, there is another angle to consider to improve the website's accessibility.
cards.example_es: Aunque el diseño parece terminado, hay otro ángulo que debemos considerar para mejorar la accesibilidad del sitio web.
cards.example_en: Although the design seems finished, there is another angle to consider to improve the website's accessibility.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a fresh angle shaped like 'otro' pointing at a triangle, reminding you there is another angle to consider
cloze_distractors_en: Nobody was listening to anyone else
These are rarely mentioned anywhere today

cards.english: Let us not lose sight of the fact that
cards.definition_en: A phrase urging someone to keep an important point in mind.
main_translations_es: No perdamos de vista el hecho de que
collocations: let us not lose sight of the fact that
we must not lose sight of the fact that
do not lose sight of the fact that
it is crucial not to lose sight of the fact that

examples.es: No perdamos de vista que nuestro objetivo principal es reducir las emisiones de carbono antes de 2030.
examples.en: Let us not lose sight of the fact that our primary goal is to reduce carbon emissions by 2030.

examples.es: No perdamos de vista que, aunque la tecnología ha avanzado, la interacción humana sigue siendo esencial para el servicio al cliente.
examples.en: Let us not lose sight of the fact that, although technology has advanced, human interaction remains essential for customer service.

examples.es: No perdamos de vista que la seguridad de los clientes debe ser nuestra prioridad absoluta durante la renovación del hotel.
examples.en: Let us not lose sight of the fact that client safety must be our absolute priority during the hotel renovation.

cards.example_sentence: Let us not lose sight of the fact that our primary goal is to reduce carbon emissions by 2030.
cards.example_es: No perdamos de vista que nuestro objetivo principal es reducir las emisiones de carbono antes de 2030.
cards.example_en: Let us not lose sight of the fact that our primary goal is to reduce carbon emissions by 2030.
cards.mnemonic_en: Let us not lose sight—imagine a lighthouse keeping the vista clear.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that something should be kept in mind.
collocations: It is worth remembering that you should...
It is worth remembering that the rules apply

examples.es: Conviene recordar que la puntualidad es una muestra de respeto.
examples.en: It is worth remembering that punctuality is a sign of respect.

examples.es: Conviene recordar que los datos actuales contradicen nuestra hipótesis inicial.
examples.en: It is worth remembering that the current data contradicts our initial hypothesis.

examples.es: Conviene recordar que el presupuesto del proyecto tiene un límite estricto.
examples.en: It is worth remembering that the project budget has a strict limit.

cards.example_sentence: It is worth remembering that punctuality is a sign of respect.
cards.example_es: Conviene recordar que la puntualidad es una muestra de respeto.
cards.example_en: It is worth remembering that punctuality is a sign of respect.
cards.mnemonic_en: Conveniently, I remember that it is worth noting.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to introduce an honest statement.
collocations: If we are being honest, I think...
If we are being honest, the truth is...
If we are being honest, we must admit...

examples.es: Si somos sinceros, el proyecto no estará terminado a tiempo para la entrega.
examples.en: If we are being honest, the project will not be finished in time for the deadline.

examples.es: Si somos sinceros, no creo que esta estrategia de marketing sea efectiva para atraer clientes.
examples.en: If we are being honest, I do not think this marketing strategy is effective for attracting customers.

examples.es: Si somos sinceros, la calidad del servicio en este restaurante ha bajado mucho últimamente.
examples.en: If we are being honest, the quality of service at this restaurant has dropped significantly lately.

cards.example_sentence: If we are being honest, the project will not be finished in time for the deadline.
cards.example_es: Si somos sinceros, el proyecto no estará terminado a tiempo para la entrega.
cards.example_en: If we are being honest, the project will not be finished in time for the deadline.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a truth‑telling elf saying "If we are being honest" while shouting "si somos!"

cards.definition_en: Used to indicate that a statement is true only when considered in a precise or technical sense.

examples.es: En rigor, el proyecto no está terminado.
examples.en: Strictly speaking, the project is not finished.

examples.es: En rigor, no podemos considerar este acuerdo como definitivo hasta que ambas partes firmen.
examples.en: Strictly speaking, we cannot consider this agreement final until both parties sign.

examples.es: En rigor, el candidato no cumple con todos los requisitos académicos para el puesto.
examples.en: Strictly speaking, the candidate does not meet all the academic requirements for the position.

cards.example_sentence: Strictly speaking, the project is not finished.
cards.example_es: En rigor, el proyecto no está terminado.
cards.example_en: Strictly speaking, the project is not finished.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a strict ruler shouting En rigor as the rule for every word.

cards.definition_en: A phrase used to indicate that the speaker is about to speak truthfully or sincerely.

examples.es: A decir verdad, no me gustó la película.
examples.en: To be honest, I didn't like the movie.

examples.es: A decir verdad, no tengo ni idea de cómo arreglar este problema técnico.
examples.en: To be honest, I have no idea how to fix this technical problem.

examples.es: A decir verdad, prefiero trabajar solo en este proyecto.
examples.en: To be honest, I prefer to work alone on this project.

cards.example_sentence: To be honest, I didn't like the movie.
cards.example_es: A decir verdad, no me gustó la película.
cards.example_en: To be honest, I didn't like the movie.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine an honest man chanting a decir verdad as his truth motto.

cards.definition_en: A request for the listener to avoid misunderstanding what the speaker means.
collocations: Do not get me wrong, but…
Do not get me wrong, I’m not…
Do not get me wrong, I love it
Do not get me wrong, it’s just…
synonyms_en: don't take me the wrong way

examples.es: No me malinterpretes, pero creo que tu propuesta necesita más detalles técnicos.
examples.en: Do not get me wrong, but I think your proposal needs more technical details.

examples.es: No me malinterpretes, respeto mucho tu trabajo, aunque no esté de acuerdo con tu conclusión.
examples.en: Do not get me wrong, I respect your work a lot, even if I disagree with your conclusion.

examples.es: No me malinterpretes, no estoy diciendo que el plan sea malo, solo que es demasiado costoso.
examples.en: Do not get me wrong, I am not saying the plan is bad, just that it is too expensive.

cards.example_sentence: Do not get me wrong, but I think your proposal needs more technical details.
cards.example_es: No me malinterpretes, pero creo que tu propuesta necesita más detalles técnicos.
cards.example_en: Do not get me wrong, but I think your proposal needs more technical details.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sign No me blocking a wrong road, reminding you: Do not get me wrong.

cards.definition_en: A statement used to indicate that the speaker does not mean to offend anyone.

examples.es: Sin ánimo de ofender, creo que deberías reconsiderar tu decisión.
examples.en: No offense intended, I think you should reconsider your decision.

examples.es: Sin ánimo de ofender, tu propuesta no parece muy realista para este proyecto.
examples.en: No offense intended, but your proposal does not seem very realistic for this project.

examples.es: Sin ánimo de ofender, pero tu forma de organizar el equipo está causando retrasos.
examples.en: No offense intended, but your way of organizing the team is causing delays.

cards.example_sentence: No offense intended, I think you should reconsider your decision.
cards.example_es: Sin ánimo de ofender, creo que deberías reconsiderar tu decisión.
cards.example_en: No offense intended, I think you should reconsider your decision.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a No offense sign being torn by a sad Sin ánimo de ofender banner.

cards.definition_en: A polite phrase used to preface a statement that may be critical or contrary, showing respect while disagreeing.
collocations: With all due respect, I must disagree
With all due respect, that's not accurate
With all due respect, here's my opinion

examples.es: Con el debido respeto, creo que su propuesta no es viable.
examples.en: With all due respect, I think your proposal is not feasible.

examples.es: Con el debido respeto, no estoy de acuerdo con su análisis de los datos.
examples.en: With all due respect, I disagree with your analysis of the data.

examples.es: Con el debido respeto, su comentario está fuera de lugar en esta reunión.
examples.en: With all due respect, your comment is out of place in this meeting.

cards.example_sentence: With all due respect, I think your proposal is not feasible.
cards.example_es: Con el debido respeto, creo que su propuesta no es viable.
cards.example_en: With all due respect, I think your proposal is not feasible.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a polite bow, saying 'due' (debido) while handing over a 'respect' (respeto) badge.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of saying that you do not share someone's opinion.
collocations: Allow me to disagree with you
Allow me to disagree for a moment
Allow me to disagree on this point

examples.es: Permíteme disentir, pero creo que esa solución no funcionará.
examples.en: Allow me to disagree, but I think that solution won't work.

examples.es: Aunque respeto tu punto de vista, permíteme disentir sobre la eficacia de este nuevo plan.
examples.en: While I respect your point of view, allow me to disagree regarding the effectiveness of this new plan.

examples.es: Si me lo permites, permíteme disentir con la conclusión que acabas de presentar ante el comité.
examples.en: If you will, allow me to disagree with the conclusion you just presented to the committee.

cards.example_sentence: Allow me to disagree, but I think that solution won't work.
cards.example_es: Permíteme disentir, pero creo que esa solución no funcionará.
cards.example_en: Allow me to disagree, but I think that solution won't work.
cards.mnemonic_en: I raise my hand and say Allow me while a big red DISAGREE sign flashes over DISENTIR

cards.definition_en: To present something using different wording or perspective.
collocations: frame it in a different way
look at it from another angle

examples.es: Si tuviera que explicar el problema, lo plantearía de otra forma.
examples.en: If I had to explain the problem, I would frame it differently.

examples.es: Aunque entiendo tu punto, lo plantearía de otra forma para que el cliente no se sienta atacado.
examples.en: Although I understand your point, I would frame it differently so the client doesn't feel attacked.

examples.es: No creo que el argumento sea incorrecto, pero lo plantearía de otra forma para enfatizar los beneficios.
examples.en: I don't think the argument is wrong, but I would frame it differently to emphasize the benefits.

cards.example_sentence: If I had to explain the problem, I would frame it differently.
cards.example_es: Si tuviera que explicar el problema, lo plantearía de otra forma.
cards.example_en: If I had to explain the problem, I would frame it differently.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a plant in a frame, then swap the frame for a different one.

cards.definition_en: It means that the situation or understanding is altered.

examples.es: Eso cambia las cosas, así que tendremos que replantear el plan.
examples.en: That changes the picture, so we’ll have to rethink the plan.

examples.es: Si el testigo principal ha cambiado su declaración, eso cambia las cosas para nuestra defensa.
examples.en: If the main witness has changed their statement, that changes the picture for our defense.

examples.es: Ahora que sabemos que el presupuesto fue recortado, eso cambia las cosas para nuestro proyecto.
examples.en: Now that we know the budget was cut, that changes the picture for our project.

cards.example_sentence: That changes the picture, so we’ll have to rethink the plan.
cards.example_es: Eso cambia las cosas, así que tendremos que replantear el plan.
cards.example_en: That changes the picture, so we’ll have to rethink the plan.
cards.mnemonic_en: That changes the picture, like a photo that suddenly says 'cambia' and flips.

cards.definition_en: It indicates that something makes your argument more convincing.
collocations: A strong example strengthens your argument

examples.es: Cuando presentas datos concretos, eso fortalece tu argumento.
examples.en: When you present concrete data, that strengthens your argument.

examples.es: Citar fuentes académicas en tu ensayo es vital porque eso fortalece tu argumento.
examples.en: Citing academic sources in your essay is vital because that strengthens your argument.

examples.es: Si añades un testimonio personal a tu presentación, verás que eso fortalece tu argumento.
examples.en: If you add a personal testimonial to your presentation, you will see that that strengthens your argument.

cards.example_sentence: When you present concrete data, that strengthens your argument.
cards.example_es: Cuando presentas datos concretos, eso fortalece tu argumento.
cards.example_en: When you present concrete data, that strengthens your argument.
cards.mnemonic_en: That weight adds fuerza, strengthening your argument.

cards.definition_en: It means that something reduces the strength or persuasiveness of an argument.

examples.es: Si el testigo miente, eso debilita la postura.
examples.en: If the witness lies, that weakens the case.

examples.es: Presentar pruebas falsas ante el juez, eso debilita la postura.
examples.en: Presenting fake evidence to the judge, that weakens the case.

examples.es: Al contradecir sus propias declaraciones previas, eso debilita la postura.
examples.en: By contradicting your own previous statements, that weakens the case.

cards.example_sentence: If the witness lies, that weakens the case.
cards.example_es: Si el testigo miente, eso debilita la postura.
cards.example_en: If the witness lies, that weakens the case.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a cracked case that slouches, because that weakens the case just like a bad posture.

cards.definition_en: It indicates that something provides evidence for or backs up a particular claim.
collocations: as evidence that supports the claim

examples.es: Eso respalda la idea de que el cambio climático es real.
examples.en: That supports the claim that climate change is real.

examples.es: El informe financiero muestra un aumento en las ventas, y eso respalda la idea de que la empresa está creciendo.
examples.en: The financial report shows an increase in sales, and that supports the claim that the company is growing.

examples.es: Aunque no hay pruebas definitivas, eso respalda la idea de que el sospechoso estaba en la escena del crimen.
examples.en: Although there is no definitive proof, that supports the claim that the suspect was at the crime scene.

cards.example_sentence: That supports the claim that climate change is real.
cards.example_es: Eso respalda la idea de que el cambio climático es real.
cards.example_en: That supports the claim that climate change is real.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sturdy back (respaldo) holding up a claim.

cards.definition_en: To be in disagreement or incompatibility with something.

examples.es: Tu propuesta de presupuesto entra en conflicto con nuestras políticas de ahorro actuales.
examples.en: Your budget proposal conflicts with our current savings policies.

examples.es: El nuevo horario de trabajo entra en conflicto con mis responsabilidades familiares.
examples.en: The new work schedule conflicts with my family responsibilities.

examples.es: Esta decisión estratégica entra en conflicto con los valores fundamentales de nuestra empresa.
examples.en: This strategic decision conflicts with the core values of our company.

cards.example_sentence: Your budget proposal conflicts with our current savings policies.
cards.example_es: Tu propuesta de presupuesto entra en conflicto con nuestras políticas de ahorro actuales.
cards.example_en: Your budget proposal conflicts with our current savings policies.
